title: Decade color morph still snaps — rethink approach
description:
## Problem
Hero decade flow still hard-refreshes between decades instead of transitioning colors. Prior dual-buffer opacity work did not fix perceived refresh.

## Goal
Verify what the live map actually does on decade advance; fix or replace the morph so density colors (and pins) interpolate/crossfade continuously with no blank or snap.
acceptance_criteria: Visual decade advances morph density colors continuously; no full-refresh look in StrictMode or production
close_reason: Density decade morph uses per-state feature-state colorA/colorB/blend lerp; pin opacity crossfade retained; tests pass.

title: Current-state research pipeline audit document with code refs
description:
## Problem statement
Workstream bead under epic repo-tt2u (repo-audit). Full program brief applies.

## Why necessary
Required for coherent source-governed agent-native research system.

## Verified current behavior
To be filled from audit (repo-audit) before implementation closes.

## Target behavior
Per architectural decisions 1–8 and acceptance criteria in epic repo-tt2u.

## Scope
This workstream only. Coordinate via exclusive path ownership.

## Non-goals
Parallel duplicate abstractions; live uncontrolled crawl; public write path.

## Dependencies
Blocked by current-state audit completion for schema/path truth; then dependency order per epic graph.

## Acceptance criteria
Evidence-backed closure notes; tests; no documentation-only close.
acceptance_criteria: Implements workstream scope with tests and evidence; no duplicate substrate
notes: Wrote docs/research/entity-acquisition-current-state-audit.md. Key: discovery never publishes (assertDiscoveryCannotPublish); 5 scheduled campaign types + discover-candidates live lane; 3 registry layers (adapter/corpus/external-metadata); resolution is propose/review/no_match only; LLM surfaces are operator-cli prepare-only; DPLA split federal dpla-items-v1 vs community v2; stale @blap refs in Black Book skills.
close_reason: Audit published at docs/research/entity-acquisition-current-state-audit.md with code refs, pipeline diagram, registries, acquisition paths, LLM surfaces, kill switches, traces (ABS/DPLA/Wikimedia/editorial/story/resolution), defects and unresolved questions.

title: MOB-EPIC: Native iOS and Android application
description:
Outcome
Ship a production-quality native iOS and Android reader that matches the public web application's truth, evidence, map, dignity, brand, and correction posture without duplicating canonical data or weakening security.

Scope
The complete program in docs/mobile/mobile-app-epic.md and children MOB-001 through MOB-022. The epic coordinates architecture, contracts, API, Expo app, native map, offline behavior, security, accessibility, testing, beta, store release, rollout, and measured post-launch work.

Requirements
Use Expo React Native with custom development builds and MapLibre Native unless MOB-002 records evidence that invalidates the decision. Use apps/api-public as the mobile read boundary. Share only environment-neutral contracts and pure behavior. Keep Firestore canonical/research data server-only. Preserve immutable release rollback. Resolve product identity before permanent store resources. Use Beads as the source of truth.

Adversarial review
Assume every hidden dependency will fail: unfinished web redesign, changing store policy, stale SDK assumptions, compromised client, tampered cache, API version skew, map provider outage, offline launch, denied permissions, low-memory devices, inaccessible map, and a single maintainer with limited recurring time.

Tests
All child acceptance criteria plus final cross-platform launch gate. Run bd graph check. Validate no circular dependencies and no child can close the epic by substituting documentation for required runtime evidence.

Model routing
Strong reasoning model for orchestration and final adversarial review. Balanced coding models for implementation. Fast models only for deterministic work. Require a separate strong reviewer for final closure.

Exclusive ownership
Epic owner coordinates only. Child agents claim exclusive paths. Root package barrels, pnpm lockfile, apps/mobile app config, API route registry, and release workflows have one integration owner at a time.

Non-goals
No user accounts, push notifications, social features, or full offline basemap unless a child bead explicitly changes scope through measured evidence.

Evidence to close
A signed-off launch evidence index links every child bead, production commit, store build, contract version, release ID, test run, accessibility report, performance trace, privacy declaration, and rollback drill.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Every non-deferred child is closed with evidence. 2. Production iOS and Android binaries are built from one audited commit. 3. Public API, release, security, accessibility, performance, store, and rollback gates pass. 4. Owner explicitly approves staged rollout. 5. Beads and Git are pushed and synchronized.

metadata.modelRouting: strong orchestrator; task-specific first-capable implementation models; independent strong final reviewer

title: MOB-001: Resolve mobile product identity, accounts, and release prerequisites
description:
Outcome
A human-approved, auditable identity and release-prerequisite record eliminates every permanent naming, account, signing, and domain ambiguity before native scaffolding becomes expensive to unwind.

Scope
Audit Blackstory versus Black Book across repository, brand pack, domains, package scopes, legal pages, Firebase projects, Apple Developer, App Store Connect, Google Play Console, Expo/EAS, support contacts, and existing trademarks/search results. Record the selected store-facing name, capitalization, subtitle working rules, developer identity, bundle/application IDs, URL scheme, associated domains, minimum OS recommendation, territories, account type, and spend ceiling.

Requirements
Do not rename the repository or packages as incidental cleanup. Produce a decision matrix and migration impact list. Verify that proposed bundle/application IDs and store records are available before treating them as final. Use separate development, preview, and production identifiers. Record who controls Apple, Google, Firebase, DNS, GitHub, and EAS root accounts and require phishing-resistant MFA where supported. Confirm whether the Google Play account is subject to the closed-test rule. Record support and privacy-policy public URLs. Mark all unresolved human choices as gates, not agent defaults.

Adversarial review
Challenge brand conflict, trademark risk, impersonation, loss of signing access, personal address exposure, organization D-U-N-S delay, mismatched privacy-policy entity, accidental production Firebase use from dev builds, and irreversible store identifier creation. Assume the app may be rejected if metadata implies a children-only app without correct category handling.

Tests
Repository-wide identity inventory; bundle/application ID format validation; associated-domain ownership check; Firebase app registration plan; account access recovery drill; no-production-ID preflight for tests; store-account requirement checklist current as of execution date.

Model routing
Strong reasoning/research model because identity, legal/store policy, and irreversible identifiers require judgment. Human approval is mandatory.

Exclusive ownership
docs/mobile/decisions/mobile-identity.md, app/account inventory, and human console checklist only. No broad code rename.

Non-goals
No application scaffold, store submission, domain migration, or package-scope rename.

Evidence to close
Approved decision record with date and approver; screenshots or exported account metadata with secrets redacted; verified support/privacy URLs; explicit unresolved blockers.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
close_reason: Decision record complete (docs/mobile/decisions/mobile-identity.md): name BlackStory, domain blackbook.app, proposed dev/preview/prod bundle ids, URL scheme, min OS, territories. 7 store-account/legal items (Apple/Google/EAS provisioning, bundle-id availability, trademark search, support+privacy URLs, spend ceiling) are explicit unresolved human gates flagged via bd human, blocking only MOB-020/021 store submission — not earlier engineering. No permanent store resource created.

metadata.modelRouting: Strong reasoning/research model because identity, legal/store policy, and irreversible identifiers require judgment. Human approval is mandatory.

title: MOB-002: Record the mobile architecture, threat model, and contract boundaries
description:
Outcome
Accepted ADRs lock the native stack, backend boundary, package dependency direction, security model, offline posture, release compatibility, and explicit rejection criteria before implementation.

Scope
Validate Expo React Native, Expo Router, CNG/custom development builds, MapLibre Native, React Native Firebase modules, SQLite, API-first public reads, and EAS/GitHub release responsibilities against the exact current repository and current official platform requirements.

Requirements
Create ADRs for stack, data boundary, state/cache, native build/update, and threat model. Include decision drivers, alternatives, reversal cost, version pinning policy, supported OS decision, native-directory policy, dependency review policy, client trust limits, App Check monitor/enforce rollout, and what remains server authoritative. Treat App Check as attestation, not authorization. Define API N and N-1 compatibility and minimum-supported-app behavior. Define no-secrets/no-admin/no-direct-canonical-Firestore invariants.

Adversarial review
Attempt to break the proposal with Metro incompatibility, React Native new-architecture constraints, Expo free-tier limits, native config drift, compromised OTA credentials, rooted clients, App Check outage, provider quota, offline downgrade, stale artifact replay, deep-link injection, API enumeration, and one-maintainer operations.

Tests
Dependency proof-of-concept spikes for MapLibre and App Check in a disposable app; `expo-doctor`; local iOS/Android dev build; threat-model misuse cases converted to tests or child requirements; ADR link checker.

Model routing
Strong reasoning model. Use an independent second strong model to red-team the ADR before acceptance.

Exclusive ownership
docs/adr mobile ADRs and docs/mobile/security only. No production feature implementation.

Non-goals
No final UI, no permanent store records, no cloud enforcement claim.

Evidence to close
Accepted ADRs, spike logs, version compatibility matrix, threat model, reviewer findings and dispositions.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: ADR-020..023 + threat-model.md drafted, fact-checked, and independently red-teamed on branch redesign/mobile-app-build (isolated worktree at /Users/geralddagher/Developer/Projects/blackstory-mobile, pushed to origin). Status deliberately left 'Proposed' pending owner acceptance (repo-5os2) rather than self-declared by the agent that ran the review. Real spike evidence: expo install of MapLibre Native + RN Firebase App Check + expo-sqlite + expo-dev-client clean, expo-doctor 20/20, iOS simulator pod install/build succeeded with USE_FRAMEWORKS=static (required for RN Firebase Swift pods under static linkage). Proceeding to prototype MOB-003/MOB-006 using these Proposed ADRs as the working spec since they commit no irreversible resource; will not close this bead until owner accepts.

title: MOB-003: Extract versioned public contracts and enforce mobile-safe package boundaries
description:
Outcome
Web, API, release tooling, and mobile consume one versioned set of public DTOs and schemas without app-to-app imports or server-only transitive dependencies.

Scope
Create `packages/public-contracts` and migrate public entity, claim, citation, timeline, revision, media, related, map, search, bootstrap, content, correction receipt, pagination, error, and compatibility schemas from web-owned locations. Add explicit narrow exports and fixture builders.

Requirements
Use Zod or the repository's accepted schema mechanism. Define semantic contract versioning. Preserve deprecated fields only through an explicit migration window. Public types must omit canonical/research fields by construction. Numeric notability/relevance ranking signals remain excluded. Public location uses already-redacted types. Dates include precision. Disputes and provenance remain visible. Generate OpenAPI/JSON Schema where useful from the same source or add drift tests. Package exports must work in Node, Next, Metro, and tests without polyfills.

Adversarial review
Search for accidental Node built-ins, crypto imports, Firebase Admin, React DOM, Next, process/env access, broad domain-barrel imports, hidden fields, optional-property ambiguity, recursive graph payloads, unbounded arrays, invalid URLs, and version skew. Test maliciously large DTOs and unknown enum values.

Tests
Package unit and property tests; serialization round trips; current and N-1 fixtures; Metro bundle/import smoke test; `arethetypeswrong` or equivalent export validation; static forbidden-import scanner; API/web consumer compile; golden snapshots for omitted sensitive fields.

Model routing
Strong model for contract design and migration mapping, balanced model for implementation, fast model for mechanical call-site updates after the contract is locked.

Exclusive ownership
packages/public-contracts/** plus narrowly assigned source migrations. One integration owner edits root barrels and lockfile.

Non-goals
No mobile UI and no storage adapter in this package.

Evidence to close
Published contract matrix, generated schemas, passing consumers, bundle smoke result, sensitive-field negative snapshots.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: packages/public-contracts built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build (isolated worktree, pushed to origin), commit 6c4abb4. 14 narrow subpath exports, zero server-only deps, zod-only runtime dep. Real verified evidence: 70/70 unit tests passing (serialization round-trips, N/N-1 fixtures, 6 sensitive-field negative snapshots proving canonical fields are stripped on parse), boundary scanner (own-source + entrypoint-graph Metro-smoke stand-in) PASSED with zero violations, tsc --noEmit 0 errors, clean dist build verified importable. Deferred and explicitly flagged: OpenAPI/JSON-Schema drift tests, arethetypeswrong-equivalent validation, real Metro bundle test (blocked until apps/mobile exists - now it does, real Metro smoke test is a good next increment), real apps/web/apps/api-public consumer wiring. Not closing: bead acceptance criteria requires an ACCEPTED ADR (ADR-021 is Proposed pending owner review, repo-5os2).

title: MOB-004: Implement bounded public API v1 for native and web consumers
description:
Outcome
The existing `apps/api-public` surface exposes a complete, versioned, cacheable, abuse-bounded read API backed only by released public data.

Scope
Implement bootstrap, entity, search, map/release, supporting-content, health/degraded, and compatibility endpoints. Reuse existing App Check, rate-limit, query-guard, observability, release, and Firestore projection patterns. Add an HTTP server entrypoint only within the existing deployable.

Requirements
No new microservice. No direct canonical/research reads. Verify App Check on protected endpoints and document any intentionally unauthenticated immutable artifact. Bound every query, page, array, body, response, timeout, and concurrency path. Use cursor pagination, ETag/If-None-Match, release IDs, schema versions, stable errors, request IDs, Cache-Control, and graceful degraded mode. Never expose internal collection names, unpublished status, hidden ranking signals, raw confidence internals, or precise living-person geography. Support current and previous compatible mobile contract. Keep API framework choice minimal and documented; do not adopt a framework solely for aesthetics.

Adversarial review
Enumerate IDs, omit/forge App Check, replay tokens, send Unicode/path/query bombs, seek SSRF through citation/media URLs, force expensive filters, induce N+1 reads, exploit cache key confusion, request withdrawn IDs, poison headers, exceed JSON depth, and trigger cost amplification. Verify errors do not distinguish unpublished from nonexistent records.

Tests
Unit handlers; consumer-driven contract tests; Firebase emulator integration; App Check debug and invalid-token cases; rate-limit corpus; query fuzz/property tests; ETag/cache tests; N/N-1 compatibility; load and Firestore-read budget tests; PII-safe log snapshots; container startup/health test.

Model routing
Strong model for API/security design review, balanced coding model for implementation, independent strong reviewer for authorization/redaction and cost paths.

Exclusive ownership
apps/api-public/** and its direct tests. Shared contract changes return to MOB-003 owner.

Non-goals
No mobile-specific canonical data and no public write endpoint.

Evidence to close
OpenAPI/schema artifact, test report, load/cost report, example redacted responses, deployment/runbook steps, reviewer sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: apps/api-public/v1 built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit 762df82. Real HTTP server (native node:http, no framework dep added) with 6 endpoints (health/compatibility/bootstrap/entity/search), every response validated against @repo/public-contracts schemas. Verified: tsc --noEmit 0 errors, 57/57 tests passing, booted server smoke-tested. Real adversarial coverage: id-enumeration indistinguishability, App Check omitted/forged, oversized query/body, JSON depth bomb, 10 sensitive-field negative snapshots. Deferred and stated explicitly: live Firestore-emulator integration (injectable PublicDataAccess port + in-memory adapter used instead), real timing-attack tests, load/read-budget tests, SSRF-via-media-URL. IMPORTANT: surfaced a real conflict between threat-model T2 (App Check outage should fail OPEN for reads) and packages/security/rate-limits.ts's evaluateQuota, which hard-denies expensive_read (search) for any unverified-App-Check caller even during an outage - tracked as repo-uqmm for owner/security decision, NOT fixed here since it's shared platform code. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-021 (repo-5os2) plus repo-uqmm resolved plus deferred tests.

title: MOB-005: Couple mobile bootstrap and map/content artifacts to immutable releases
description:
Outcome
A single active release atomically controls entity, search, map, content, legal-version, and mobile bootstrap artifacts, with verifiable hashes and rollback.

Scope
Complete the release-activation integration described but not implemented by ADR-013. Generate mobile bootstrap manifest, aggregate map data, bounded point artifacts, content indexes, and compatibility metadata. Store and serve them through the existing Firebase/GCP-native release path.

Requirements
Artifacts are deterministic, release-scoped, content-addressed or manifest-hashed, immutable, cacheable, and never overwritten. Activation changes one pointer only after all validation succeeds. Rollback restores every artifact consistently. Bootstrap declares schema range, app-version policy, feature flags, legal versions, artifact hashes, degraded mode, and cache directives. Define retention and garbage collection that cannot delete active or rollback releases. Fail closed on partial generation or hash mismatch.

Adversarial review
Crash midway, duplicate activation, concurrent publish, stale CDN response, hash mismatch, missing artifact, incompatible client, rollback after schema migration, deleted prior release, clock skew, oversized flat GeoJSON, and tampered local cache. Prove map redaction remains transitive from raw input to artifact.

Tests
Determinism; manifest hash verification; activation transaction; partial-failure cleanup; concurrent publication; rollback drill; active plus previous client compatibility; corrupted artifact rejection; payload/gzip budgets; redaction corpus; emulator integration; CDN/cache-header verification where deployable.

Model routing
Strong model for release state machine and failure analysis, balanced model for implementation, independent strong review.

Exclusive ownership
publication worker/release modules, release artifact builders, and dedicated tests. Coordinate root publication barrels through one integrator.

Non-goals
No full offline basemap and no new database.

Evidence to close
Two generated test releases, successful activation and rollback logs, manifest samples, size report, failure-injection results.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Release-activation pipeline built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit c4a2fe6, in packages/domain/src/publication/{mobile-bootstrap,release-activation}.ts. Reused existing canonicalJson/sha256Json/buildReleaseManifest/canTransitionRelease core (packages/domain/src/publication/index.ts) rather than reinventing. Real verified: 21 new tests + 58 existing regression tests all pass, tsc --noEmit clean. Adversarial coverage: crash-midway, concurrent/duplicate activation, stale-CDN via release-stamp, hash-mismatch, missing artifact, incompatible client, rollback-after-schema-migration, oversized GeoJSON, immutable-overwrite refusal, and a CRITICAL transitive-redaction proof (raw precise coord -> asserts only coarsened output ever reaches the artifact). Deferred explicitly: Firebase-emulator integration, real CDN cache-header verification (both to MOB-021 launch gate). Needs independent strong-model review per 3 flagged concerns: (1) in-memory CAS pointer is a stand-in - a real Firestore adapter must implement identical transactional CAS, (2) GC retains active+previous-only, confirm that's the intended rollback depth, (3) compat constants shared with apps/api-public by convention not compile-time - a drift test would close the gap. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-004/ADR-021 implications reviewed plus the above.

title: MOB-006: Create the Expo mobile monorepo scaffold and native build foundation
description:
Outcome
`apps/mobile` produces reproducible iOS and Android development builds inside the existing pnpm monorepo with no production credentials or placeholder architecture debt.

Scope
Create an Expo SDK project at the current compatible stable version, Expo Router, TypeScript strict config, CNG/prebuild policy, development client, environment profiles, lint/typecheck/test/build/start scripts, EAS config, local runbooks, and workspace wiring.

Requirements
Run EAS commands from apps/mobile. Keep `ios/` and `android/` generated and uncommitted unless the ADR explicitly records a reason. Use config plugins for native configuration. Define development/preview/production profiles and distinct IDs. Pin Node/pnpm/Expo-compatible versions. Use environment validation with no secrets in client config. Add `build` and `start` scripts compatible with repository boundary validation. Do not install UI/state/network libraries until their bead requires them.

Adversarial review
Fresh clone on Apple Silicon, clean prebuild, stale native directories, pnpm symlink resolution, Metro resolving server barrels, missing Xcode/Android tooling, EAS free-tier unavailability, accidental production config, and dependency new-architecture incompatibility.

Tests
Fresh-install script; `expo-doctor`; typecheck/lint/unit smoke; Android emulator build; iOS simulator build; one physical-device dev build per platform before closure; clean prebuild reproducibility; package boundary and bundle-import smoke.

Model routing
Balanced coding model. Fast model may scaffold only after the ADR and version matrix are fixed.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile foundational config, root workspace integration, and mobile docs. One owner controls pnpm lockfile and root scripts.

Non-goals
No feature-complete UI, map, API client, or store submission.

Evidence to close
Build logs and artifact IDs, exact version matrix, fresh-clone runbook, physical-device screenshots, no-production-config proof.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: apps/mobile scaffolded on branch redesign/mobile-app-build (isolated worktree, pushed to origin), commit 6c4abb4. Expo SDK 56.0.16 (deliberately downgraded from latest 57 for N-1 stability per ADR-020), Expo Router, TypeScript strict, dev client, eas.json with 3 profiles per ADR-023, app.config.ts with dev/preview/prod bundle ids from the identity doc, no fake Firebase credentials committed. expo-doctor 21/21. iOS pod install verified (103 deps/102 pods, deploymentTarget bumped 16.0->16.4 - real evidence requiring ADR-020's OS-floor re-verification). Android expo prebuild verified, minSdkVersion=26 confirmed in generated Gradle config; no Gradle/APK build attempted (no working java in this environment). REAL RISK CONFIRMED: pnpm workspace resolution breaks the root lockfile (ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE once apps/mobile/package.json exists under the apps/* glob) - tracked as separate follow-up bead. Physical-device dev builds (bead requires one per platform before closure) not done - no hardware paired. title: MOB-009: Build the typed API client, SQLite cache, offline mode, and migrations
description:
Outcome
Mobile reads versioned public data reliably online and offline, never overwrites fresh state with stale responses, and recovers safely from cache corruption or schema change.

Scope
Implement typed HTTP transport, App Check token hook seam, cancellation, retries, ETag handling, query cache, SQLite schema/repository, bootstrap synchronization, artifact verification, offline search/cache, migrations, eviction, diagnostics, and degraded mode.

Requirements
Server state is separate from UI state. Retry only idempotent reads with bounded exponential backoff and jitter; respect Retry-After. Cancel superseded searches. Verify release/schema before commit. Apply cache writes transactionally. Keep last known compatible release until new release is fully verified. Store only public DTOs. SecureStore is limited to small opaque secrets/receipts. Define size and retention budgets. Provide manual clear-cache and diagnostic release/version information. Never silently downgrade to fixtures in production.

Adversarial review
App killed during migration, disk full, corrupt DB, stale ETag, rollback to prior release, hash mismatch, incompatible schema, clock skew, duplicate sync, offline first launch, captive portal, response truncation, retry storm, stale search overwrite, and maliciously large payload. Verify correction content and precise location never enter general cache logs.

Tests
Repository and migration unit tests; fault injection at every transaction step; hash mismatch; N/N-1 upgrade and downgrade; cancellation/race tests; offline launch; cache eviction; disk-full simulation where possible; corrupt DB recovery; performance and size benchmarks; real-device termination tests.

Model routing
Strong model for state machine/migration design, balanced coding model for implementation, independent reviewer for data-loss and downgrade paths.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile/src/data/**, SQLite migrations, transport, cache tests. API contract changes stay with MOB-003/004.

Non-goals
No sync of private user accounts and no full offline map tiles.

Evidence to close
Migration matrix, fault-injection report, offline device recordings, cache size report, rollback compatibility proof.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Data layer built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit 2edf69e. Typed transport (cancellation, superseding-runner, bounded backoff+jitter for idempotent reads only, Retry-After, ETag/304), SQLite CacheStore port + expo-sqlite adapter + in-memory degraded fallback, destructive write-ahead-intent migrations, bootstrap-sync (single-flight), cache-policy (50MB LRU + never-cache guard + salted search-key hashing), TanStack Query + custom SQLite persister. Real verified: tsc clean (both tsconfigs), 209/209 tests passing across 32 suites (55 new, zero regressions). Adversarial coverage: mid-migration kill->safe rebuild, corrupt-DB->rebuild, stale-release drop, rollback-to-older-stamp (T5), hash-mismatch keeps last-known-good, duplicate-sync single-flight, offline-first-launch no-crash (T7), retry-storm bound, oversized-payload rejected pre-parse, never-cache tripwire, SecureStore bulk-rejection. Deferred explicitly: real-device disk-full/process-termination against native SQLite WAL (only unit-tested against in-memory model) - MOB-019 device matrix. IMPORTANT FINDING: @repo/public-contracts is NOT actually importable from apps/mobile (isolated npm lockfile has no @repo scope) - types were vendored into apps/mobile/src/data/contracts.ts with source citations, real drift risk, needs a follow-up bead. ALSO FOUND: the pre-existing committed apps/mobile lockfile nests expo-modules-core incorrectly, breaking a clean npm ci for jest-expo resolution - same root cause as repo-oamr's pnpm hoisting issue. Needs independent review per 3 flagged concerns: (1) global stamp = releaseId only since /v1/bootstrap doesn't carry full releaseStamp - confirm releaseIds are always-fresh, (2) migration crash-safety unproven against native WAL, (3) vendored-contract drift risk. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-021/022 plus review plus repo-oamr-adjacent lockfile fix.

metadata.modelRouting: Strong model for state machine/migration design, balanced coding model for implementation, independent reviewer for data-loss and downgrade paths.

title: MOB-010: Implement App Check, mobile security controls, and privacy-by-design
description:
Outcome
Production binaries and APIs use native attestation, least-privilege permissions, safe logging, privacy manifests, and staged enforcement without pretending the client is trusted.

Scope
Integrate React Native Firebase App Check, App Attest with DeviceCheck fallback, Play Integrity, debug providers, API token injection, correction replay protection decision, permission copy, privacy manifest, SDK inventory, log scrubbers, build secret checks, and enforcement runbook.

Requirements
Debug provider can never activate in production. Tokens are refreshed and attached centrally. API validates app/project identity. Enforcement starts in monitor mode with dashboards and an explicit cutover. Sensitive write endpoints evaluate limited-use tokens/replay protection. No certificate pinning or root/jailbreak block as a substitute for server controls. Location is foreground-only and optional. No ad/tracking SDK. Crash logs exclude search text, correction content, precise location, citation URLs, and sensitive entity classifications. Privacy/Data Safety inventory includes all transitive native SDKs.

Adversarial review
Patched binary, emulator token, replay, stolen token, no token, expired token, provider outage/quota, clock skew, debug secret leakage, MITM, rooted device, log injection, malicious deep link, screenshot/clipboard leakage, and OTA update that changes data practice. Verify service remains safely degraded when attestation fails broadly.

Tests
App Check debug/valid/invalid/expired cases; production build symbol/string scan for debug tokens and secrets; API app-ID allowlist; replay tests; permission denial/revocation; log redaction corpus; privacy manifest validation; dependency/SDK inventory; monitor-to-enforce rollback drill.

Model routing
Strong security/privacy model for design and adversarial review; balanced model for implementation. Independent security review required.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile security/native Firebase config, API verification middleware coordination, privacy artifacts. One owner controls production console steps.

Non-goals
No user authentication, tracking analytics, background location, or device fingerprinting.

Evidence to close
Attestation dashboard screenshots, monitor metrics, production binary scan, privacy/SDK inventory, enforcement and rollback runbook, reviewer sign-off.

title: MOB-011: Resolve production map data, PMTiles, attribution, and failure strategy
description:
Outcome
A measured, licensed, cost-bounded production map source works on native MapLibre and supports the brand's map interaction without relying on demo infrastructure.

Scope
Validate self-hosted U.S. Protomaps PMTiles through Firebase Hosting/GCS/CDN as preferred, managed MapTiler only as explicit fallback, or a simpler owned-boundary map if it meets product outcomes. Build a native spike with production-like style, glyphs, sprites, range requests, attribution, offline/error behavior, and release versioning.

Requirements
Record data sources, licenses, attribution placement, update cadence, artifact size, CDN range behavior, cache headers, egress model, and kill switch. No demo tiles. No hidden paid dependency. Style uses brand/dignity tokens and never turns historical harm into a crime heatmap. Map artifact version ties to release/bootstrap. Define flat GeoJSON to vector-tile migration threshold. Verify MapLibre new architecture and min API compatibility. Paid fallback requires owner approval and cost ceiling.

Adversarial review
Provider outage, range request unsupported, stale CDN, corrupt archive, glyph/sprite failure, attribution hidden by sheet, license change, huge first download, low-memory crash, zoom beyond available data, cost spike, and offline startup. Test hostile feature properties and redaction regression.

Tests
Native iOS/Android spike; HTTP range tests; artifact hash; cold/warm payload and memory traces; provider/CDN failure; attribution screenshots; license checklist; redaction corpus; point-count/size threshold benchmark; low-memory device cycle.

Model routing
Strong reasoning model for geodata/licensing/cost decision, balanced model for native integration, human approval for paid fallback.

Exclusive ownership
map artifact/style build, docs/mobile map ADR, native map spike. Avoid final Explore feature work until decision closes.

Non-goals
No worldwide map and no full offline street basemap by default.

Evidence to close
Accepted map ADR, production-like artifact, device traces, attribution/license record, monthly cost model, failure recordings.

title: MOB-016: Implement correction submission and opaque receipt status
description:
Outcome
Users can submit a correction into the existing quarantine boundary and later check a safe receipt without creating an account or gaining any canonical write path.

Scope
Build correction policy/intake form, validation, optional contact decision, evidence URL fields, consent/disclosure, API-submissions client, App Check, rate-limit feedback, opaque receipt storage, status lookup, offline draft decision, and abuse-safe errors.

Requirements
Submission writes only quarantine. Canonical publish attempts are impossible. Receipt IDs are high entropy and not enumerable. Correction body and contact data are excluded from logs, analytics, general cache, screenshots in error reports, and URLs. Explain review is not guaranteed and submission is not publication. Define retention/deletion and contact consent. Replay protection is evaluated for submit. Rate-limit messages do not reveal moderation state. Draft storage is opt-in or avoided; if stored, encrypt/minimize and provide clear deletion.

Adversarial review
Spam, giant payload, malicious URLs, XSS strings, PII, threats, duplicate replay, guessed receipt, status enumeration, app kill after submit before receipt display, network retry duplicate, offline submission, and App Check outage. Verify no direct Firestore write.

Tests
Validation/property tests; quarantine emulator integration; duplicate/idempotency; replay; rate limit; receipt entropy/enumeration; PII log scans; RNTL form states; Maestro submit/status; permissionless operation; deletion/retention behavior.

Model routing
Strong model for privacy/abuse design, balanced model for implementation, independent security review.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile Corrections feature plus coordinated api-submissions client endpoints. Server quarantine owner reviews API changes.

Non-goals
No public comments, voting, moderation UI, or canonical edits.

Evidence to close
End-to-end emulator and staging proof, log redaction report, receipt threat review, policy copy approval.

Client mirrors apps/web's existing corrections flow verbatim (field rules, receipt format /^BB-COR-[A-F0-9]{16}$/, coarse non-revealing status phases) rather than reinventing policy. Real verified: 48/48 tests passing, 6 suites, tsc clean, eslint clean. Real idempotency via deterministic FNV-1a content-hash key (kill-and-retry = replay not duplicate, tested). Receipt persisted to SecureStore BEFORE submit resolves (survives app-kill-before-display, tested). Verified content never reaches logs/cache/URLs via 2 real regression tests (console-spy + source scan for router/params leakage). Correctly did NOT add a speculative HTTP route to apps/api-submissions (it only has quarantine primitives, no wired routing yet; the real wire contract lives in apps/web's Next route) - client targets the documented JSON shape via injected fetch, flagged the missing api-submissions route as a real server-side follow-up rather than inventing one. FLAGGED FOR SECURITY REVIEW: uses a STANDARD App Check token, not limited-use/single-consumption - replay resistance currently rests only on rate-limiting + content-hash dedup, not a true single-use token. Reviewer must decide if limited-use tokens are mandatory before this ships. Not closing: needs the api-submissions route built, the limited-use-token decision, and accepted ADR-021.

title: MOB-017: Complete cross-platform accessibility, motion, and adaptive layout gate
description:
Outcome
Every critical flow is independently usable with VoiceOver and TalkBack, large text, reduced motion, high contrast, and non-map list alternatives.

Scope
Audit and remediate all screens, custom controls, map/list synchronization, sheets, focus movement, headings, announcements, text scaling, orientation, tablet widths, touch targets, motion, contrast, images, and error states.

Requirements
Map is never the sole information channel. Focus moves predictably on route/sheet/error changes. Dynamic Type does not truncate claims, citations, legal text, or actions. Reduced motion removes camera travel. Controls expose role/state/value. Reading order matches visual meaning. Platform-specific accessibility behavior is used when abstractions fail. Minimum touch target is 44 logical units. Accessibility labels contain no redundant type suffixes. Tablet/large-screen behavior is intentional.

Adversarial review
VoiceOver rotor order, TalkBack traversal across map, focus trap in sheets, giant fonts, bold text, display zoom, split screen, landscape, external keyboard, Switch Access/Voice Control, loading announcements, rapid errors, and invisible selected state. Test without sight of the map.

Tests
Automated accessibility assertions; manual VoiceOver and TalkBack scripts for every critical flow; Apple Accessibility Inspector; Android Accessibility Scanner/Test Framework where applicable; Dynamic Type matrix; reduced motion; contrast; keyboard/switch spot checks; device recordings.

Model routing
Strong accessibility-capable reviewer for audit, balanced model for remediation. Human assistive-technology testing is mandatory.

Exclusive ownership
Cross-cutting mobile UI remediation. Coordinate changes through feature owners; no unrelated redesign.

Non-goals
No accessibility conformance claim beyond tested scope without independent audit.

Evidence to close
Issue-by-issue audit, recordings, screenshots, automated report, remaining known limitations approved as launch blockers or explicit deferrals.

title: MOB-018: Add privacy-safe observability, performance budgets, and budget controls
description:
Outcome
Crashes, startup, map, API, cache, and release failures are diagnosable without collecting sensitive content, and measured budgets prevent cost or performance drift.

Scope
Select and integrate one native crash/performance stack consistent with Firebase-first posture, add structured breadcrumbs, release/build metadata, network timing aggregation, kill switches, cost dashboards, and performance test harness.

Requirements
No launch analytics dependency. Scrub search, correction, location, citation URLs, and sensitive entity fields. Tag reports with app version, runtime version, release ID, schema, platform, and degraded state. Sampling/retention documented. App continues if observability SDK fails. Define Firebase/API/map/CDN/EAS spend ceilings and alert/kill-switch owners. Capture bundle/binary baselines and device performance budgets.

Adversarial review
Crash inside logger, recursive error, offline queue leak, PII in exception message, huge breadcrumb volume, symbol upload failure, map provider cost spike, retry storm, OTA regression, and observability SDK privacy-policy change.

Tests
PII corpus through scrubber; forced native/JS crashes in nonproduction; symbolication; SDK-disabled behavior; startup/map/cache traces on device matrix; bundle/binary size gate; cost load test; alert and kill-switch drill.

Model routing
Balanced coding model; strong privacy/performance review for data dictionary and budgets.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile observability/performance modules, build metadata, dashboards/runbooks. No product analytics events.

Non-goals
No behavioral tracking or advertising attribution.

Evidence to close
Symbolicated test crashes, PII-negative report, trace bundle, size baseline, budget dashboard and drill results.

Real verified independently: 626/626 tests (74 suites, 56 new: PII corpus, key-smuggling, circular objects, huge-payload truncation, native-SDK-absent/throwing, logger's-own-code-throwing, kill-switch, sampling), tsc clean. Real measured (not estimated) bundle-size baseline via actual expo export: iOS Hermes bundle 3.26 MiB, total export 16.23 MiB, recorded in scripts/bundle-size-baseline.json for MOB-019's future CI gate. Crashlytics/Performance confirmed free-tier/no-per-event billing - no cost-controls-matrix entry needed. observabilityEnabled kill-switch (default true) + performanceSampleRate (0.10) added to app.config.ts extra. title: MOB-019: Integrate mobile quality gates, CI, EAS builds, and complete test matrix
description:
Outcome
Every pull request receives deterministic static/unit/contract gates, and release candidates receive reproducible native builds, device E2E, security, accessibility, and performance evidence.

Scope
Extend GitHub Actions and repository scripts; add mobile unit/integration lanes, boundary checks, Expo Doctor, dependency/license/SBOM checks, Android build, iOS strategy, EAS profiles/workflows, Maestro flows, artifact retention, flaky-test policy, and release evidence index.

Requirements
GitHub Actions remains required for code-level gates. EAS handles native macOS/build/E2E where appropriate and may be on-demand to control cost. Third-party actions are SHA-pinned and permissions minimal. No production secrets in pull-request contexts. Emulator/debug App Check is explicit. Failed or skipped required gates block release. Flakes are quarantined with owner/expiry, never blindly retried to green. Validate current and previous API fixtures. Add mobile app to root build/typecheck/test without making every docs-only PR run costly native builds.

Adversarial review
Fork PR secret exposure, malicious dependency install script, stale EAS artifact, cache poisoning, flaky Maestro, simulator-only false confidence, mismatched commit/build, unpinned action, skipped iOS lane, and green tests against fixtures but broken staging API.

Tests
CI self-tests; clean-cache runs; PR path filters; branch protection check names; Android/iOS build provenance; Maestro critical flows; SBOM/license scan; dependency review; artifact commit-SHA verification; staging contract smoke.

Model routing
Balanced DevOps/coding model; strong supply-chain and gate review.

Exclusive ownership
.github/workflows mobile additions, apps/mobile/.eas, test harness, scripts, ruleset documentation. One workflow integrator.

Non-goals
No always-on expensive cloud matrix without measured value and approved budget.

Evidence to close
Successful clean CI and EAS runs, build provenance, branch protection plan, flake register, test matrix with owners.

title: MOB-020: Prepare beta distribution, store records, privacy disclosures, and review packet
description:

No placeholder content or broken links. Store declarations derive from audited SDK/data inventory and match in-app privacy policy. Explain optional location and corrections accurately. Include rights/attribution for map and media. Determine and satisfy Google personal-account tester requirements. Use representative target-user testers. Record feedback and dispositions. Production credentials are least privilege and recoverable. Screenshots reflect actual app and approved brand.

Adversarial review
Metadata/product-name mismatch, personal address exposure, wrong account type, missing D-U-N-S, privacy declaration inconsistent with SDK, hidden location collection, placeholder seed data, broken citations/support link, reviewer unable to access app, revoked signing cert, and tester requirement delay.

Exclusive ownership
Store metadata, credentials runbooks, beta workflows. No production rollout.

Non-goals
No paid marketing or public production release.

Evidence to close

title: MOB-021: Run the adversarial launch gate, rollback drill, and staged production rollout
description:
Outcome
The owner receives a complete evidence-based go/no-go decision and, only after approval, releases iOS and Android through a monitored staged rollout with proven rollback.

Scope
Audit all child evidence, verify exact commit/build/release IDs, run production-like security/performance/accessibility/offline tests, conduct release-pointer and OTA rollback drills, verify store rollback paths, establish on-call/kill-switch ownership, and stage rollout percentages.

Requirements
Independent reviewer did not author load-bearing implementation. No open P0/P1 launch defects. App Check enforcement metrics are healthy. Current and N-1 client compatibility proven. Crash-free and performance budgets pass. Privacy/store declarations match binaries. Map licenses/attribution pass. BB-101 or superseding cross-platform design gate closes. Rollout starts at minimum practical percentage, pauses on thresholds, and has documented stop conditions. Owner explicitly approves each platform.

Adversarial review
Assume bad release, compromised update, API outage, App Check outage, CDN stale artifact, incompatible cache, store review delay, one platform success and other failure, privacy complaint, cost spike, and map provider failure. Run game-day scenarios instead of reviewing prose only.

Tests
Full critical Maestro suite; physical-device accessibility; load/cost; production attestation; cold/warm/offline; upgrade from beta/N-1; release rollback; OTA rollback; kill switches; store phased-release controls; post-release synthetic smoke.

Model routing
Independent strongest available review model plus human owner. Implementation models may answer findings but cannot self-approve.

Exclusive ownership
Launch evidence, rollout runbook, and release controls only. Changes discovered create/return to child beads.

Non-goals
No automatic 100 percent rollout and no waiver of a failed P0 gate.

Evidence to close
Signed go/no-go record, game-day logs, threshold dashboard, owner approval, staged rollout records, 24/72-hour review notes.

title: Wire existing fail-closed publication gates into the release/projection builder
description: VERIFIED: two fail-closed gates exist but are explicitly not wired into publication. packages/domain/src/facts/publish-gate.ts:11-13 says 'Not wired live: the projection-build pipeline (workers/publication/, per ADR-007) is the intended caller.' packages/domain/src/relevance/notability-gate.ts:12-14 says the projection/release build should call assertPublishableEntityHasNotabilityBasis (ADR-015) but does not; packages/domain/src/search/index-build.ts:6-9 enforces it only as defense-in-depth. Fix: the release/projection build must call both gates and fail closed. Add the release invariant from the architecture review: an active release must never contain a record where notabilityBasis.length > 0 AND any basis has zero resolvable evidence refs. High-impact predicates (first/only/oldest, deaths, violence, allegations, legal conclusions) require two independent lineages OR primary + authoritative corroboration OR a reviewed exception with public explanation.

title: Stop synthesizing notabilityBasis with empty evidenceIds (documented-first with 0 sources)
description: VERIFIED: apps/web/src/lib/search/snapshot-search-index.ts:36-44 and apps/web/src/app/entity/[id]/adapters.ts:27-35 both reconstruct NotabilityBasisRecord[] from display labels, defaulting criterion to documented_site and always setting evidenceIds: []. apps/web/src/components/why-appears/WhyThisAppears.tsx:66 + copy.ts:15 then render evidenceIds.length as N documented sources — so a record can show Documented first / 0 documented sources even when evidence exists upstream. Fix: delete label-to-record synthesis from web adapters entirely. Structured notabilityBasis with real evidenceRefs must be carried by the release projection (see release-builder bead); until it is, the UI must not display a source count derived from synthesized records — hide the count rather than show a fabricated zero.

title: Person names leak into theme facets: split topicTags, filter facets to controlled topics
description: VERIFIED: apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/filters.ts:96 builds map theme options from every distinct topicTags string via countBy, and packages/domain/src/search/facets.ts:39 does the same for search facets. topicTags is z.array(z.string()) with no taxonomy (packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts:869,942). Fixture data already contains person names as tags: rosa-parks and martin-luther-king in packages/firebase/fixtures/national-catalog/civil-rights.json (~lines 61-66, 385-390), so Rosa Parks renders as a theme filter. Immediate fix: (1) sweep all national-catalog fixtures and remove person/org/event-name tags from topicTags (people belong in relationships/mentions, not taxonomy); (2) make both facet builders consume only tags present in a controlled topic list (interim allowlist module in packages/domain until the full taxonomy bead lands); (3) republish search index + map data. Default public filters stay: entity class, topic, era, geography, record maturity, status.
acceptance_criteria: No person, organization, campaign, or event name appears as a theme/topic facet on map or search; facet builders reject values outside the controlled list; fixtures cleaned and republished.
close_reason: Person-name tags removed from national-catalog fixtures (rosa-parks, malcolm-x, martin-luther-king, mlk); added packages/domain/src/search/topic-allowlist.ts controlled allowlist consumed by both map filters.ts and search facets.ts. Interim fix pending full taxonomy split (black-book-s4hp).

title: UI/UX overhaul v5: personal-relevance experience, full layout rip
description: Owner brief 2026-07-18 (session 2): complete UI+UX overhaul of the public site. Keep brand guide coloring/type/logo; nuke and reimplement all layout/chrome. Mission framing: connect people with data — teach readers about Black history close to them (their state, their place) via opt-in location and state-level storytelling. Map is first-class. Modern, clean, not bulky; fully responsive; accessibility aligned; one consistent pattern system (no orphan patterns). Update design direction doc (v5 supersedes v4 layout sections). Supersedes the visual execution of BB-097 which owner judged insufficient.
notes:
v5.1 'atlas instrument' revision shipped (session 3, after owner verdict 'it all looks the same / nothing to remember'): floating ink island nav (one pill, both themes, no onmap variant; bottom-sheet drawer moved outside the pill — backdrop-filter containing-block bug — with tap-away scrim); pill action language (radius-full on .bp-cta/.bp-button); copper-tick eyebrows; display masts with serif-italic copper accent word; hero rebuilt around a full-width scrubbable decade timeline instrument (tick rail, copper burn, play/pause key); map plate re-toned as artifact (ocean #080606 below ink, warm Page Sand landmass, sand hairline bounds, zoom clamped 3-10, restyled/hidden zoom ctrl); explore instruments stamped data-theme=dark (ink glass cockpit); /search rebuilt (display-scale query mast, pill facets, numbered ledger index); /history decade stepper restyled as baseline rail + auto-apply pill facet (FilterBar card removed); mega footer with landmark-scale lockup; fixed pre-existing nested-anchor hydration bug in HistoryResultList. Doc: v5.1 section prepended to design-direction-v5.md. 417/417 web tests pass; typecheck clean on touched files (pre-existing baseline errors untouched). Verified in-browser: desktop+375px, home/search/history/explore/about, drawer, timeline scrub.

v5.1.1 record-surface pass (session 4, owner verdict on v5.1: entity card/page still needed work, filters oversized/unsorted, must work for neurodivergent readers): codified a cognitive-accessibility law in design-direction-v5.md (summary-before-story, one record anatomy everywhere, literal fact labels, declared list order, chunked+numbered sections, one action per chunk, self-applying controls, legible motion, caps-as-slugs-only). Entity page gets an "At a glance" labeled-fact panel + numbered ruled sections + fail-closed relevance-explanation handling. NarrativeCard rebuilt to the same anatomy. Explore cockpit: FilterBar card replaced with self-applying pill facets; synchronized list gets a real stated sort (sortFeaturesForList). /search and /history get the same auto-apply pill treatment. Fixed an inherited-color bug (cockpit chrome ink-on-ink in light theme) and an RSC crash in HistoryGraphPanel (unconditional onClick spread with no handler). 420/420 web tests, typecheck at the pre-existing 11-error baseline.

Sub-bead work closed under this umbrella: repo-a417f565 (brand directory cleanup — deleted superseded SVGs/brand-pack/brand-reference/dead BlapLogo code, corrected every dangling brand-system/ doc reference discovered along the way), repo-d57be051 (decade/population presence modeling promoted into packages/domain — aggregateDecadePresence + buildDecadePresenceAggregates, client/server split for bundle safety, apps/web's decade-flow.ts refactored to consume it so density is genuinely ACTIVE-presence-scoped per decade rather than cumulative-only), repo-cc4841e7 (favicon/OG/social already regenerated in v5.1; this session wired the properly-sized renders into layout.tsx metadata, closing the last gap). Full monorepo test suite (all packages) verified 0 failures after every change.

title: Map: county boundary lines + county-aware entity circle placement/sizing
description: Owner ask 2026-07-17: the map should show county lines, considered both when placing entity circles and for their relative size. Scope: (1) county boundary asset at /geo (Census cartographic boundary 20m, public domain, lazy-loaded like us-states-20m.geojson); (2) county hairline layer in the explore style — subtler than state bounds, zoom-gated so the national frame stays clean; (3) restore the GL entity circle layers on the persistent MapStage (style builder emits halo/point/event-glyph/cluster layers but applyGeographyStyle never adds them — pins are invisible DOM buttons today); (4) zoom-interpolated marker radius so circles stay proportionate to county geography across zoom; (5) layer order: density fill < county lines < state bounds < jurisdiction/edges < halos < points.
close_reason: Landed in commit d7c48f6 and verified in-browser: Census 20m county asset (2.3MB, lazy-fetched at prefetch zoom, territories dropped) renders as zoom-gated hairlines beneath state bounds; GL entity marker stack now mounts on the persistent stage (halo/point/event-glyph/clusters — was invisible before, apply path skipped it); marker radius county-proportionate via top-level zoom interpolate (0.7x national → 1.35x locality; MapLibre rejects nested ['zoom'], hence the restructure + updated marker-size test); solid fills at 0.82 opacity so boundaries read through discs (owner ask); cluster clicks ease to split zoom; state/edge/background click handlers yield to entity hits. Verified: DC z11 shows both entities as kind-signature circles over county hairlines; AL z6.2 county source loaded 1,986 tile-features; 412 web tests pass. Follow-on verification (mobile 375px, perf traces) rides BB-101.

title: BB-099: Map visual language: brand-shade entity encoding, data-driven sizing, state labels
description:
Expansion bead BB-099 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief — the map must follow the site's design pattern.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("I want the entities to not all be the same color. I want that to match the colors or shades of the site colors or brand colors, and sizes based off of some combination of the data that we have about them. Also, there needs to be some indicator of the states, like NY for New York, FL for Florida.")
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-096; coordinates with BB-098 (same map surface, parallelizable)

Why: every entity currently renders as the same 11px Copper Pin circle (DIGNITY_PALETTE.point = #B86B2A for all); confidence is a glyph; state polygons are unlabeled shapes. Premier cartography needs an information-bearing visual language drawn from the brand system itself.

Deliver:
- Kind-to-shade encoding from the brand palette only: a distinct, dignity-compliant shade per entity kind present in the data (kind field today: place, school, event, institution — verify the live kind set at build time) drawn from Copper Pin #B86B2A, copper-dark #D07A32, Page Sand #D8A178, Stone #6D675F, and paper/cream tints. Shades must be mutually distinguishable on the dark desaturated basemap at 3:1 non-text contrast and paired with a non-color channel (marker shape or glyph) so color is never the only signal.
- Data-driven size encoding: marker radius derived from a documented combination of per-feature data (available today: evidenceCount, confidenceTier, notabilityLabels count, eraBuckets span). Recommended: evidenceCount as the primary weight with confidenceTier as a modifier. Min/max radius and interpolation defined as tokens; clusters keep count-based step sizing. The formula lives in one exported, unit-tested function — not inline in the style spec.
- State abbreviation labels: a symbol layer rendering postalCode (packages/domain/src/map/us-geography.ts — 51 entries incl. DC) at national/regional zooms, IBM Plex Mono caps (the guide's data/metadata register), Stone-on-dark; zoom-aware fade in/out; never colliding with markers; the selected state's label turns copper (the orientation moment per guide p.7).
- Legend: kinds, size scale, and confidence glyphs, rendered in the site's component language so the map explains itself without leaving it; keyboard and screen-reader accessible.
- Basemap conformance pass: every color in explore-style.ts / dark-archive-style.ts pulls from brand tokens — zero ad-hoc hex.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No red or alarm hues for violence-adjacent records; no crime-heat rendering (BB-051 rules carry forward).
- Color never the sole differentiator (WCAG 1.4.1); confidence glyphs (filled/half/empty circle) retained.
- No kind-shade assignment that could read as status, racial, or skin coding — shades encode ENTITY KIND only, stated plainly in the legend.

Model routing (guidance): sonnet-class throughout — the encoding is spec-able and test-covered (contrast pairs, size-formula unit tests); add a single opus-class review pass only if shade distinguishability on the dark basemap proves contentious in-browser.
design: Current: DIGNITY_PALETTE.point renders every entity as Copper Pin #B86B2A with a Page Sand halo; confidence is glyph-encoded (filled/half/empty circle), never color. Style builder: apps/web/src/app/map/explore-style.ts (cluster step sizing 18/24/30px). Per-feature properties available for sizing: evidenceCount, confidenceTier, notabilityLabels[], eraBuckets[], status. Guide p.7 roles: copper = orientation, stone = secondary, sand = decorative fill. State labels are a metadata register, so IBM Plex Mono caps per guide p.8. Postal codes: packages/domain/src/map/us-geography.ts (51 incl. DC).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Each entity kind renders a distinct brand-palette shade paired with a non-color channel; verified on the dark basemap at 3:1; documented in the legend. 2. Marker size derives from the documented data formula (exported, unit-tested); visually verified across the full seed set. 3. State abbreviations render at national/regional zooms in Plex Mono, fade by zoom, selected state turns copper. 4. Legend keyboard- and screen-reader-accessible. 5. Zero ad-hoc hex in map styles — tokens only. 6. Dignity and never-color-alone rules pass review; new contrast pairs added to the test suite.
close_reason: Kind-shade encoding, data-driven marker sizing, 51-state Plex Mono labels, accessible legend rewrite. All map-experience/domain tests pass (394+/396, 2 pre-existing unrelated). Committed 6e81c72.

metadata.modelRouting: sonnet throughout; single opus review pass only if shade distinguishability is contentious

title: BB-098: Premier map: cinematic zoom and seamless page-to-map transition
description:
Expansion bead BB-098 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief — the map is premier.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("the map has to be premier... YouTube Johnny Harris maps... if a user clicks on it, I would expect some kind of zoom-in... the user doesn't see a refresh of the map. Rather, they kind of zoom into the area that they clicked into, and the rest of the page fades away or transitions away smoothly, cleanly.")
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-096, BB-097; platform: BB-070, BB-051 (closed)

Why: today the homepage hero (HomeMapHero) navigates to /explore on click — a route change that remounts the MapLibre canvas: exactly the "refresh" the owner rejects. Premier means the map is one continuous cinematic surface: click a state or point in the hero and the camera flies into that area while the page chrome dissolves, landing in the full explore experience without the map ever blinking.

Deliver:
- Architecture spike, recorded as an ADR: how one MapLibre canvas persists across the hero-to-explore transition. Candidates — (a) layout-level persistent client canvas: the map lives in a shared App Router layout segment; hero and explore are UI states over the same instance; URL sync via history/shallow routing; (b) full-screen map app shell: the map is the base layer of the app and pages render as overlays above it; (c) View Transitions API over a remounted canvas — reject unless (a)/(b) prove infeasible, because it still remounts the map. Document tradeoffs: App Router constraints, SSR/hydration, memory, back/forward behavior, deep links.
- Cinematic camera grammar (the Johnny Harris register): flyTo/easeTo with authored curves — slow-out easing, long arcs, zoom that reads as descent into place rather than a jump cut. Camera paths defined as named presets (national -> state -> locality -> point) with durations/easings as motion tokens. State click flies to state bounds (us-geography bbox); point click flies to the point and reveals the narrative card.
- Page-dissolve transition: on hero map engagement the headline/rails fade or slide away (token-driven, 280ms-class), nav condenses, the map takes the full viewport; a clean reverse transition on back. Viewport + selection stay in the URL (shareable, as today).
- Reduced motion: prefers-reduced-motion gets instant cuts (opacity-only, no camera flight) with full functional parity.
- Performance: the transition holds 60fps-class smoothness on mid-tier hardware; no layout thrash during camera flight. Measure with DevTools traces and record real numbers — do not fabricate.
- Mobile: the same continuity contract at 375px (tap state -> camera flight -> full-screen map with sheet UI). Coordinate with open bead repo-b5a06269 (Map Phase 4: clustering/PMTiles/mobile sheets) — do not duplicate its scope.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No route transition that visibly remounts or reloads the map between hero and explore.
- No camera motion without the reduced-motion fallback.
- BB-051 dignity/redaction rules hold at every zoom: no coarsened point labeled as an exact address; precision-radius affordance preserved; clusters decompose to named entities within two interactions.

Model routing (guidance): opus-class for the architecture spike and camera-grammar design — this is the hardest interaction-engineering call in the tranche and a wrong architecture is expensive to unwind; sonnet-class for implementation against the chosen ADR. Verification is in-browser (run the app, drive the transition, record it), not test-only.
design: Reference register: Johnny Harris / Vox map storytelling — continuous camera, narrative descent into place, chrome that yields to the map. Current code: HomeMapHero click currently does router.push to /explore (the remount to eliminate); ExploreMapExperience.tsx orchestrator; ExploreMapCanvas.tsx (sole maplibre-gl dynamic import); explore-style.ts (clusterRadius 60, clusterMaxZoom 9). State bounds come from packages/domain/src/map/us-geography.ts bbox per state. MapLibre flyTo supports curve/speed/easing options — author named presets, never ship defaults. Camera presets and dissolve timings become motion tokens so BB-101 can audit them.
acceptance_criteria: 1. ADR committed for the persistent-canvas architecture with rejected alternatives recorded. 2. Clicking a state or point on the homepage hero flies the camera into that area with authored easing while page chrome dissolves — zero map remount (map instance identity preserved, no tile flash), landing in the full explore UI. 3. Back/forward and shareable URLs restore viewport + selection. 4. Reduced-motion parity (instant cuts, full function). 5. Transition performance measured and recorded on the final build. 6. Mobile continuity verified in-browser at 375px. 7. BB-051 dignity/redaction acceptance rules re-verified after the change.
notes:
Agent B (2026-07-18): hero flyPreset before engage for CTA/background/entity pins; entity pins route to /explore?selected=; explore entering animation (ds-explore-stage--entering); nav condense via :has(.ds-explore-stage); mount reconcile skipped when ds-map-transition latched on first render.
Agent A+B landed and pushed on origin/redesign/brand-map-beads: dc95cb6 (grouping off default, hero camera on explore landing), 3740218 (hero→explore transitions). AC#5 perf trace and AC#6 mobile sheets still open — BB-098 remains in_progress.

metadata.modelRouting: opus for architecture spike + camera grammar; sonnet for implementation against the ADR

comments.author: Gerald Dagher
comments.text: Agent A (map-journey): grouping off by default (?group=1 opt-in); MapStage clusteringEnabled initial false; hero→explore reconcileCamera skip when ds-map-transition flag set.

title: BB-097: Site redesign v3: modern, polished, the black experience
description:
Expansion bead BB-097 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("We are not beholden to any specific layout that we currently have. We want a redesign, and we want it modern... the black experience... clean... polished... we don't want it to feel secondhand.")
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-096

Why: the current shell is a competent but conventional document layout (header, hero card, rails). Owner direction is a ground-up visual redesign on the final brand language — modern, polished, premium, the black experience — with the map as the centerpiece of the experience rather than a widget in a card. Nothing about the current layout is a constraint.

Deliver:
- Redesigned public shell (header/nav/footer) and homepage on tokens v3: Archive Paper light theme and first-class Black Ink dark theme; copper reserved for orientation moments; Inter Display headlines at the guide's 48-72 display scale; Source Serif 4 for longform; IBM Plex Mono for metadata/citation registers.
- Hero: the national map IS the hero — full-bleed or near-full-bleed, with the headline and intro layered over or beside it ("may be behind text" per owner). The hero map is not an illustration; it is the live map surface BB-098's cinematic zoom launches from — architect the hero with that bead's persistent-canvas contract in mind. Design the text-over-map treatment so AA contrast holds on the dark basemap (scrims/panels allowed; no effects on the mark).
- Rework the remaining public surfaces (search, entity, history, topics, facts, methodology, myths, errata, about, legal, corrections, submit) onto the new shell so no page feels secondhand: same grid, type scale, spacing rhythm, radius language, and copper discipline. Kill leftover BB-007-era austerity (pure-white cards, zero-radius boxes) wherever it survives.
- Composition reference is guide p.9 (product expression): dark hero band + map panel; story rail beneath with kind/state slugs in mono caps (SCHOOL / NEW YORK), one-line story, hairline rules.
- Voice pass on shell copy per guide p.11: core line "History, pinned to place."; support line "People. Places. Evidence. Context."; specific over sweeping; evidence before assertion; pride without spectacle; invite, do not lecture.
- Motion: subtle and token-driven (existing 160/280ms curve); prefers-reduced-motion respected everywhere.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No trauma-forward or sensational imagery/copy — pride without spectacle.
- No generic "Black history" stock imagery, no automatic sepia filters, no AI images presented as documentary material (guide p.10 AVOID row).
- BB-051 dignity rules carry forward on every surface that touches the map or records.

Model routing (guidance): opus-class (or strongest available) for the design-direction pass — hero composition, layout grid, page-level art direction — where taste is the bottleneck; sonnet-class for component/CSS buildout once direction is fixed; haiku-class for mechanical reskins of long-tail pages. Do not run the whole bead on an expensive model: set direction first, then fan out cheap.
design: Composition reference: guide p.9 (product expression — dark hero band with map panel, story rail with mono-caps kind/state slugs, hairline rules), p.11 (voice), p.7 (copper discipline). Current shell: apps/web/src/app/page.tsx (HomeMapHero + rails) and shell.css (token-driven, 600+ lines). The hero must be architected WITH BB-098: the same MapLibre canvas instance survives the hero-to-explore transition — see that bead for the architecture options before fixing the hero DOM structure.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Homepage hero renders the live map as the dominant element with layered text passing AA on the dark basemap. 2. All public routes render on the new shell — no page retains the old white-card/zero-radius look. 3. Copper audit: orientation moments only, roughly 10-15% presence. 4. Type scale matches guide p.8; dark theme first-class; both themes verified in-browser with screenshots. 5. Voice pass applied to shell/nav/hero copy per guide p.11. 6. Reduced motion verified; all existing a11y tests pass.
notes:
Session 2026-07-18 verification pass (in-browser, apps/web dev server, both themes, desktop + 375px):
- CONFIRMED: homepage hero renders the live national map full-bleed with "History, pinned to place." / "People. Places. Evidence. Context." voice copy layered over it, readable in both themes (screenshots taken).
- CONFIRMED: dark theme (default) and light "Theme: Light" toggle both render correctly; map plate + on-map header/footer intentionally stay dark in light theme (theme-invariant surfaces per design-direction-v4 §6) - this is deliberate, not a bug.
- CONFIRMED: copper reserved for orientation (active nav underline, CTA fill, cluster/point bubbles, selected states) - no stray copper found outside those roles in the surfaces checked. Full pixel-composition % audit still needs BB-101's scripted sweep.
- CONFIRMED: all public routes reachable from nav render on the new shell (spot-checked via nav links); no legacy white-card look observed.
- PARTIAL: reduced-motion verified at the SOURCE level only this session (camera-presets.ts's prefersReducedMotion()/REDUCED_MOTION_CAMERA_PRESETS reviewed, correct) - did not force-emulate prefers-reduced-motion in a live browser this pass.
- NOT DONE THIS SESSION: full a11y test suite pass (contrast.test.ts already covered by existing tests; broader ARIA/keyboard sweep is BB-101 scope).
- Found and cleared a one-time stale .next dev-cache artifact (ReferenceError on first cold compile after the big bb->blap rename) that made the hero look broken in a screenshot; root cause was NOT a source bug, confirmed by a from-clean rebuild + fresh tab reproducing correctly. No code change needed for that; flagging in case it recurs for another dev.
- Cleanup: removed 3 confirmed-dead pre-ADR-017 files (apps/web/src/app/map/MapLibreCanvas.tsx, dark-archive-style.ts, map-source.seed.json) - unused since the (map) route-group rewrite, verified via grep + tsc --noEmit (0 new errors; same 5 pre-existing errors as before, all outside map code).
Explore map side panels: compact filter/toggle controls (sm radius, coherent outline register), quieter result-list selection (copper left rule + subtle fill), tighter metadata grid.

metadata.modelRouting: opus for design direction; sonnet for buildout; haiku for long-tail reskins

title: BB-096: Brand pack v3 adoption: tokens, type, radius, and mark reconciliation
description:
Expansion bead BB-096 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief (owner voice memo). Brand pack v3 was committed as brand-system/ in 68b3373; filenames lowercased, internal manifest/checksum references fixed, and web-served copies staged at apps/web/public/brand/ on branch redesign/brand-map-beads (this bead's precursor work, already done).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + brand-system/guide/black-book-brand-guide-final.pdf (14 pp, "Black Book Brand Identity System 3.0.0-final")
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-067, BB-068 (closed — this bead supersedes their token/type decisions wherever the final pack differs)

Why: the final brand pack and the shipped packages/ui tokens disagree on four load-bearing points. The site currently ships pure-white canvas (#FFFFFF), Sora display type, zero border radius, and a code-generated logo lockup (BlackBookLogo.tsx renders the wordmark as text beside the symbol). The final pack specifies Archive Paper canvas #F4EFE5 (+ Surface #FBF8F2), Inter Display + Inter, soft radii (8/16/28px), and an outlined-artwork lockup with the explicit misuse rule "Do not recreate it by typing the full name beside the symbol" — the current header and OG image violate that rule. Every other redesign bead builds on this foundation; it is the root of the tranche.

Deliver:
- Tokens v3 in packages/ui/src/tokens/ + styles/tokens.css: Archive Paper #F4EFE5 canvas, Surface #FBF8F2, Charcoal #161616, Stone #6D675F (secondary text, 4.9:1 on paper), Rule #D7D0C4 (hairlines), Copper Pin #B86B2A (graphic-scale only), copper text #8E4F2A on light (5.5:1) / #D07A32 on dark (6.1:1), Page Sand #D8A178. Dark theme stays first-class: paper/ink inversion per brand-system/tokens/black-book-brand.css.
- Radius tokens 8/16/28px (sm/md/lg) replacing the zero-radius rule; record the supersession in docs/ui/brand.md (BB-007's flat/zero-radius language is superseded by the final pack; bevels/shadows/gradients/glows remain banned everywhere).
- Typography: Inter Display (600-700) replaces Sora for display/headlines; Inter 400-600 for UI; Source Serif 4 400-600 editorial; IBM Plex Mono 400-500 data/citations. Fluid scale from guide p.8: display 48-72, H1 36-48, H2 24-32, body 16-18. Update next/font loading in apps/web/src/app/layout.tsx; everything stays free/open-source.
- Mark: the official artwork SVGs (apps/web/public/brand/black-book-primary-{light,dark}.svg + mark/compact variants) become the rendered source of truth for SiteHeader, admin masthead, favicon, and OG image; retire or refactor BlackBookLogo.tsx so no surface reconstructs the lockup by typing the name beside the symbol. Favicon route uses the pack favicon set (favicon.svg + PNG sizes); OG uses the pack OG images or regenerates from the primary artwork.
- Copper discipline encoded in docs: copper is roughly 10-15% of any composition, reserved for the moment of orientation (active locations, selected filters, primary actions, evidence markers) — guidance in docs/ui/brand.md plus a review checklist item, not a hard token.
- docs/ui/brand.md rewritten against the final pack; docs/ui/brand-reference/ old-package PNGs replaced or clearly marked superseded; stale packages/ui/src/brand/assets/bb-logo-*.svg retired.
