title: Fix unstable_cache 2MB overflow for live public entities
description:
## Outcome
Stop Next.js console failures when live entity catalogs exceed the 2MB data-cache limit while preserving correct map/search/history behavior and public-data dignity boundaries.

## Context
`listPublicEntityViews` → `loadLiveEntities` → `cachedLiveEntities` puts the full hydrated entity array in `unstable_cache`. Payloads ~2.4MB fail with: items over 2MB can not be cached.

## Acceptance
- Oversized live entity payloads no longer attempt to store the full array in Next data cache
- Map/search/history consumers still get correct public entity views
- No private data leaked into public cache
- Focused unit tests for public-data / map loading pass
acceptance_criteria: Oversized live catalogs do not blow Next 2MB cache; public consumers still correct; tests pass
close_reason: Fixed Next.js 2MB unstable_cache overflow for live public entity catalogs. Size-gate stores only a tiny oversized sentinel in Next data cache; fat payloads live in process-local TTL memory (public projections only). Added live-catalog-cache helpers + unit tests; public-data/map tests pass (30/30).

title: Align /history graph with site encoding + adaptive density
description:
## Outcome
Fix the /history relationship viz so it follows site design patterns and stays readable at catalog scale.

## Problems
1. Force-plot of up to 80 labeled nodes becomes a hairball with real data volume.
2. Nodes use ink fill instead of shared kind shade+glyph encoding from kind-encoding.ts / map legend.
3. Overview kind bars also use flat ink, inconsistent with KindBadge/map colors.

## Approach
1. Adaptive modes: aggregate kind hubs (default when many records), ego-neighborhood when a record is selected, full record graph only when filtered set is small (≤~24).
2. Always paint with kindEncodingFor shade + glyph; selection = copper ring; never invent new palette.
3. Labels only when sparse or on selected node; aggregate mode labels kinds + counts.
4. Compact kind legend reusing ds-legend-glyph vocabulary.
5. Overview kind bar fills use kind shades.
acceptance_criteria:
1. High-count views render kind-aggregate graph, not a labeled node hairball.
2. Selected record focuses to neighborhood viz.
3. Node fills/shapes match map kind encoding shades+glyphs.
4. Overview composition bars use the same kind shades.
5. Tests cover adaptive mode switching and encoding consistency.
close_reason: Adaptive graph modes (aggregate/neighborhood/records) + shared kind shade/glyph encoding across viz, overview, and chips. 47 history tests pass.

acceptance_criteria: No document scrollWidth > clientWidth at 320/768/1024; decade rail scrolls internally; layout stacks on narrow viewports.

title: Modernize /history: real graph viz, richer filters, responsive layout
description:
## Outcome
Redesign blackstory.app/history so the surface feels like a modern temporal graph browse: a real visual relationship graph (not a mislabeled list), richer filtering, engaging overview elements, and a fully responsive layout with no page-level horizontal scroll.

## Why
The live page labels a grouped list as \"History graph\", offers only kind/sort/search filters, and overflows horizontally because the decade rail expands ancestors instead of scrolling within a bounded width.

## Scope
1. Fix responsive overflow (decade rail, layout grid, toolbar, min-width:0 cascade).
2. Replace/supplement HistoryGraphPanel with an SVG network visualization + keep accessible list peer.
3. Add filters: status, topics/tags, has-connections, kind chips with counts; keep URL-shareable state.
4. Add engaging overview: decade density, kind composition, connection/record stats.
5. Preserve SSR/noscript GET filters, dignity rules (no violence heat), WCAG.

## Non-goals
No new chart libraries unless already in deps; no crime-heat colors; no Firebase/cloud apply.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Page never requires horizontal page scroll at common viewport widths (320–1440); decade rail scrolls internally.
2. Primary viz shows nodes+edges as a graph; list remains synchronized accessible peer.
3. At least status + has-connections (or topic) filters work via URL state and SSR/noscript.
4. Overview stats/composition visible above results.
5. Existing history tests updated/pass; new viz + filter + overflow tests added.
close_reason: Modernized /history: SVG relationship graph, richer filters (status/topic/connections + kind chips), overview strip, responsive overflow fix. 50+ unit tests passing.

title: [bug] Map records list should match filtered/visible map entities
description:
## Problem
The explore map records panel (e.g. '656 documented records in view') should list only entities currently filtered/displayed on the map — not a broader set.

- Changing filters or map view updates the list accordingly

title: [bug] Entity image credit shows Unknown author garbage
description:
## Problem
Entity pages show unacceptable image credit text like:
'Unknown authorUnknown author or not provided · Public domain · Wikimedia Commons · public domain'
and duplicated rights ('Momos · Public domain · Wikimedia Commons · public domain').
Mast photos also vary in aspect/crop across entities.

## Acceptance
- Placeholder/unknown author strings are not shown as creator credit
- Rights label is not duplicated with bare PD/license wording already in credit
- Credit line remains readable and brand-appropriate
- Mast photo + record-mark share a uniform 4:3 media box; object-fit cover (no stretch); person kinds bias object-position toward upper frame
close_reason: Credit sanitization + uniform 4:3 mast crop with person upper focal bias; display wired; tests pass

title: Deploy admin console publicly (App Hosting) + bulk actions for published catalog
description:
Deploy apps/admin publicly via Firebase App Hosting in the same black-book-efaaf project (pragmatic path, deferring ADR-012's Cloud Run+IAP+new-project design until an actual multi-operator team justifies it), wire NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_ORIGIN so the public web nav shows a working "Admin login" link at admin.blackstory.app, and add bulk decision/action support for the published entity catalog (currently read-only) mirroring the existing bulk-transition (research cases) and bulk-review (story packets) patterns.

Plan: /Users/geralddagher/.claude/plans/zesty-skipping-barto.md

Scope:
0. Rename GCP/Firebase project display name blacktory -> BlackStory (cosmetic).
A. apps/admin/apphosting.yaml + new App Hosting backend black-book-admin-production + admin.blackstory.app custom domain (Cloudflare DNS step needs owner - no Zone API token in 1Password) + NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_ORIGIN wired into apps/web/apphosting.yaml + one-off admin-claim bootstrap script.
B. New catalogDecisions Firestore collection + bulk-decision API route + catalog page bulk-select UI (flag_for_retraction/needs_review/clear) + audit logging + release-builder reads decisions when building the next release (actual publish stays gated behind existing signed-manifest privileged-apply flow, no bypass).
acceptance_criteria: Admin login nav link visible and working on blackstory.app; admin console reachable and gated by Firebase Auth+custom-claims; bulk decision action available on published catalog entities, audited, and wired into the release builder.
notes:
Both parts shipped and verified live 2026-07-19:

Part A: black-book-admin-production App Hosting backend (same project,
minInstances:0, admin-runtime@ service account, ADMIN_AUTH_MODE=firebase).
Fixed 3 real bugs found along the way: operator-cli cross-package deep
import breaking tsc rootDir, exactOptionalPropertyTypes violation in
quick-add/actions.ts, optional-field bug in editorial-run.ts's progress
callback. Also fixed the App Hosting build itself: a custom buildCommand
override disabled the framework adapter and broke the buildpack's own
pnpm-install step; replaced with a shared scripts/apphosting-build.mjs
(root apphosting:build script) that branches on APPHOSTING_BUILD_TARGET
(GOOGLE_BUILDABLE itself isn't forwarded into the build script's env).
NEXT_PUBLIC_ADMIN_ORIGIN wired into apphosting.yaml; "Admin login" nav
link confirmed live on blackstory.app, reaching the admin login page.

Part B: apps/admin/src/catalog/catalog-decisions-store.ts + POST
/api/catalog/bulk-decision + catalog page bulk-select UI (flag_for_retraction
/needs_review/clear_flag, cap 50, mandatory audited reason) — mirrors the
existing research-case bulk-transition / story-packet bulk-review pattern
exactly. Verified live: /catalog returns 200, bulk-decision route registered
(401 unauthenticated, not 404). release-builder.ts's buildReleaseEntityArtifacts
takes an optional catalogDecision context field and fails closed with a new
'catalog_decision_retracted' reason — pure-function gate only, tested.

Deliberately NOT done this pass (documented, not silently dropped):
- Wiring a real Firestore catalogDecisions fetch into
  packages/firebase/scripts/publish-national-catalog.ts so a real release
  build actually reads live decisions (the gate exists and is tested, but
  the publish script doesn't call it with real data yet).
- admin.blackstory.app custom domain — needs a Cloudflare DNS record from
  the owner (no Zone API token in 1Password, only dashboard login).
- Bootstrapping the owner's actual Firebase Auth admin account + custom
  claim (no seed script exists for this yet either).
close_reason: Admin console deployed publicly (App Hosting, same project) with a working nav link, and bulk catalog decisions shipped end-to-end. Remaining pieces (custom domain DNS, admin user bootstrap, wiring the publish script to actually read decisions) are owner-gated or scoped-out follow-ups, tracked in the close notes.

title: Wire QueryClientProvider and data-layer composition root in apps/mobile
description: Independently flagged by MOB-013, MOB-014, and MOB-015: apps/mobile has no app-root composition wiring anywhere - no QueryClientProvider, no shared bootstrap-sync trigger on launch, nothing connecting MOB-009's tested data-layer primitives (transport, ReleaseCache, TanStack Query + SQLite persister) into the actual React tree at apps/mobile/src/app/_layout.tsx. Every feature module (search, entity, explore, learn) currently composes its own lazily-memoized runtime singleton independently as a workaround, which works but means: (1) bootstrap-sync/release-stamp comparison isn't actually triggered app-wide on launch (each feature's requests aren't governed by one shared bootstrap check), (2) potential for divergent runtime configuration per feature, (3) no single place to wire App Check initialization, log redaction setup, or the enforcement-mode config from MOB-010. Needs a real integration pass: add a single composition root in _layout.tsx (or an app-level provider file) that initializes App Check, runs bootstrap-sync on launch, provides one QueryClient instance to the whole tree, and have each feature consume it via context instead of an independent singleton.

title: Wire real @repo/public-contracts import into apps/mobile (currently vendored/duplicated)
description: MOB-009 found apps/mobile cannot actually import @repo/public-contracts (isolated npm lockfile, no pnpm workspace symlink to @repo scope) - types were vendored into apps/mobile/src/data/contracts.ts and release.ts with source citations as a stopgap. This is a real drift risk: the contracts package can change without apps/mobile noticing. Root cause is the same apps/mobile pnpm-workspace integration gap tracked in repo-oamr (lockfile break) - likely needs the same integration-owner pass: either get apps/mobile properly resolving pnpm workspace packages, or establish a generated/synced type-export step from packages/public-contracts into apps/mobile as a deliberate boundary (Metro doesn't always play well with pnpm symlinks for RN, so a workspace symlink may not be the right fix - needs a real decision, not just a lockfile patch). Also: MOB-009 found the pre-existing committed apps/mobile/package-lock.json nests expo-modules-core incorrectly, breaking a clean 'npm ci' for jest-expo resolution.

Register the @maplibre/maplibre-react-native withMapLibre config plugin in apps/mobile/app.config.ts and set USE_FRAMEWORKS=static via expo-build-properties, reconciled with RN Firebase's identical static-frameworks requirement (MOB-010). Then run expo prebuild --platform ios + pod install as real native evidence. Also device-based: cold/warm payload + memory traces, live CDN range-request 206 verification against a deployed PMTiles archive, low-memory-device cycling, attribution-not-occluded screenshots. Blocked on the production PMTiles archive authoring and a coordinated build-config pass (app.config.ts is outside MOB-011 exclusive ownership).

title: Independent review: release-activation state machine (MOB-005)
description: packages/domain/src/publication/{mobile-bootstrap,release-activation}.ts (branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit c4a2fe6) implements the release CAS/activation/rollback/GC state machine with 21 new + 58 regression tests passing and a verified transitive-redaction proof. Bead's own model routing calls for independent strong review before trusting it for production release safety. 3 specific concerns flagged by the builder: (1) concurrency is a synchronous in-memory CAS - a real Firestore-backed ReleaseStore adapter must implement an equivalent transactional CAS, not yet built/verified; (2) garbage collection retains only active+immediately-previous release - confirm this is the intended rollback depth policy, not an oversight; (3) mobile bootstrap compat constants are kept in sync with apps/api-public's /v1/bootstrap handler by convention/code-review only, no compile-time or test-level drift check exists yet.

title: Resolve App Check outage fail-open/closed conflict: threat model vs @repo/security rate-limits
description: Mobile threat model (docs/mobile/security/threat-model.md, Threat 2) requires read endpoints to fail OPEN (degrade to rate-limited anonymous access) during an App Check outage or transient attestation failure, so a legitimate-but-unattested client isn't locked out. But the EXISTING shared packages/security/src/rate-limits.ts (lines ~476-482, evaluateQuota) hard-denies any 'expensive_read' cost-tier request (e.g. search) with app_check_required for callers without a verified App Check token - there is no outage/degraded-mode carve-out today. This is not mobile-specific: apps/api-public's real search endpoint (built in MOB-004) inherits this exact behavior today, and it's shared code apps/web could also depend on. Fixing it means changing platform-wide security policy (packages/security), which is a judgment call for the security/owner review, not something to change unilaterally inside the mobile program. Options: (a) add an explicit outage-detection signal that relaxes expensive_read to rate-limited-anonymous only when App Check's own service is confirmed down (not just an unverified token), (b) accept current hard-deny behavior for expensive_read and revise the threat model's fail-open claim to apply only to static_read (which is already correctly ungated today), (c) something else. Needs an explicit decision, then a small, reviewed change to packages/security if (a) is chosen.

title: Fix pnpm workspace lockfile break from apps/mobile
description: apps/mobile/package.json now exists under the apps/* workspace glob (pnpm-workspace.yaml). Confirmed real evidence (MOB-006, 2026-07-19): pnpm install --frozen-lockfile at repo root fails with ERR_PNPM_OUTDATED_LOCKFILE once apps/mobile is present, and npx expo install inside apps/mobile auto-shells to pnpm add, mutating the 5000+ line root pnpm-lock.yaml. ADR-020-mobile-stack.md proposes a fix (exclude apps/mobile from the root pnpm workspace resolution, e.g. a pnpm-workspace.yaml negation entry or public-hoist-pattern scoping) but that edit to root workspace config was correctly out of scope for the agent session that found it (root pnpm-workspace.yaml is a shared-resource file). Needs an integration-owner pass: verify root pnpm install/build/test are unaffected for web/admin/api-* apps after whatever fix is applied.
notes: Additional evidence (MOB-004, 2026-07-19): root node_modules/@types/node is missing/unhoisted after apps/mobile + packages/public-contracts + apps/api-public installs ran independently. Each package's own 'tsc -p tsconfig.json' (run from within that package dir, resolving its own local node_modules symlinks into the pnpm store) passes cleanly and was verified directly - so this is not a functional break in any package's own build/test, but IDE/whole-workspace-level type checking sees false 'Cannot find module node:*' errors for apps/api-public/src/{server,handlers,index,responses}.ts and packages/public-contracts's excluded test files. Likely the same root cause as the apps/mobile lockfile break: adding apps/mobile disrupted normal pnpm workspace hoisting. Same integration-owner pass should fix both.

title: Accept MOB-002 architecture ADRs (ADR-020..023) and mobile threat model
description: 4 ADRs (docs/adr/ADR-020-mobile-stack.md, ADR-021-mobile-data-boundary.md, ADR-022-mobile-state-cache-offline.md, ADR-023-mobile-build-release.md) and docs/mobile/security/threat-model.md are drafted, fact-checked against the live repo, and passed an independent adversarial red-team (every open question resolved, no invented facts, internally consistent). Status is deliberately left 'Proposed' — an agent should not self-declare ADR acceptance for a program-wide architecture decision. Owner review needed to flip Status to Accepted (or send back for revision). Located on branch redesign/mobile-app-build (isolated worktree, not yet merged). Real technical spike backing ADR-020: a disposable Expo app with MapLibre Native + RN Firebase App Check + expo-sqlite installed clean, expo-doctor 20/20, iOS simulator pod install/build succeeded with USE_FRAMEWORKS=static.

title: HUMAN: deploy lineage UI fix to production
description: UI fix 9a03024 on redesign/brand-map-beads resolves false zero independent sources (EntityEvidencePanel uses citation-derived lineage). Firestore backfill complete 2026-07-19. Production App Hosting deploys pinned main SHA via workflow_dispatch (Deploy Production). Steps: merge redesign/brand-map-beads → main (or cherry-pick 9a03024), run staging validation, dispatch deploy-production with commit SHA + confirm=deploy. Verify Gabriel page shows non-zero lineage.
notes:
RESOLVED 2026-07-19. Full reconciliation completed instead of a narrow cherry-pick:
merged redesign/brand-map-beads (98 commits: rebrand, admin portal, App Hosting build
fixes, mobile epic docs, Corsair discovery pipeline) into main via merge commit 520fda0,
resolving 11 file conflicts (evidence-lineage code took the branch's evolved version;
seed-catalog files combined both bodies of content: Dunbar-lineage + 15 national-catalog
stories/entities/facts). Fixed @black-book/* import scope drift introduced by a prior
direct-to-main port (actual scope is still @repo/* — see corrected brand-agnostic-code-trio
memory) and added missing derivedFromClaimIds/derivedFromRelationshipIds on all 28
national-story facts. Updated 7 pre-existing seed-catalog test assertions (explore/search/
facts count expectations) to match the combined dataset; confirmed next build + full
workspace test suite green except pre-existing unrelated failures (filed as repo-8p1w).
Pushed to origin/main at 0fafb12. Rolled out staging then production via
firebase apphosting:rollouts:create at 0fafb12bfcb94506bfdf4779c1a79a40f0608d78; verified
both staging and production Gabriel's Rebellion page now show "2 independent sources"
(was 0). Deploy Production GH workflow remains dry-run only per its own design — this was
always meant to be a human/agent CLI rollout.

title: Fix false '0 independent sources' source lineage on entity evidence
description:
## Problem
Entity evidence Research coverage shows '0 independent sources across this record’s evidenced claims' for many records that have citations/evidenced claims. This is untrue.

## Root cause
Public claim projections and toEvidenceClaimInputs never carry independentLineageCount. EntityEvidencePanel falls back to totalSourceLineageCount which sums missing claim sourceLineage to 0, then always renders that zero.

## Acceptance
- Do not display '0 independent sources' when lineage data is absent/unknown
- Wire independentLineageCount through public claim projection → PublicClaimView → EvidenceClaimInput.sourceLineage when available
- For cited public claims lacking stored lineage, derive a non-zero record rollup from unique citation sources (public proxy) so seeded/catalog records are honest
- Tests cover adapter mapping, panel no-zero-when-missing, and rollup with citations
acceptance_criteria: No false zero lineage; lineage wired or citation-derived; tests pass
close_reason: Fixed false 0 independent sources: panel resolves lineage from claim counts or unique citations (never synthesizes 0); optional independentLineageCount wired through public claim projection path; citation-proxy kept at record rollup only to avoid overcounting.

title: Source-policy audit: blackwomenleadproject.org (Black Women Lead)
description:
## Problem statement
Owner confirmed blackwomenleadproject.org as the intended community source formerly labeled “Black Women in STEM.” Identity is documented; rights, retention, living-person, and acquisition method are not yet approved.

## Why necessary
All sources start disabled. Robots allow ≠ terms/rights. Site includes living people and portrait art. WP surface is thin (stub feed); bios may live with BPL/Northeastern partners.

## Verified current behavior
See docs/research/black-women-in-stem-source-identification.md (2026-07-19 no-persist probe): robots allow public paths; RSS is Hello World; no privacy/terms pages found; homepage describes 212 Boston leaders; STEM not site-exclusive.

## Target behavior
Completed source-policy envelope (rights, robots evidence archived, retention, lineage, living-person, rate limits) with register-disabled decision. Prefer partner structured bios if clearer rights. Fixtures only after rights allow.

## Non-goals
Live scrape; bulk image retention; enabling adapter; publishing from this source alone; inventing STEM-only framing.

## Dependencies
Blocked by nothing for desk research; blocked for fetch/canary until this audit closes with evidence.

## Acceptance criteria
Policy record + archived robots snapshot hash; terms/license verdict or explicit unknown-with-owner-contact plan; living-person controls; acquisition method recommendation; disabled source registration stub or documented deferral; no uncontrolled crawl.
acceptance_criteria: Policy verdict documented; source remains disabled; no live crawl; living-person and image-rights risks recorded

title: Corsair overnight hybrid LLM discovery+enrichment (~1k records)
description: Wire multithreaded overnight job on Corsair via Tailscale using OpenRouter free router + local Ollama. Target ~1k additional records. Proper failure handling, efficiency, tested end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: Job runs on Corsair; OpenRouter key from 1Password; Ollama failover; concurrency; failure handling; smoke test fires; path to ~1k overnight
notes:
Survivor landing verified 2026-07-19: --queue-survivors --commit writes researchCases/{uuid} (state=candidate) + submissionInbox on Firebase project black-book-efaaf only (no separate DEV project). Corsair discovery.env: FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID=black-book-efaaf, APP_FIREBASE_ALLOW_PRODUCTION=1, COMMIT_SURVIVORS=1. Tonight's SearXNG phase committed 166 unique researchCaseIds (run 2026-07-19T06-43-43Z); Firestore get confirmed sample doc. Admin review: redesign/brand-map-beads + apps/admin .env.local targeting black-book-efaaf → http://localhost:3001/console/candidate-queue (also /console/research-cases, /inbox, /cases). main console is fixture-only — will not show survivors. Overnight service still running (Phase 3 enrichment). Not a wrong-project bug; do not retarget commits until ADR-012 internal/staging exists.
Diagnosis 2026-07-19 (itemCount:0 chase):
- NOT empty subjects / Phase-3 short-circuit / ENRICH_MAX=0.
- subjects-2026-07-19T06-43-43Z.json count=622; Phase 3 started 02:53 EDT, finished 04:09.
- Enrichment DID run (~76m wall); partial decisions visible in truncated run log (~19 subjectIds; mix keep/reject/needs_evidence; openrouter+ollama).
- Root cause: run-2026-07-19T06-43-43Z.json truncated at exactly 65536 bytes (Linux pipe default). overnight script used `node … | tee LOG`; multi-MB console.log JSON not fully flushed before process exit → invalid JSON → summary parser items=[] → itemCount:0 (parseError path). Service still exited 0.
- Evidence: .cache/overnight-enrichment/summary|run|subjects-2026-07-19T06-43-43Z.json; journalctl --user -u blackstory-overnight-enrichment.service around 04:09:29.
- Fix: enrichment-run --output (sync writeFile) + --omit-raw-model; overnight script fail-closes on parseError/0 items. Corsair needs rsync of script+cli before next overnight.
Fix landed on redesign/brand-map-beads @ 95958ad (repo-6nr7.1 closed). Corsair rsync still required for scripts/run-overnight-hybrid-enrichment.sh + packages/operator-cli/src/cli.ts before next overnight; do not restart long overnight unless asked.
Corsair synced 2026-07-19 from Mac 95958ad (redesign/brand-map-beads): scripts/run-overnight-hybrid-enrichment.sh, packages/operator-cli/src/cli.ts (+ cli.test.ts), docs/runbooks/overnight-hybrid-enrichment.md. Verified --output/--omit-raw-model on Corsair; SHA256 match. Timer still enabled/active — next Mon 2026-07-20 02:15:01 EDT (~14h). Service not restarted (last run exited 0 at 04:09; inactive waiting for timer).
Bounded triage apply 2026-07-19 (~13:49 EDT, triage-corsair-candidates.ts --apply on black-book-efaaf): 262 overnight survivors inventoried; 84 excluded (listicle/spam/low-signal), 96 insufficient_evidence, 82 relevance_review (high-signal); 0 remaining candidate. Transitions 442/442 succeeded. Artifacts: .cache/corsair-triage/apply-summary.json, enrichment-subjects.json (60 cap).
Post-triage mock enrichment pass 2026-07-19: 60/60 keep on relevance_review subset (.cache/corsair-triage/enrichment-mock.json); staging only — no --commit. Human review queue: /console/research-cases filtered relevance_review (82).
Corsair candidate triage completed 2026-07-19 (admin ADC black-book-efaaf):
- Inventoried 258 state=candidate researchCases (+2 arrived during run); joined submissionInbox URLs.
- Heuristic buckets: reject 84 | needs_evidence 96 | promote_worthy 82 (scripts/triage-corsair-candidates.ts).
- Applied admin transitions (send_to_relevance → exclude/needs_evidence): excluded 84, parked 96 insufficient_evidence, sent 82 to relevance_review. Original Corsair candidate queue now 0.
- Hybrid enrichment (top 60 promote_worthy, openrouter/free + Ollama qwen3:8b, concurrency 4, ~13m): keep 53 | reject 1 | needs_evidence 6 | servedBy openrouter 14 / ollama 40 / hybrid 6.
- Staged quarantine: committed 53 solid keeps (confidence ≥0.6, no validationIssues) via commit-enrichment-keeps.ts → 53 Editorial:* draft cases in candidate (expected packet staging, not catalog publish).
- Post-enrichment dispositions on relevance_review subset: +1 excluded, +6 insufficient_evidence.
- Remaining operator queues: relevance_review 75 (Corsair leads awaiting confirm_relevance), editorial candidate 53 (staged packets), insufficient_evidence 102, excluded 85. No promotion/release activation performed.
- Artifacts: .cache/corsair-triage/{apply-summary.json,enrichment-run.json,enrichment-commit-summary.json,enrichment-subjects.json}
- CLI footgun fixed in ops: use `--catalog-from firestore` (space), not `--catalog-from=firestore`, or `--provider` is swallowed and mock provider runs.

title: Epic: Entity acquisition, identification, enrichment, agent-native research
description:
## Problem statement
Discovery, scraping, entity ID/resolution, deterministic + LLM enrichment, story prep, and agent workflows are fragmented. Operators and coding agents cannot run outcome-oriented research safely without knowing internal JSON/CLI/package details.

## Why necessary
Need one coherent, source-governed research system with LLMs never publishing/merging/approving rights, agent-discoverable CLI/MCP, and preserved lane semantics.

## Target behavior
Shared source identity/policy substrate; resumable acquisition; query-plan v2; deterministic enrichment before LLM; mentions/claims distinct from canonical entities; ER v2; durable LLM jobs; agent-native CLI/MCP; vertical slices A–E.

## Non-goals
K8s migration; public write to canonical; uncontrolled live crawl in CI; second provenance/job/source/entity systems.

## Scope
Full program per owner brief on branch redesign/brand-map-beads. Child beads cover workstreams 1–12. Execution continues after bead graph creation.

## Acceptance
Final acceptance criteria 1–35 from the program brief, with demonstrated vertical slices and pushed commits.

title: Honest why-this-appears: cite sources, not internal rubric alone
description:
## Problem statement
Public entity pages render a "Why this appears" / notability basis block on nearly every record. For seed and many projected records the basis is synthesized from `notabilityLabels` with empty `evidenceIds`, while adapters inject generic thematic/geographic "evidence" that is not a source citation. Readers see an auditable-sounding inclusion claim without checkable references. That is inaccurate relative to how the corpus was assembled (resourced/ingested from external sources) and reads as dishonest or unneeded boilerplate.

## Why necessary
Brand and product invariants require evidence before assertion, citations on public claims, and no completeness/authority overclaim. Inclusion reasons must resolve to retained source evidence, not BlackStory-internal rubric text alone.

## Verified current behavior
- `apps/web/src/app/entity/[id]/page.tsx` always mounts Relevance / Why this appears when `buildWhyThisAppearsForEntity` succeeds.
- `apps/web/src/app/entity/[id]/adapters.ts` synthesizes `notabilityBasis` from `notabilityLabels` with `evidenceIds: []` when structured basis is missing; injects fixed thematic + geographic RelevanceEvidence for every entity.
- `WhyThisAppears` shows criterion label + note and only a count ("N documented sources") when evidenceIds exist — not citation labels/hrefs.
- Seed entities in `public-seed.ts` have real claim citations but those are not wired into the why-appears basis UI.

## Target behavior
- Why-this-appears only asserts inclusion when linked to resolvable claim/citation evidence (source label + href when public).
- Notes explain the specific reason for this record, not a repeated rubric sentence.
- When evidence linkage is missing, show an honest gap (or omit the assertive basis), never empty-evidence "auditable" theater.
- Copy frames BlackStory as assembling/citing sources, not originating the historical facts.
- Generic topic-tag / jurisdiction metadata must not be presented as corroborating source evidence.

## Scope
Public why-appears composer, entity adapters, WhyThisAppears UI, seed/catalog projection wiring for evidenceIds→citations, tests.

## Non-goals
Full research-program rewrite; enabling new sources; changing constitution relevance gates themselves beyond honest public presentation.

## Acceptance criteria
- No public record shows "documented sources" without named, linkable citations (or explicit gap copy).
- Seed entities surface their claim citations inside why-appears basis.
- Boilerplate thematic/geographic lines are not labeled as source evidence.
- Tests cover empty evidenceIds, dangling ids, and citation rendering.
acceptance_criteria: Public why-this-appears links inclusion to real citations or honest gaps; no empty-evidence auditable theater; tests pass
notes: Implemented: release-builder claim-specific cited notes; WhyThisAppears renders named citation links; adapters rewrite rubric-only notes + wire claim evidenceIds; no topic/jurisdiction-as-evidence. Tests: domain release-builder + web WhyThisAppears + snapshot-search-index.
close_reason: Why-this-appears now shows claim-specific cited notes and named citation links; rubric-only dumps rewritten at read path; tests green. Commit 41ddbfe.

title: Link card/page metadata (Where, Era, Evidence) to site views

title: MOB-007: Generate native brand tokens, assets, and accessible UI primitives
description:
Outcome
The mobile app implements brand v3 through generated tokens and native primitives without copying web CSS or degrading accessibility.

Scope
Generate TypeScript color, spacing, radius, typography, motion, and logo constraints from `brand-system` source. Add native Text, Button, Link, Surface, Notice, Badge, Divider, EmptyState, ErrorState, Image, and sheet/list primitives needed by the program.

Requirements
Generated files are deterministic and never hand-edited. Preserve approved palette and flat matte rules. Bundle licensed fonts correctly and verify font licensing. Use platform-native press/focus/accessibility semantics. Support light/dark app surfaces while keeping the map's accepted fixed register unless superseded. Dynamic Type, high contrast, reduced motion, minimum touch size, loading/error/empty/offline states, and right-to-left safety are designed from the start. Logos use official assets, clear space, and minimum size.

Adversarial review
Token drift, incorrect color contrast, font fallback, missing glyphs, asset rasterization, huge font clipping, dark-mode logo inversion, accessibility label duplication, press-state invisibility, and arbitrary one-off styling. Verify no skin-tone classification or decorative anonymous portrait system enters the primitives.

Tests
Token generation snapshot; no-drift CI; unit/accessibility tests; contrast checks; font loading on both platforms; large text and dark/light screenshots; touch target assertions; screen-reader labels; asset checksum against brand source.

Model routing
Balanced design-capable coding model. Fast model may implement generator after formats are locked. Human visual approval required.

Exclusive ownership
brand token generator and apps/mobile/src/ui/**. Do not edit web UI in parallel.

Non-goals
No cross-platform component package until measured reuse warrants it.

Evidence to close
Generated token diff, component catalog on devices, accessibility/contrast report, owner visual sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: apps/mobile/src/ui built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit 03187e6. Deterministic token generator reads brand/tokens/{colors,typography}.json + brand.css with a WCAG contrast gate (caught and fixed 2 real inaccessible values). Verified independently: tsc --noEmit clean, 66/66 tests across 13 suites passing (jest-expo). 12 accessible native primitives (Text/Button/Link/Surface/Notice/Badge/Divider/EmptyState/ErrorState/Image/ListRow/Logo), EntityMark geometric non-figurative placeholder mirroring web's EntityRecordMark (honors no-avatar non-goal), fonts Sora/Inter/Source Serif 4/IBM Plex Mono all SIL OFL via @expo-google-fonts. FINDING for web owner: brand/tokens/colors.json (current BlackStory v1.0 guide) differs from packages/ui's shipped web palette (still older 'Pinned Page' colors) - the rebrand updated logo assets but not packages/ui's tokens; mobile correctly follows current brand/tokens, web/mobile palette drift needs separate reconciliation. Deferred, explicitly not self-approved: bead requires 'Human visual approval' - pending, screenshot-diff visual QA not run.

metadata.modelRouting: Balanced design-capable coding model. Fast model may implement generator after formats are locked. Human visual approval required.

title: MOB-008: Implement mobile navigation, deep links, and state restoration
description:
Outcome
Users can navigate Explore, Search, Learn, More, nested content, and canonical links predictably on both platforms, including cold starts and process restoration.

Scope
Implement Expo Router structure, four primary tabs, nested stacks/sheets, typed route parameters, web URL mapping, Apple Universal Links, Android App Links, development scheme, back behavior, and restoration rules.

Requirements
Canonical HTTPS links map to one route parser shared with tests. Validate all parameters and discard malformed or unsafe actions. Entity URLs fall back to web when app is absent. Back behavior follows platform conventions. Selection/filter/camera state is shareable only where useful and bounded. Sensitive correction content is never encoded in URLs. Associated-domain files are served from controlled domains. Restore safe navigation state after process death without restoring stale incompatible DTOs.

Adversarial review
Malformed percent encoding, duplicate params, overlong URLs, unknown entity IDs, open redirects, path traversal strings, link to withdrawn record, app installed with stale schema, repeated link while locked, and Android intent interception. Universal links must not trigger destructive actions.

Tests
Parser property/fuzz tests; route integration tests; iOS universal-link verification; Android Digital Asset Links verification and adb tests; cold/warm/background launch; back-stack tests; process restoration; web fallback; malformed-link corpus.

Model routing
Balanced coding model; strong review for link security and state-restoration edge cases.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile/src/app/** route tree and link configuration. Coordinate app config through scaffold owner.

Non-goals
No marketing attribution links or third-party dynamic-link service.

Evidence to close
Route map, association-file proof, device test recordings, fuzz report, web fallback verification.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Expo Router navigation built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit 7d351c6. 4 tabs (Explore/Search/Learn/More consolidating web's actual nav from packages/config/src/shell-nav.ts), entity/[id] stack push, filters-sheet + corrections/submit modals, +not-found catch-all safe fallback. Shared route-params parser (no new deps added - apps/mobile has its own isolated npm lockfile, correctly avoided touching package.json outside scope). Real verified: 38/38 fuzz tests on the parser (malformed %-encoding, double-encoded traversal, oversized ids, open-redirect attempts in query+returnTo, prototype-pollution-shaped restoration blobs all safely fall back), 104/104 total jest tests, typecheck 0 errors, eslint clean. Correction content confirmed never touches a URL param. Universal links: app.config.ts wired (production-only), AASA/assetlinks.json committed as local TEMPLATES with explicit TODO markers - structurally correct but NOT publishable until real Apple Team ID + Android release signing SHA-256 exist (ties to repo-fsxq human gates); web-fallback behavior is inherent to correct AASA/assetlinks config once real values land, no mobile code needed. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-020 (repo-5os2), and universal links need repo-fsxq's Apple/Google identifiers before real device verification is possible.

metadata.modelRouting: Balanced coding model; strong review for link security and state-restoration edge cases.

title: MOB-012: Implement the native Explore map, synchronized list, filters, and narrative sheet
description:
Outcome
Explore delivers the premier map-led experience on iOS and Android with native performance, full list parity, dignity rules, shareable selection, and graceful failure.

Scope
Build MapLibre Native screen, one-instance lifecycle, national/state/locality/point camera presets, clusters, state density, history edges where supported, filter sheet, synchronized result list, entity preview sheet, selection/restoration, loading/offline/error states, and map fallback.

Requirements
All public coordinates come from released redacted artifacts. Aggregates render before points. Clusters resolve to names within two interactions. List and map stay synchronized without focus theft. Filters are deterministic and reflected in result counts. Named motion presets support reduced motion. Tap targets meet accessibility minimums. A failed map leaves list/search usable. No raw MapLibre expression is duplicated when a shared pure style contract can be extracted safely. Do not promise persistent map continuity identical to unimplemented web ADR behavior; document native behavior and intentional differences.

Adversarial review
Rapid pan/filter/search races, repeated mount/unmount, background memory pressure, map style failure, cluster overlap, malicious labels, empty state, 100k points, withdrawn selection after release change, stale camera restore, screen-reader interaction, and denied network. Verify no hidden exact location emerges through zoom or radius.

Tests
Pure camera/filter/selection tests; RNTL screen tests; Maestro map/list/entity flows; map failure injection; reduced motion; VoiceOver/TalkBack list alternative; performance/memory cycles; low-end device; screenshots across sizes/themes; release-change invalidation.

Model routing
Strong model for interaction architecture and red-team, balanced coding model for implementation, fast model only for locked fixture/test expansion.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile Explore/map feature paths. Shared map contracts require MOB-003 owner; app routes require MOB-008 coordination.

Non-goals
No turn-by-turn navigation, user pins, or user-generated map edits.

Evidence to close
Device videos, performance traces, accessibility walkthrough, map failure proof, parity matrix against approved web snapshot.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Explore map experience built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit 9684697, extending MOB-011's spike backward-compatibly (its tests still green). Native clustering, dignity-safe cluster bubbles, one-shot token-driven camera (zoom hard-capped at 12), named national/state/locality/point presets, no-focus-theft controller, safe selection restoration, entity preview sheet using MOB-007 primitives (no new deps). Real verified: 71 tests in own suites, 565/568 full mobile suite (3 failures were MOB-013's own in-flight work, not this bead's), tsc clean, eslint clean. Real privacy invariant test: member coords byte-identical, cluster marker coarsened to coarsest member, zoom never >12, no interpolation that could de-redact. Clusters resolve to names within 2 interactions (tested, incl. coincident-point case). Withdrawn-selection-after-release reconciliation tested. Malicious label handling (1M-char/bidi/zero-width) tested. Failed-map-leaves-list-usable tested. Rapid mount/unmount leak test (bounded, not exact, due to RNTL concurrent-flush nondeterminism - flagged for device verification). State density and history edges explicitly deferred - MOB-011's demo source has neither, documented as intentional native difference per bead's own 'do not promise unimplemented behavior' clause. NEEDS RED-TEAM per 3 flagged concerns: (1) one-way camera-command token vs real MapLibre onRegionDidChange feedback loop - could echo as a pan and desync list, (2) viewport-membership filtering on redacted points - verify no edge-inference leak, (3) leak test needs device/Maestro confirmation of true subscription balance. Left claimed/open per its own honesty: evidence-to-close requires Maestro/device artifacts this sandbox cannot produce. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-020/024 plus red-team plus device evidence.

metadata.modelRouting: Strong model for interaction architecture and red-team, balanced coding model for implementation, fast model only for locked fixture/test expansion.

title: MOB-013: Implement bounded mobile search and filters
description:
Outcome
Search returns transparent, bounded, cancellable results online and from compatible cached content without exposing hidden ranking or allowing query cost abuse.

Scope
Build search screen, input normalization, recent local searches, deterministic filters, pagination, loading/zero/error/offline states, result cards, cancellation, and deep links.

Requirements
Do not send a request below the agreed query threshold unless browsing mode is explicit. Debounce and cancel superseded requests. Store recent terms locally only and provide clear/remove controls. Do not log query text. Explain applied filters and release/degraded state. Results never expose numeric relevance/notability. Offline search is limited to cached compatible release and labeled as such. Manual place search is the alternative to location permission.

Adversarial review
Unicode confusables, huge paste, rapid typing, stale overwrite, duplicate pages, cursor reuse across release, network flap, empty query enumeration, malicious snippets, screen-reader focus churn, and cache with withdrawn result.

Tests
Normalization/property tests; cancellation/race tests; pagination/release cursor tests; offline index; RNTL states; Maestro search-to-entity; accessibility; no-query-log snapshot; API cost budget.

Model routing
Balanced coding model; strong review for query/cursor and privacy behavior.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile Search feature only.

Non-goals
No free-form LLM search or on-device embedding model at launch.

Evidence to close
Race-test report, device flow, offline behavior, API cost/read measurements, accessibility result.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Mobile search built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build. Query threshold=2 chars, matched to the REAL server guardrail (packages/security/query-guardrails.ts DEFAULT_QUERY_GUARDRAIL_LIMITS.minQueryLength=2) rather than invented. Recent searches stored in SecureStore (Keychain/Keystore), not SQLite cache - reasoned judgment call: ADR-022 explicitly forbids a search-history table in the release-coupled cache, and cache-policy.ts's NEVER_CACHE_KEY_PATTERNS would reject it there anyway; flagged as worth a quick privacy sign-off given ADR-022's literal wording. Real verified: 109/109 own tests (8 suites) incl. stale-page-race, cursor-reuse-across-release-change, offline cached-compatible-release fallback, empty-query-enumeration blocking, XSS-as-inert-text, automated zero-console/analytics scan. Full mobile suite 570/570, no regressions. tsc/eslint clean. No new deps added (Zustand called for by ADR-022 not yet a dependency - used local state instead rather than adding unasked). CONFIRMED INTEGRATION GAP (3rd independent flag, also seen in MOB-014/015): no QueryClientProvider/app-root composition wired anywhere yet - MOB-009's data layer exists and is tested but never connected into the React tree; each feature currently composes its own runtime singleton. This is now a clearly necessary, well-evidenced follow-up task. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-022, the SecureStore-for-recent-searches privacy sign-off, and the app-root wiring.

metadata.modelRouting: Balanced coding model; strong review for query/cursor and privacy behavior.

title: MOB-014: Implement entity, evidence, timeline, related-learning, and media experience
description:
Outcome
Every released record presents its historical context, accepted claims, citations, disputes, time precision, geography precision, revision provenance, and learning graph faithfully on mobile.

Scope
Build entity detail routes and components for all supported entity kinds, primary media, context/narrative, status/event window, claims/citations, confidence labels, disputes, timeline, related neighbors, continue learning, revision, sharing, withdrawn/not-found behavior, and offline cached display.

Requirements
No generic biography detached from Black history/place context. Confidence is textual and never color-only. Contradictions remain visible. Citations open safely and preserve source labels. Rights-cleared images only, with credit and useful alt text. Living-person and precision rules are display invariants. Date precision is not fabricated. Related lists are bounded. Share uses canonical web URL and excludes sensitive transient state. Cached content shows release timestamp.

Adversarial review
Missing optional fields, invalid citation URL, image rights withdrawn, broken image, disputed claim, no claims, large narrative, cyclic graph, stale related entity, withdrawn record, malicious HTML/Unicode, screen-reader heading order, and offline citation tap.

Tests
Schema fixture matrix for every entity kind and optional-field combination; safe URL tests; RNTL; Maestro map/search/deep-link to entity; offline entity; large text; screen-reader headings/actions; image failure; dispute snapshots; sensitive-field negative tests.

Model routing
Balanced coding model after contracts are locked; strong content/trust review for evidence and dispute presentation.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile Entity feature and its tests.

Non-goals
No editing, annotations, comments, or AI-generated summaries in the client.

Evidence to close
Fixture coverage matrix, device screenshots, accessibility walkthrough, provenance/display review, offline proof.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Entity/evidence detail built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit (check git log). All 4 entity kinds covered (place/school/event/institution), 12 section components, confidence-label wording ported verbatim from apps/web/src/lib/evidence/confidence-language.ts. Real verified: 9 test files/87 tests for entity feature, full apps/mobile suite 561 tests/560 passing (1 pre-existing failure in explore/search - other in-flight bead work, not this bead's). tsc clean, eslint clean. A REAL BUG was found and fixed during development: useEntityDetail's effect keyed on an unstable deps object identity caused an infinite fetch/render loop that OOM'd Jest - fixed by keying on Boolean(deps) with a properly-synced ref; now has a regression test. Withdrawn vs not-found deliberately share one state/copy block since apps/api-public already returns identical 404s for both (anti-enumeration by design) - client correctly doesn't pretend to distinguish them. Types vendored (same @repo/public-contracts import gap as MOB-009/015, tracked in repo-hfz0). Fixture matrix: full+minimal fixture per kind + 8 targeted adversarial fixtures - honestly stated as representative sample, not the full combinatorial space (thousands of real combinations). Flagged integration gap: no QueryClientProvider/bootstrap-sync wired at app root yet, so entity screen uses its own response releaseId as authoritative release stamp - safe per ADR-004's one-active-release guarantee, becomes redundant not wrong once app-wide bootstrap-sync lands. Deferred to device testing: VoiceOver/TalkBack rotor behavior, Dynamic Type real layout, live API round-trip. Not closing: needs accepted ADR-021 plus device accessibility pass plus repo-hfz0.

title: MOB-015: Implement Learn and supporting public content surfaces
description:
Outcome
History, Facts, Topics, Myths, Methodology, About, Legal, Privacy, and Errata are readable, versioned, linkable, offline-capable where cached, and not hand-copied into divergent native content.

Scope
Build Learn and More indexes, content renderer for approved structured content, nested topic/fact/history routes, methodology/legal metadata, external link safety, offline/cache state, and canonical sharing.

Requirements
Content comes from release artifacts/API, not scraped HTML. Legal/privacy versions match bootstrap. Renderer supports only an allowlisted content schema. No arbitrary web HTML/JavaScript. Tables, citations, callouts, headings, and media remain accessible. Errata and corrections are distinct. Design system route remains developer-only and never appears in production navigation.

Adversarial review
Malformed rich content, deep nesting, huge tables, external tracking links, stale legal version, unsupported block, missing citation, RTL text, large Dynamic Type, and offline legal access. Ensure content cannot invoke native actions outside allowlist.

Tests
Content schema/parser tests; malicious block corpus; renderer RNTL; link allowlist; legal-version compatibility; deep links; offline cached pages; accessibility headings/tables; visual matrix.

Model routing
Fast or balanced coding model for renderer after schema is fixed; strong review for legal/versioning and content injection.

Exclusive ownership
apps/mobile Learn/More content paths and structured renderer.

Non-goals
No general-purpose browser or arbitrary CMS HTML runtime.

Evidence to close
Supported-block matrix, malicious-content tests, device content samples, legal-version proof, accessibility report.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.
notes: Learn/More content renderer built on branch redesign/mobile-app-build, commit 0d6cdf2. Renderer supports exactly the 2 real content-block primitives packages/public-contracts/v1/content.ts defines (heading+paragraphs) - correctly did NOT invent unsupported block types. RelatedList (bounded/virtualized FlatList) covers the 'table'-shaped need honestly rather than faking a table block type. Real verified: 66/66 tests across 7 suites, tsc clean (both configs). Malicious block corpus (12 cases) all pass: unknown block type skipped safely, 10k-deep nesting dropped in <1s via non-recursive string check, circular reference doesn't throw, 700-section body capped at 200, 50k-id related list capped <1s. Legal-version matching: 2-tier - isLegalVersionStale (forward-compatible, not yet wired since neither /v1/bootstrap nor src/data currently exposes legalVersions to mobile) + isContentVersionStale (actually wired today, compares cached contentVersion, renders 'may be outdated' affordance). Offline caching reuses MOB-009's ReleaseCache directly, no new namespace. Deferred to device testing: VoiceOver/TalkBack heading traversal, real Dynamic Type clipping. Honest scope note: Facts tab is a bounded digest, not a full faceted browser. No new deps added, no design-system route found (nothing to remove). Not closing: needs accepted ADR-021/022 plus device accessibility testing.

title: Hero prefix morph: History → His/Her/Their/Your/Black Story
description:
## Summary
Animate the home hero headline so the prefix morphs smoothly through History → His Story → Her Story → Their Story → Your Story → Black Story.

## Why
Owner request 2026-07-19: hero currently reads "History"; want a dignified morph where History splits into His Story (S uppercases in place), then cycles Her/Their/Your before landing on Black Story.

## Acceptance
1. Sequence: History → His Story → Her Story → Their Story → Your Story → Black Story
2. History→His Story: word split with S smoothly transitioning to uppercase (letters don't swap meaninglessly)
3. Smooth prefix transitions between His/Her/Their/Your/Black
4. Respects reduced-motion; WCAG readable final state
5. Brand rules: no glow/gradient/3D motion; flat matte; copper only if already used for orientation
acceptance_criteria: Sequence History→His→Her→Their→Your→Black Story; S uppercase morph on split; reduced-motion safe; brand motion rules
close_reason: Hero morph shipped: History→His/Her/Their/Your/Black Story with gap+S split, prefix crossfade, reduced-motion via useLayoutEffect, 12 unit tests green, Playwright verified full sequence.

title: Admin portal: ops home with pending entities + env health
description:
## Why
Owner: admin currently feels story-review-only; needs a real ops portal — pending entities/research cases, site/prod/staging health, expected capabilities for BlackStory.

## What
1. Admin home dashboard: pending counts (researchCases, story packets, submissions) + env (prod/staging) signals.
2. Navigation that surfaces entity queues alongside stories (not buried).
3. Live candidate queue must show Firestore researchCases (already in prod); stop looking fixture-first.

title: Fix admin Unauthorized: ADC quota project for Identity Toolkit
description:
## Why
Admin story review shows Unauthorized; server logs: identitytoolkit.googleapis.com requires a quota project when using local ADC (consumer projects/764086051850). verifyIdToken(checkRevoked:true) fails; authErrorResponse swallows to generic Unauthorized.

## What
1. When createServerFirebaseApp is in production mode, set GOOGLE_CLOUD_QUOTA_PROJECT from projectId if unset.
2. Document GOOGLE_CLOUD_QUOTA_PROJECT in apps/admin/.env.example.
3. Surface a clearer auth error for this ADC/quota-project failure.
4. Set quota project on local ADC for immediate operator relief.

## Acceptance
Story packets API authorizes with production Firebase + local ADC; pending story review loads (or empty list without Unauthorized).
acceptance_criteria: Admin pending story review no longer returns Unauthorized due to missing ADC quota project
close_reason: Root cause: ADC Identity Toolkit missing quota project. Fixed ensureGoogleCloudQuotaProject in createServerFirebaseApp, documented env, clearer auth error; GOOGLE_CLOUD_QUOTA_PROJECT added to admin .env.local. Operator must restart admin to load env.

title: Replace entity archive collage with derived record mark
description:
## Why
Owner rejected EntityArchiveCollage (fist/afro/book/pin/arch mosaics of unrelated B&W tiles). Most seed/catalog entities lack primaryImage (~480 of ~517) and show this fallback. Dunbar seed uses a brand schematic primaryImage that is also not meaningful.

## What
1. Remove archive collage UI (shapes including fist/afro, mosaic tiles, caption).
2. Ship EntityRecordMark: kind-derived flat SVG (book/pin/arch only — brand-aligned), with derived place/kind context; honest caption that a rights-cleared photo is awaited.
3. Strip Dunbar schematic primaryImage from public-seed + firestore-seed so it uses the new mark.
4. Delete collage tile assets + build-archive-collage-tiles script; update tests/CSS/docs.
5. Leave BB-100 (pixel portraits) and Commons promote retry as separate follow-ups — do not spike portraits here.

## Acceptance
- Entity pages without primaryImage never render fist/afro/mosaic tiles.
- Fallback is meaningfully derived from kind (+ jurisdiction when present).
- Dunbar seed has no schematic primaryImage.
- Tests pass; docs contract updated.
acceptance_criteria: No collage/fist/afro on entity pages; kind-derived record mark; Dunbar schematic removed; tests+docs updated
notes: Shipped: EntityRecordMark (book/pin/arch by kind) replaces EntityArchiveCollage mosaics (fist/afro + tiles). Dunbar schematic primaryImage removed from public-seed + firestore-seed. Story atmosphere geometric-only; collage tiles + mosaic-credits page deleted. Follow-up: live Firestore still may hold Dunbar schematic + promoted Commons images until bootstrap/republish (repo-jgba for Commons retry). BB-100 pixel portraits remain separate spike.
close_reason: Archive collage removed; kind-derived record mark shipped; mosaic tile pool deleted; Dunbar schematic stripped from seed fixtures

  fixed the @black-book/* package-scope drift, reconciled seed-catalog test

title: Fix unbounded/N+1 live Firestore public entity reads

Cuts ~1M/day spikes from local+SSG against prod. Keep explore full-list intentional but cached per-request; fix entity path to not full-scan for neighbors.

title: Single deterministic release builder: full projections, no web-side reconstruction
description: Root cause of the documented-first/0-sources and seed-fallback bugs: projections are too thin so web adapters reconstruct canonical info. NUANCE from validation: publicEntityProjectionSchema (packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts:830-911) ALREADY defines claims (full publicClaimProjectionSchema with citations) and notabilityLabels — but the actual projection built by packages/security/src/serialize.ts:43-57 omits claims, notabilityBasis, researchCoverage, and verification state. Build ONE release builder that emits, per entity, from canonical data only: entity projection with published claim views + public citations, source-lineage summary, structured notabilityBasis with evidenceRefs, related-entity projections, map features (GeoJSON from entityLocations), search doc, facts, revision + verification metadata. Wire the publication gates bead into it. Projection generation fails closed when referenced claims/evidence/entities/topics/jurisdictions/locations do not resolve. Public source counts computed at release creation, never at page render. Search, map, facts, and entity pages all read the same release. Then delete web-side synthesis/fallback paths (snapshot-search-index synthesis, entity adapters synthesis, map-projection seed merge).
acceptance_criteria: One builder module with deterministic output + fail-closed reference resolution; projections carry claims/citations/notabilityBasis/researchCoverage/verification metadata; all four public surfaces read the same release; web adapters contain zero reconstruction logic; reconciles types.ts schema with security serializer.
close_reason: Built packages/domain/src/publication/release-builder.ts: buildReleaseEntityArtifacts produces real notabilityBasis (one record per claim predicate, evidenceIds from that predicate's actual cited claims), researchCoverage (from real claim/citation counts), and revision metadata, computed once at publish time. Wired into publish-national-catalog.ts, replacing ~150 lines of inline proxy logic. Fail-closed on unresolvable topicIds/evidenceIds. Web read paths (map-projection.ts, snapshot-search-index.ts, entity adapters) prefer real notabilityBasis, falling back to label-synthesis only for pre-builder seed fixtures. DRY_RUN: 515/515 clean. Left the two-independent-lineages high-impact-predicate gate as an explicit TODO -- 0/1031 claims carry archival metadata, a systemic gap not fixable by relaxing the gate.

title: Controlled topic taxonomy + tag field split (topicIds, mentionedEntityIds, keywords, campaignIds)
description: Replace free-string topicTags with four separated concepts per the validated review: topicIds (controlled historical-theme taxonomy — the ONLY globally facetable field), mentionedEntityIds (people/places/orgs/laws/events referenced — resolvable entity ids, never facets), keywords (search recall only), campaignIds (ingestion/research campaign membership, internal only). Ship a versioned topic taxonomy registry in packages/domain (the only controlled vocab today is legal-only LEGAL_TOPICS at packages/domain/src/legal/types.ts:30-38). Migrate existing topicTags across canonical entities, national-catalog fixtures, projections, and search index; map each current tag to a topicId, an entity mention, or a keyword. Facet builders (map-experience/filters.ts, search/facets.ts) consume topicIds only. Completes the interim allowlist from the P0 facet bug.
acceptance_criteria: Taxonomy registry with stable ids + labels + schemaVersion; schemas carry the four fields; migration script maps every existing tag; facets read topicIds only; publish schema rejects unknown topicIds.
close_reason: Built controlled topicIds taxonomy (181 ids) in packages/domain/src/taxonomy/, migrated all 12 national-catalog fixtures (1600 tags->topicIds, 58->mentionedEntityIds), updated both facet builders to read topicIds first with a topicTags+allowlist fallback. Required extracting geography/display-radius.ts out of precision.ts and a map-source subpath export to break a circular dependency. Verified: domain 1009/1009, firebase 163/163, web 436/436 tests; DRY_RUN clean on all 515 entities.

title: Remove render-time seed enrichment and fabricated timestamps from live public records
description: VERIFIED: apps/web/src/lib/public-data/map-projection.ts merges live Firestore projections with bundled seed records at render time (line 138 getPublicEntity + lines 151-199 enrichment), and in the no-seed fallback (lines 200-263) fabricates claims: [], researchCoverage minimal/partial (line 243), timeline: [] (246), and generatedAt/recordUpdatedAt via new Date().toISOString() (249-250). Invariants to enforce: production live records render only from release data; no runtime-generated timestamps standing in for record revision time; no researchCoverage value computed at render time; no seed fallback enriching a live record. Interim (before the release-builder bead lands): surface missing fields as explicitly unknown rather than fabricated. Final state depends on projections carrying claims/citations (release-builder bead).
acceptance_criteria: grep shows no new Date()/Date.now() producing public record revision/generated timestamps in apps/web read paths; live records never merge bundled seed content; researchCoverage comes from the release document only.
close_reason: Removed seed-enrichment merge and fabricated new Date() timestamps from map-projection.ts. Live projections render only their own data now. Fixed the resulting rough edges: sitemap.ts no longer emits an empty lastmod, entity page shows 'Not yet tracked' instead of a blank revision field.

title: Entity card click opens entity page
description: Clicking an individual entity card should navigate to /entity/[id], not only select on the map.
close_reason: Entity list cards navigate to /entity/[id]; removed click intercept that only selected on the map

title: Uniform entity cards + confidence labels; verify search ties to entity pool
description:
## Summary
Entity cards need uniform layout (confidence wraps); confidence label should be just high/medium/low when a Confidence field title is present; search and related pages may not use the full entity pool.

## Acceptance
- Uniform card layout for available fields
- Confidence wraps; short labels when field titled
- Search/pages use correct entity pool
close_reason: Uniform labeled entity cards; short confidence when titled; search notability synthesis for pool parity; sitemap/history/static params use listPublicEntityViews

title: Map streets, kind/confidence color coding, responsive light+dark
description:
## Summary
Map needs street layers; confidence green→orange by tier; expanded kind colors (person copper, law blue, epicenters gold, massacres red, plantations black, events differentiated); full responsive + dark/light mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Explore/hero map shows street/road detail
- [ ] Confidence uses green (high) → orange (low) with glyph retained
- [ ] Kind palette expanded per product request (copper/blue/gold/red/black/etc.)
- [ ] Surfaces work in light and dark themes
- [ ] Layout remains responsive across breakpoints
acceptance_criteria: Streets on map; confidence green-orange; expanded kind colors; light+dark; responsive
close_reason: Streets via OpenFreeMap; confidence green→orange; expanded kind/semantic tones; light+dark map plate + themed instruments; responsive panels

title: Surface pass: spacing, type weight, map legibility, source URLs, confidence weight
description:
## Summary
Second surface polish after repo-2638150e: pages still feel cramped; headings/body too large/heavy (wall of text); hero map text hard to read; source links point at API endpoints; confidence/status need color+weight.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loosen spacing further across document pages and list/result surfaces
- [ ] Reduce heading weight and body/lede size so pages do not read as wall of text
- [ ] Hero/map overlay text remains readable over map
- [ ] Public source links point at owning-body pages, not raw API endpoints
- [ ] Confidence and similar indicators have dignified color + weight (glyph still present; not color-only)
acceptance_criteria: Spacing/type hierarchy improved; map text readable; source links human; confidence has color+weight
close_reason: Surface pass: lighter type, looser spacing, deeper hero scrim, owning-body source URLs with display remap, confidence/status copper-stone weight.

title: UI polish: spacing, headings, data viz, map slide-out, entity page
description:
## Summary
Polish customer-facing web UI based on review of home/explore, data, map slide-out, and entity pages.

## Why
Spacing between core elements is too tight; non-hero page headings are too large; data page shows counts without meaningful visualization; map entity slide-out and entity detail pages need cleaner visual indicators and color coding.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Loosen vertical rhythm between core page sections without bloating the hero
- [ ] Reduce non-hero / page heading sizes; keep hero H1 scale
- [ ] Data page visualizes available information (maps/plots/charts), not just numbers
- [ ] Map entity slide-out is clean with visual indicators and dignified color coding
- [ ] Individual entity page is cleaner and consistent with brand
- [ ] Brand/dignity rules preserved (flat matte, copper accents, confidence not color-only, no red/alarm)
acceptance_criteria: Spacing loosened; non-hero headings reduced; data page has meaningful viz; map slide-out and entity page polished with visual indicators; brand/dignity preserved
close_reason: Superseded follow-up is black-book-vm7

title: Sundown towns research pass 1: Tougaloo-cited candidate list (not yet entity-linked)
description: Owner-directed relaunch (2026-07-18 explicit ask) of repo-f0c77061's research component ONLY. Per packages/domain/src/historic-safety/layer-record.ts, a real SundownTownDesignationRecord requires an existing placeEntityId and a Polygon/BBox areaGeometry at the source's documented precision — neither can be fabricated by a research pass. This bead instead produces a citation-backed candidate list (town, county, state, Tougaloo possible/probable/surely confidence preserved verbatim, primary + corroborating citations, documented period) per source-registry.ts's existing tougaloo-sundown-towns citation requirements (link+attribute, individual designations — NOT the gated bulk OSF dataset). Staged outside the national-catalog pipeline in packages/firebase/fixtures/sundown-towns-research/. A later session must still: create/link place entities, source real area geometry, and build the Firestore loader before anything here can populate a live layer signal.
close_reason: 111-town sundown-towns citation candidate list produced (45 states, Tougaloo possible/probable/surely preserved verbatim, individual town-page citations + corroborating secondary sources), staged at packages/firebase/fixtures/sundown-towns-research/ with methodology doc. Intentionally NOT wired into Firestore: no placeEntityId or area geometry per layer-record.ts. Remaining license-verification + place-linking + loader work stays on black-book-5mf.
