title: Visit block: map hand-off, directions, and visitable status on every record
description:
Expansion bead from the 2026-07-18 blap redesign brief (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §5 'Visit hand-off' + §6 entity row).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-18 ('users may be using this to visit places they find here'; 'where possible should link to appropriate map tool').
Size: M
Dependencies: none (works on today's canvas; street basemap w72 improves context).

Why: the hand-off from record to real world is the product's payoff moment. A reader who found the Rosa Parks Museum must reach turn-by-turn navigation in one tap — and must never navigate to an empty lot expecting a building.

Deliver:
- Visit block on /entity/[id] (above the fold on mobile) and on explore narrative cards: 'Open in Maps' + 'Get directions' via geo: URI with Apple Maps and Google Maps web fallbacks (user-agent-appropriate ordering), built ONLY from the record's public-precision geoAnchor.
- Precision guard, fail-closed: redacted/coarse records hand off at locality/county centroid with honest labeling ('approximate area — precise site not published'); a unit test proves no sub-precision coordinate can reach a hand-off URL (extends the public-numeric/precision policy test pattern).
- Visitable status on the record: standing / rebuilt / demolished / marker-only — derived from the existing statusHistory vocabulary where present (currentEntityStatus), an explicit new status label where not; rendered as a mono-register chip in the visit block; catalog backfill for the 104-entity release (research pass reusing the 2026-07-18 provenance pattern).
- Copper discipline: the visit CTA is a primary action (copper); everything else in the block stays ink registers.

Never: exposing finer-than-public precision through ANY hand-off URL; implying a demolished site is visitable; affiliate/tracking parameters on map links.

Acceptance:
1. Rosa Parks Museum record: one tap reaches Apple/Google directions at the public coordinate, 375px both themes. 2. A locality-precision record hands off at centroid with the approximate label. 3. Precision-guard test fails the build if a redacted coordinate leaks. 4. All 104 release records carry a visitable status with a citation where the status is non-obvious. 5. a11y: links have distinguishing accessible names.

Model routing: fast model for the block + links; the status backfill research rides the established parallel-research pattern.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: Address and place search: one input for records, addresses, and places
description:
Expansion bead from the 2026-07-18 blap redesign brief (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §5 'Address + place search').

Source: owner brief 2026-07-18 ('users need to be able to search addresses').
Size: L
Dependencies: repo-b5a06269 (street basemap makes results legible); reuses repo-40192496's adapter surface (census-geo geocoder, packages/domain/src/adapters/census-geo — same client, user-facing this time).

Why: a visitor planning a trip starts from where THEY are ('what's near 125th and Lenox') — the archive must answer place-first questions, not only record-first ones. On mobile this becomes the primary entry into the map.

Deliver:
- One search input (explore panel + /search) with typed result groups: Records (existing search pipeline) and Places (census-geo geocode: addresses, cities, landmarks). Free-first: Census Bureau geocoder, no key; extend provider only if match quality demonstrably fails.
- Selecting a place result flies the camera via the existing presets (no new camera grammar) and shows nearby records within the flown viewport; no permanent pin is dropped.
- Debounced, cancellable requests; graceful zero-state ('No places matched — try a record search'); keyboard + screen-reader parity with the existing result list pattern.
- Geocode responses cached (same never-re-geocode-the-same-query rule as repo-40192496's cache).
- URL state: a place search that flew the camera is shareable via the existing viewport params — no new URL dimension.

Never: sending user queries to a paid geocoder by default; logging raw address queries beyond operational need (privacy posture); a second competing search UI.

Acceptance:
1. Typing '1616 Chappelle St, Tulsa' or 'Sweet Auburn Atlanta' flies to the place with nearby records visible, 375px and desktop, both themes. 2. Record search behavior unchanged and same-speed. 3. Zero-result and error states render the Notice pattern. 4. Repeat queries hit the cache (verified by request count). 5. A11y: results navigable by keyboard, announced count.

Model routing: fast model — well-specified integration against an existing adapter; escalate only if provider-quality judgment is needed.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: Black population by county by decade: census data model, ingestion, and map layer
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: pull population data at least by decade — Black population by county, decade over decade — and model the relationship between population flows (Great Migration) and entities/events. Think holistically; model data consistently across datasets.

REUSE: county join key = 5-digit FIPS already on /geo/us-counties-20m.geojson properties (stateFips+countyFips, landed 2026-07-18) and on MapCountyAggregate (packages/domain/src/map/map-source.ts); decade machinery = deriveEraBuckets (packages/domain/src/era.ts) + per-decade graph views (graph/decades.ts); presence-not-incidents dignity framing + tier philosophy = apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/density.ts (no false-absence tier) + DENSITY_TIER_FILL (dignity-style.ts); ingestion provenance = RetrievalEvent/SourceCapture (contentHash) + source registry + drift tracking (packages/domain/src/adapters/registry.ts, drift.ts); operator lane = operator-cli subcommand + thin skill (BB-085).

Data model: demographics county-decade docs {fips5, stateFips, countyFips, decade, totalPopulation, blackPopulation, source, retrievedAt, contentHash} — public census statistics (counts are fine; the non-numeric policy bans entity notability/relevance scores, not published government statistics — record that reading in the schema doc comment). One collection, keyed {fips5}_{decade}, idempotent re-ingest via contentHash.

Sources (free-first): NHGIS/IPUMS API for machine-readable historical decennial county tables 1790–2020 (needs free account + key — see companion HUMAN bead); modern slice available NOW keyless via api.census.gov decennial P.L. tables (2000/2010/2020 race by county) to prove the pipeline end-to-end before NHGIS lands.

Map/UI (follow the 6-step filter-extension recipe: url-state.ts → filters.ts applyExploreFilters → buildExploreFacetOptions → ExploreMapFeatureProperties → feature enrichment → parse/serialize): county choropleth mode on the existing density layer with a decade control tied to the era filter; joins to the county polygons already on the canvas (repo-b91695d2 county lines layer). Holistic tie-in: decade graph views + entity eraBuckets let the UI show entities/events active in the same decade as the population frame (Great Migration flows beside Bronzeville/Harlem/Greenwood records). Respect BB-051 dignity rules: population presence is the story, never deficit framing.
notes: Map layer half completed via repo-zfvj (layerMode + choropleth). Ingestion was already done. Close when operator regenerates full /geo/county-population-decades.json from Firestore.

title: Promote catalog related entries to canonical EntityRelationship records + release graph artifacts
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: get really good at mapping relationships between entities. The FULL pattern already exists — do not invent a parallel one: packages/domain/src/relationship.ts (EntityRelationship, 19 types with RELATIONSHIP_TYPE_SEMANTICS, evidenceIds REQUIRED >=1), graph/build.ts (deterministic release artifacts at publicReleases/{rel}/graph/adjacency/{entityId}, graph/decades/{decade}, graph/all-time), graph/adjacency.ts (evidence-ranked capped PublicRelatedEntry), graph/decades.ts (EraSpan bucketing via deriveEraBuckets).

Today's shortcut (2026-07-18): the national catalog carries inline 'related' entries (22 curated bidirectional links: located_at/occurred_at/related_to — museum↔arrest site, marches↔Pettus Bridge↔Brown Chapel, Harlem↔Apollo/Schomburg/Studio Museum/Abyssinian, Greenwood↔Vernon AME, Sweet Auburn↔Ebenezer/Big Bethel, etc.) written straight into the projection docs. That is the bootstrap path, not the pattern.

Deliver: (1) Convert those links into canonical EntityRelationship records, evidenceIds citing the entities' claim ids. (2) Run graph/build.ts over them to emit the release graph artifacts; entity pages + History edge lines read adjacency artifacts (the reader path source.ts hydrates related neighbors already). (3) A repeatable extraction step for future research waves: relationship candidates from co-located anchors (shared geohash prefix) + shared jurisdiction + claim-text mentions, reviewed via the existing triage skill lane, never auto-published without evidence links. Acceptance: /entity pages for Rosa Parks Museum and Edmund Pettus Bridge show related records from graph artifacts; decades graph views include the new edges.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.
close_reason: Catalog related promoted via extractCatalogRelationships; release-builder relatedEntries; publish-national-catalog writes entityRelationships + graph adjacency/decade/all-time. Offline test: museum+bridge related=1.

title: Location resolution pipeline: census-geo adapter → EntityLocation → projection geoAnchor
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: get really good at validating/approximating locations and associating lat/lng with entities WITHOUT relying on an LLM each time, consistently across the project. REUSE, do not reinvent: the Census Bureau geocoder adapter already exists at packages/domain/src/adapters/census-geo (free, no key; matchMethod vocab already includes geocode_census); canonical location records are entityLocationSchema (packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts — role, geometry, point, match {method, precision, confidence, recordedAt}, jurisdictionIds); geohash + prefixes via packages/domain/src/geography/geohash.ts / buildGeoPointFields; precision redaction via geography/precision.ts + apps/web geo-precision.ts.

Deliver: (1) operator-cli subcommand 'locate' (thin caller per BB-085, matching packages/operator-cli/src/bin.ts pattern) + .claude/skills/black-book/locate skill wrapper: input entity id + address/place text → census-geo geocode → deterministic validation (state-bbox containment vs jurisdictionLabel using US_STATES from map/us-geography.ts — same check the 2026-07-18 catalog QA script ran) → EntityLocation doc with full match provenance. (2) Geocode result cache keyed on normalized query text so the same address is NEVER re-geocoded (reuse the idempotency-record pattern from firestore/audit-outbox.ts). (3) publish-national-catalog.ts (packages/firebase/scripts) reads EntityLocation when present, catalog lat/lng only as manual_research fallback. (4) Retire apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/entity-geo.ts per its own documented retirement note — live projections now carry geoAnchor (landed 2026-07-18, commit d7c48f6). Acceptance: locating one new entity end-to-end writes a cached, validated, provenance-carrying EntityLocation and the map renders it with zero hand-edited coordinate tables.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.
close_reason: Shipped deterministic location pipeline: domain location-audit (street/named/area evidence + drift thresholds including ~1mi neighborhood + Blvd≠St safety), file-cached audit-entity-locations.ts, applied 30 Census street corrections to national-catalog, operator-cli locate + skill, publish prefers EntityLocation over catalog lat/lng. Follow-up repo-ftt1 for 185 named-place reviews. entity-geo.ts seed fallback remains until seed entities fully use projection location.

title: Replace fictional seed catalog with real, cited historical entities
description: Owner directive 2026-07-17: kill mock data, seed with real researched entities. Replaced the fictional 4-entity D.C. fixture (public-seed.ts, entity-graph-seed.ts, entity-geo.ts, facts-seed.ts, myths-seed.ts, firestore-seed.ts + bootstrap scripts) with a real, fully-cited cluster: Fifteenth Street Presbyterian Church (1841) -> Paul Laurence Dunbar High School (1870, first public HS for Black students in the US) -> its 1975 DC Inventory of Historic Sites listing -> the Dunbar Alumni Federation (2002). All facts verified via live WebSearch/WebFetch research this session with real citation URLs (Wikipedia, BlackPast, DC Preservation League, Howard University MSRC, National Trust for Historic Preservation, Boundary Stones, daf-dc.org). ~30 dependent tests updated. Fixed 2 readonly/mutable array typecheck errors in firestore-seed.ts invisible to the package's own typecheck (fixtures/ sits outside tsconfig include scope) discovered via live diagnostics and verified via direct tsc + full test reruns (firebase 149/149, web 396/396).
close_reason: Committed 9618e6e. See issue description for full scope.

title: BB-101: Redesign quality gate: a11y, motion, performance, and brand conformance
description:
Redesign quality gate for the blap experience (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md is the binding spec; brand-pack/ is the binding kit).

Source: owner briefs 2026-07-17 (redesign) + 2026-07-18 (rebrand, full dark/light support, fully responsive, brand-pack scheme adherence).
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-097, BB-098 (in progress); gates their output plus the 2026-07-18 rename/reskin work.

Why: one gate that proves the redesign is actually done — brand-conformant, accessible, both-theme, responsive, performant — instead of per-stream self-assessment.

Deliver — four matrices, evidence committed (screenshots + traces in docs/ui/qa/):
1. BRAND-PACK CONFORMANCE: palette-only colors (5 core + 2 neutrals via tokens, no raw hex in app code); typography = Sora SemiBold headlines / Inter UI / Source Serif 4 editorial / IBM Plex Mono data (Sora landed 2026-07-18 — verify every heading register actually renders Sora); type scale within kit p.2 (H1 56/64 → caption 12/16, fluid between breakpoints); mark usage rules p.3 (clear space, min sizes 120/48/32, no separation/stretch/shadow/recolor, page-band order); copper discipline 10-15% orientation-only.
2. THEME MATRIX: every route in §6's surface inventory rendered light AND dark; AA contrast on every text/control pair in both; theme-invariant surfaces (map plate, footer, on-map header) verified deliberate; no hydration flash.
3. RESPONSIVE MATRIX: every surface at 375/768/1280; no horizontal page scroll; wide content scrolls in-container; touch targets >=44px; map surfaces one-handed usable at 375px (street zooms included once w72 lands).
4. MOTION+PERF: prefers-reduced-motion collapses every animation including camera flights; route-arrival + hero-dissolve within token durations; map cold load + navigation traces recorded; existing a11y test suite green.

Never: signing off from memory — every cell needs an artifact; treating dark theme or 375px as best-effort (owner: P0 findings).

Acceptance: all four matrices complete with zero open P0 findings; failures filed as beads with screenshots; gate re-runnable (scripted screenshot harness preferred over hand capture).

Model routing: fast model + scripted capture for the sweep; strong model only for judgment calls on contrast/copper findings.
design: Mirrors BB-057 for the redesign tranche. Checklists to execute: guide p.13 launch checklist (verbatim), BB-051 dignity/redaction acceptance list, contrast suite across tokens v3, reduced-motion audit of BB-098 camera work, Lighthouse mobile preset on homepage + explore, cross-browser sweep Chrome/Safari/Firefox at 375/768/1280.
