title: BB-050 follow-up: parent barrel/config merges (blocked on shared-file ownership)
description:
BB-050 (U.S. address and current-location discovery) implementation is functionally complete and tested, but four changes were deliberately left out because they touch files outside this bead's exclusive-path allowlist (shared barrels / cross-cutting config another session owns):

1. `packages/domain/src/index.ts` — add: `export * from './geocode/index.js';` (new module: `packages/domain/src/geocode/**`, currently unreachable from `@black-book/domain`'s package barrel; `apps/web/src/lib/geocode/domain-mirror.ts` + `pipeline.ts` duplicate the small pure functions as a documented interim.)
2. `packages/domain/package.json` `test` script — append: `src/geocode/geocode.test.ts src/geocode/jurisdiction-ids.test.ts src/adapters/census-geo/geocoder-client.test.ts` (42 passing tests today, run manually but not wired into `npm test`).
3. `apps/web/package.json` `test` script — append the new BB-050 test files: `src/lib/geocode/browser-geolocation.test.ts src/lib/geocode/analytics-client.test.ts src/lib/geocode/locate-client.test.ts src/components/location/LocationPrivacyNotice.test.ts src/components/location/ManualPlaceSearchForm.test.ts src/components/location/LocationConsentButton.test.ts src/components/location/LocationResolutionPanel.test.ts src/app/locate/api/route.test.ts` (44 passing tests today, run manually but not wired into `npm test`).
4. `apps/web/src/lib/web-security/security-headers.ts` and `next-config-headers.mjs` — `PERMISSIONS_POLICY` currently sets `geolocation=()` (blocks all browser geolocation repo-wide). BB-050's `LocationConsentButton` calls `navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition` only after an explicit user click, but the browser will refuse the permission prompt entirely until this policy is relaxed to at least `geolocation=(self)`. Needs a scoped decision (self vs a route-specific override) from whoever owns web-security policy.

Once (1) lands, `apps/web/src/lib/geocode/domain-mirror.ts` should be deleted and its two call sites (`apps/web/src/app/locate/api/handler.ts` indirectly via `pipeline.ts`, and `pipeline.ts` itself) switched to importing from `@black-book/domain` directly — see `domain-mirror.ts`'s own module doc.
close_reason: Parent merged BB-050 follow-ups: domain geocode barrel export, package.json test wiring (domain+web), Permissions-Policy geolocation=(self), removed apps/web domain-mirror interim, rebuilt domain dist. Verified geocode/locate tests + web-security + ui suites green.

title: BB-094: Vetted-corpus bulk intake lane (settled record classes)
description:
Expansion bead BB-094 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (vetted-corpus bulk intake lane).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 (second message — bulk intake) (settled record classes — statutes, documented riots/massacres, known plantations, National Register sites — are effectively indisputable and bulk-loadable, "as close to the known location as possible")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-016, BB-037, BB-085, BB-090, BB-091

Why: per-record consensus review is the right cost for contested or judgment-dependent entries and the wrong cost for the National Register. The owner's intake-tiers insight: vet a corpus ONCE (licensing, provenance fields, authority tier), then import in bulk with per-record provenance and best-documented precision. Everything needed already exists as substrate: BB-085 landed the bulk-import CLI + quarantine pipeline with proposer-not-approver proven by test; BB-037 is the source registry; BB-091 defines the precision policy; BB-090 makes corpus-class membership (e.g., National Register listing) an auditable notabilityBasis. This bead adds the corpus-vetting gate and the streamlined promotion path on top — no new import framework, no parallel writer.

Deliver:
- Corpus vetting record model + storage, one per corpus ({corpus, custodian, licenseVerdict, authorityTier, provenanceFieldsRetained, precisionExpectation, refreshCadence, vettedBy, vettedAt}), registered through the BB-037 registry; bulk batches fail closed against unvetted corpora.
- Bulk import exclusively through the BB-085 CLI/quarantine pipeline: per-record provenance (corpusId, batchId, sourceRecordId), idempotent re-runs, budget caps, kill switch (BB-035 pattern).
- Streamlined promotion keeping every hard gate: citation completeness; geo at best-documented precision with precisionBasis=source-documented (BB-091 — bulk records are NOT default-coarsened); notabilityBasis auto-derived from corpus class (BB-090); mandatory per-batch human spot-check sample recorded via the BB-047 harness; ambiguous/contested records demote to the standard consensus lane, never silently publish.
- Launch corpora with named free sources and licensing verdicts (verify at execution): NRHP weekly dataset (NPS, public domain, coordinates included — also covers National Historic Landmarks and many documented plantation sites); HABS/HAER (Library of Congress, public domain); NYPL Green Book digitizations + Navigating-the-Green-Book extracted data (NYPL public-domain marks, per-derivative vetting); documented massacres/riots (EJI reports link+attribute per the citation requirements BB-082 already records; NPS/state-archive records public domain); HBCU list (NCES/ED, public domain); Rosenwald schools (Fisk database — terms verified before use, deferred if restrictive).
- Boundary rules: statutes/cases are NOT this lane (BB-087 owns the legal corpus); Tougaloo sundown data is NOT this lane (BB-082 owns it). Shared corpora register once in BB-037 and are referenced by both intake and the safety layers — never double-ingested.

Stress-test addition (2026-07-17): Mapping Inequality — HOLC Residential Security Map grades and boundaries (University of Richmond Digital Scholarship Lab, sourced from NARA, public domain) — is named as an additional launch corpus, feeding the BB-082 exclusion-infrastructure layer. This is the first launch corpus requiring real historic polygon ingestion (the actual graded neighborhood boundaries) rather than point+radius; batches for this corpus carry Polygon geometry compiled into BB-070 map tile assets per the BB-091 pattern, with grade + boundary + retrieval-date recorded per record.
design: Vet-once-import-bulk is a promotion-path optimization, not a gate removal: every fail-closed check (citations, precision, provenance) survives; only the per-record human consensus step is replaced by corpus-level vetting + per-batch sampling — the review budget moves to where contestability actually lives. Built on BB-085's landed pipeline because a second import framework is exactly the parallel-system the house rules ban. Notability auto-derivation works because corpus membership IS the criterion (a National Register listing is the notability basis) — recorded per record, still auditable. Rejected: auto-publish without sampling (violates fail-closed); a new ingestion service (BB-085 exists); ingesting EJI content wholesale (their citation requirements mean link+attribute, recorded in the vetting record); treating laws or sundown data as corpora here (owned by BB-087/BB-082 — one registration, many consumers).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Corpus vetting record model + storage: one record per corpus ({corpus, custodian, licenseVerdict, authorityTier, provenanceFieldsRetained, precisionExpectation, refreshCadence, vettedBy, vettedAt}) registered through the BB-037 source registry; bulk batches fail closed against an unvetted corpus. 2. Bulk imports execute exclusively through the BB-085 CLI/quarantine pipeline with per-record provenance (corpusId, batchId, sourceRecordId), idempotent re-runs, budget caps, and a kill switch — no parallel import path exists. 3. Streamlined promotion keeps every hard gate: citation completeness enforced; geo stored at best-documented precision with precisionBasis=source-documented per BB-091 (no default coarsening); notabilityBasis auto-derived from corpus class per BB-090; a mandatory per-batch human spot-check sample is recorded; records failing any check or flagged ambiguous demote to the standard consensus lane, never silently publish. 4. Launch corpora onboarded with recorded license verdicts: NRHP (NPS, public domain, coordinates), HABS/HAER (LoC, public domain), NYPL Green Book data (public-domain marks verified), documented massacres/riots (EJI link+attribute per recorded citation requirements; NPS/state records), HBCU list (NCES), Rosenwald schools (Fisk terms verified or deferred). 5. Boundary rules enforced in review: statutes/cases stay in BB-087, Tougaloo sundown data stays in BB-082; shared corpora are registered once and referenced, never double-ingested. 6. Every batch run produces a report (counts, precision tiers, spot-check results, rejects) in the operator audit trail. 7. Mapping Inequality (Univ. of Richmond/NARA, public domain HOLC redlining maps) is onboarded as a named launch corpus with a recorded license verdict, feeding the BB-082 exclusion-infrastructure layer; its batches carry real polygon geometry (not point+radius), compiled into BB-070 map tile assets per the BB-091 pattern.
close_reason: Vetted-corpus bulk intake lane landed: corpus-vetting record model with a fail-closed gate (vetting record + license verdict + BB-037 registry/kill-switch check), budget caps, and boundary-lane exclusions (statutes/cases stay BB-087's, Tougaloo sundown data stays BB-082's) in packages/domain/src/corpus-vetting.ts. Streamlined promotion (spot-check sampling, every hard gate preserved — citation completeness, source-documented precision, auto-derived notabilityBasis) in packages/domain/src/promotion/corpus-promotion.ts. All 7 launch corpora registered (NRHP, HABS/HAER, NYPL Green Book, documented massacres/riots, HBCU list, Rosenwald schools deferred pending terms, Mapping Inequality/HOLC with real polygon geometry) in packages/domain/src/launch-corpora.ts. CLI-facing integration (prepareCorpusBulkImportBatch in packages/operator-cli/src/bulk-import.ts) was completed by the parent session — the executing agent's process was interrupted mid-function, leaving only a doc-comment promise and unused imports; the actual batch-runner (fail-closed gate, budget check, idempotent duplicate detection, per-record routing through the same real BB-085 quarantine intake every other proposal uses, BB-018 audit/outbox event) was written and tested from scratch against the already-complete, already-tested domain layer. domain 607/607, operator-cli 57/57 including 8 new integration tests covering the gate, budget cap, idempotency, and both spot-check outcomes. CLI command wiring (a corpus-bulk-import subcommand) is a documented follow-up: no Firestore-backed SourceRegistryStore/CorpusVettingStore adapter exists yet anywhere in the repo (only in-memory test stores), and building one wasn't in this bead's scope.

title: BB-093: History graph and decade browse experience
description:
Expansion bead BB-093 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (history graph and decade browse experience).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 (second message — entity ontology) ("a solid graph/dependency map of entities flowing through history — an all-time view and decade-by-decade views")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-048, BB-051, BB-068, BB-092

Why: BB-092 makes the graph exist as data; nothing lets a reader walk history through it. This is the signature post-launch surface: an all-time view plus a decade stepper answering "what was true and active in this decade" — a place shows its 1930s condition, a law shows in_force, a person shows living — from BB-090 statusHistory and the BB-092 decade artifacts. Deliberately P1: the launch path is map/search/entity pages (BB-051/049/052); this surface consumes only published artifacts and can land the release after beta without blocking it. Reuses the BB-051 interaction grammar (narrative off-ramps, list-as-accessibility-peer, dignity rules, shared URL state) rather than inventing a second design language.

Deliver:
- /history surface: all-time graph view + decade stepper (coverage from the earliest published decade forward; sparse early decades render approved missing-information language, not emptiness).
- Decade view renders point-in-time truth from statusHistory (status-as-of-that-decade, never present-day status backfilled), including designation records with confidence wording (a sundown designation shows Tougaloo possible/probable/surely phrasing).
- Progressive-disclosure graph rendering with narrative off-ramps: every node opens the entity card/page; every edge exposes its citations within two interactions; no evidence-free connection renders.
- Synchronized list as the full keyboard/screen-reader peer (BB-051 parity rule); shareable URL state (decade + filters); degraded snapshot mode; BB-049 guardrails + App Check for any dynamic query.
- Dignity rules: no red violence styling, no crime-heat rendering; the decade view must show life alongside harm (presence/affirmation entities surface with equal weight — the BB-082 layer-4 principle applied to browsing).
design: Separate surface bead mirroring the BB-070(platform)/BB-051(experience) split — the graph data layer and the browse surface are different agents' exclusive paths (workers/projection vs apps/web routes). Consumes only published release artifacts: no request-time traversal, so the surface cannot create unbounded read costs. P1 not P0: the beta launch path is map/search/entity pages; this is the flagship follow-on (owner directive: promote to P0 with a BB-063 dep if the decade view should gate launch instead — a one-line change). Rejected: folding into BB-051 (already XL; /explore is spatial, /history is temporal — different interaction model); a force-directed physics graph as the primary view (illegible at this density, poor accessibility — progressive disclosure with a list peer instead); starting at 1619 with empty decades (missing-information language over fake sparseness).
acceptance_criteria: 1. A /history surface ships with an all-time view and a decade stepper; every decade view derives from BB-092 published decade artifacts (no request-time graph traversal). 2. The decade view renders point-in-time truth: entities show their status-as-of-that-decade from BB-090 statusHistory (a place shows its era designation with Tougaloo confidence wording where applicable), never present-day status. 3. Graph rendering uses progressive disclosure with narrative off-ramps: every node opens its entity card/page and every edge exposes citations within two interactions; no evidence-free connection renders. 4. A synchronized list is the full keyboard and screen-reader peer of the visualization; URL state (decade + filters) is shareable. 5. Dignity rules hold: no red violence styling, no crime-heat rendering; sparse early decades use approved missing-information language; presence/affirmation entities surface alongside harm. 6. Degraded snapshot mode works; dynamic queries reuse BB-049 guardrails with App Check enforced.
close_reason: Shipped /history all-time + decade browse over BB-092 graph release artifacts: SSR view-model, progressive-disclosure graph panel, synchronized a11y list, shareable URL state, entity/fact links, sparse-decade copy, /history/api App Check refine, 19 tests passing.

title: Reconcile BB-078 infra artifacts: 4 gaps found while writing the BB-079 cloud-apply checklist
description:
While consolidating BB-079's production cloud-apply checklist, the delivering agent found 4 genuine inconsistencies between BB-078's own Terraform/design artifacts (not fixed there, since BB-079's file ownership was the one new checklist document only). Full detail: docs/runbooks/production-cloud-apply-checklist.md, 'Gaps found during consolidation' section.

1. Service-account topology conflict (blocks BB-079 section 4): infra/gcp/terraform/locals.tf (BB-005's original single-project stub) still lists admin/publication/security/research in its service_accounts map, unchanged since BB-078, but infra/gcp/wif/deploy-roles.md says those four move to blackbook-internal under ADR-012. Applying both Terraform modules as literally written would create duplicate identities in both black-book-efaaf and blackbook-internal.

2. private-evidence bucket location undefined (blocks BB-079 section 4): infra/gcp/terraform/buckets.tf still creates it in blackbook-prod; no bucket resource or cross-project grant for it exists in the multi-project module; yet production-environment-resplit-migration.md's AC-ISO-3 verification checks a blackbook-internal-private-evidence bucket that nothing provisions.

3. Named Firestore databases (raw-ingest/curated) have no rules/indexes deploy target: infra/firebase/firebase.json only declares the default database; firebase deploy --only firestore:rules cannot reach either named database.

4. Named-database PITR is hardcoded POINT_IN_TIME_RECOVERY_DISABLED in infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/firestore.tf with no variable gate, unlike every other provisioning switch in that module.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Read infra/gcp/terraform/locals.tf's service_accounts map alongside infra/gcp/wif/deploy-roles.md's ADR-012 relocation statement -- the four identities (admin/publication/security/research) appear in both the original single-project stub and the ADR-012 target list with no edit removing them from the former.
2. Read infra/gcp/terraform/buckets.tf alongside infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/iam-cross-project.tf and infra/gcp/isolation-matrix.json's crossProjectGrants -- private-evidence has no cross-project grant or blackbook-internal bucket resource, yet production-environment-resplit-migration.md's AC-ISO-3 verification step checks gs://blackbook-internal-private-evidence.
3. Read infra/firebase/firebase.json -- only the default Firestore database has a rules/indexes deploy target; raw-ingest/curated have none.
4. Read infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/firestore.tf's google_firestore_database.internal resource -- point_in_time_recovery_enablement is hardcoded off with no variable gate, unlike every other provisioning switch in the module.
design:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Read infra/gcp/terraform/locals.tf's service_accounts map alongside infra/gcp/wif/deploy-roles.md's ADR-012 relocation statement — the four identities (admin/publication/security/research) appear in both the original single-project stub and the ADR-012 target list with no edit removing them from the former.
2. Read infra/gcp/terraform/buckets.tf alongside infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/iam-cross-project.tf and infra/gcp/isolation-matrix.json's crossProjectGrants — private-evidence has no cross-project grant or blackbook-internal bucket resource, yet production-environment-resplit-migration.md's AC-ISO-3 verification step checks gs://blackbook-internal-private-evidence.
3. Read infra/firebase/firebase.json — only the default Firestore database has a rules/indexes deploy target; raw-ingest/curated have none.
4. Read infra/gcp/terraform/multi-project/firestore.tf's google_firestore_database.internal resource — point_in_time_recovery_enablement is hardcoded off with no variable gate, unlike every other provisioning switch in the module.
acceptance_criteria: 1. infra/gcp/terraform/locals.tf's service_accounts map no longer lists the four ADR-012-relocated identities (admin/publication/security/research), OR the multi-project module's README claim that it doesn't duplicate them is corrected to match reality — pick one and make the artifacts agree. 2. private-evidence bucket has an explicit home: either a blackbook-internal-private-evidence resource + migration plan is added to the multi-project module and dropped from buckets.tf, or it's kept in blackbook-prod with the missing cross-project grant added and the migration runbook's verification step corrected to match. 3. infra/firebase/firebase.json (or a per-database config) gives raw-ingest/curated a real rules+indexes deploy target. 4. Named-database PITR gets a variable gate consistent with the rest of the multi-project module's provisioning switches. 5. terraform validate still passes for both modules after changes; the isolation-invariants test suite still passes.
close_reason: All 4 BB-078 infra gaps reconciled and validated. (1) ../locals.tf drops admin/publication/security/research; multi-project README documents the split. (2) private-evidence relocated to multi-project/buckets.tf (blackbook-internal); removed from ../buckets.tf; isolation-matrix.json updated. (3) firebase.json deploy targets for raw-ingest+curated with new rules/indexes files. (4) internal_firestore_pitr_enabled variable gates named-database PITR. Evidence: terraform validate OK (both modules), terraform fmt -check OK, isolation-invariants 7/7 pass.

title: BB-088: Editorial trust and pre-bunking surfaces
description:
Expansion bead BB-088 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (editorial trust & pre-bunking surfaces: protection framed as rigor, never defensiveness).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("People are going to hate it. People are going to try to discredit the information. People are going to try to mock it... I need you to protect it without saying that is what we are doing explicitly")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-053, BB-055, BB-069, BB-086

Why: the application will face coordinated attempts to discredit it. The winning posture is radical transparency experienced as rigor and reader empowerment — never a defensive crouch. The mechanisms exist (confidence + disputes BB-053, corrections BB-055, link integrity BB-083, fact records BB-086) but the PUBLIC-FACING trust layer that converts "someone found an error" into evidence of rigor, and that inoculates readers against bad-faith framing before they meet it, does not. Pre-bunking (inoculation) research is explicit that it works precisely because it is "educational, non-judgmental, and non-accusatory" — which is exactly the owner's ask to protect the work without ever announcing that's what we're doing. The whole bead is that principle: teach readers how records get attacked so they can verify for themselves; never argue with critics in copy.

Deliver:
- Methodology page ("How we work"): mission & scope; definitions & inclusion criteria (the load-bearing section — EJI's definitional precision is what defuses "your numbers don't match theirs"); source hierarchy (what counts as primary); verification + triangulation process; known limitations & gaps stated plainly (this section IS the pre-bunk); update cadence; how to report an error; funding/independence; masthead. Adopt all 8 Trust Project practices and emit the CC-BY-SA schema vocabulary they contributed, but never use their trademarked name or badge. Publish the five IFCN commitments verbatim as "aligned with" (the badge requires paid signatory status; the text is public).
- Corrections policy + public errata log: four-way change taxonomy (correction | clarification | update | editor's note — PolitiFact); never-silent, appended, timestamped notes on the item itself (NPR); a single reverse-chronological errata page (NPR/ProPublica pattern) ALSO exposed as an RSS/JSON feed; AP tone standard — errors fixed "fully, quickly, and ungrudgingly." Wire to BB-055 and the BB-086 revision log; emit schema.org/correction on corrected facts (an early-adopter trust signal; <1,000 domains use it).
- Per-fact provenance display + revision history (MediaWiki pattern): status + confidence with a tooltip linking to the methodology definitions; full sources list rendered from CSL-JSON with archived-copy link + access date + supporting excerpt per source (so a reader can replicate the work); "Our basis" prose note; counter-claims addressed inline. "Updated <date> — see what changed" -> per-revision diff view with mandatory edit summaries, corrections visually distinguished from routine updates. Git-backed content storage gives this nearly free and yields an independently verifiable audit trail a hostile reader can clone.
- Pre-bunking copy system (framed only as reader empowerment; voice owned by BB-069):
  - a site-wide "How to read this record" component (forewarn + technique micro-dose): "Historical records get challenged in predictable ways — quoting a source out of context, demanding a single 'official' document for events that were deliberately never documented, or attacking the messenger instead of the evidence. Here's how this record is built so you can check it yourself."
  - confidence labels that pre-empt the gotcha: never bare "disputed" — "Corroborated: three independent primary sources agree on the date; contemporary accounts differ on the crowd size, and we show both."
  - a per-fact "Common misreadings" section powered by BB-086 counterClaims[]: "You may see this described as X. That reading relies on [technique]; the primary record shows Y (source, page, archived copy)."
  - methodology copy that inoculates against the error-hunt: "An archive of thousands of sourced facts will contain errors. What matters is what happens next: every correction is logged publicly, timestamped, and preserved in the record's history — nothing is silently edited."
  - name techniques, never people or groups ("this framing," "this tactic" — never "critics"/"deniers"); the non-accusatory tone is what makes inoculation land across the whole spectrum.
- Schema.org trust markup: site-level NewsMediaOrganization with correctionsPolicy / verificationFactCheckingPolicy / ethicsPolicy / ownershipFundingInfo / masthead / actionableFeedbackPolicy; per-page publishingPrinciples.
- Myths/misconceptions surface: the ONE place ClaimReview markup is emitted (reviews of genuinely circulating third-party claims, per Google eligibility — attributed to a distinct external origin, one per page); optionally submitted to the free Fact Check Tools API for Explorer/research distribution, with ZERO SEO expectation (Google retired ClaimReview snippets June 2025 — treat as distribution, never infrastructure).

Design: inoculation = forewarning + preemptive refutation carrying a weakened micro-dose of the manipulation; technique-based prebunks scale better than claim-by-claim debunks; effects decay, so the trust chrome (status chip, source count, "updated — see what changed") appears on EVERY surface — map popup, place page, embed — as an ambient booster, not a buried about-page. Sources (all free): Cambridge/Jigsaw A Practical Guide to Prebunking Misinformation; Trust Project 8 indicators; IFCN five commitments; methodology exemplars EJI / Mapping Police Violence / ProPublica / Marshall Project; corrections exemplars NPR / ProPublica / AP.

Notes: This is the owner's "protect it without saying explicitly that's what we're doing" — the entire surface reads as rigor and empowerment. Directly answers the red-team's cherry-picked-screenshot / nitpick-one-error / discrediting-campaign class: the error-hunt is converted into the trust signal, and per-revision permalinks (BB-086) refute out-of-context screenshots with the live cited version. Mostly one-time content + small reusable UI components — write once, runs forever.

Ontology and disclaimer integration: the methodology definitions section publishes the BB-090 per-kind notability/inclusion rubric and status-lifecycle definitions; the BB-095 disclaimer framework is integrated into the trust surfaces rather than duplicated.
design: Inoculation = forewarning + preemptive refutation with a weakened micro-dose; technique-based prebunks scale better than claim-by-claim; works because it is educational, non-judgmental, non-accusatory (the owner's protect-without-saying-so). Effects decay, so trust chrome (status chip, source count, updated-see-what-changed) is an ambient booster on every surface incl. map popups and embeds. Free sources: Cambridge/Jigsaw Prebunking Guide, Trust Project 8 indicators, IFCN commitments, EJI/MPV/ProPublica/Marshall methodology exemplars, NPR/ProPublica/AP corrections exemplars.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Methodology page shipped (mission/scope, definitions & inclusion criteria, source hierarchy, verification+triangulation, known limitations, update cadence, report-an-error, funding/independence, masthead); 8 Trust Project practices adopted and their CC-BY-SA schema vocabulary emitted without using the trademarked name/badge; IFCN five commitments published as aligned-with. 2. Corrections policy + a single reverse-chronological errata page also exposed as RSS/JSON; four-way change taxonomy; never-silent timestamped notes on the item; schema.org/correction emitted on corrected facts. 3. Per-fact provenance display (status+confidence w/ definitions tooltip, sources list with archived-copy link + access date + excerpt, Our-basis note, counter-claims inline) + MediaWiki-style revision history with per-revision diffs and mandatory edit summaries. 4. Pre-bunking copy system live (site-wide How-to-read-this-record component, gotcha-pre-empting confidence labels, per-fact Common-misreadings from BB-086 counterClaims, error-hunt inoculation copy) - non-accusatory, names techniques never people. 5. Site-level NewsMediaOrganization trust markup + per-page publishingPrinciples. 6. Myths surface is the only ClaimReview emitter, optionally fed to the free Fact Check Tools API with zero SEO expectation. 7. The methodology definitions section publishes the per-kind BB-090 notability/inclusion rubric and status-lifecycle definitions; the BB-095 disclaimer framework is integrated into the trust surfaces (one framework, no ad-hoc banners).
notes: The owner's protect-it-without-saying-so, made concrete. Converts the error-hunt into the trust signal; per-revision permalinks (BB-086) refute out-of-context screenshots with the live cited version. Answers red-team cherry-picked-screenshot/nitpick-one-error/discrediting class. Mostly one-time content + small reusable components - write once, runs forever.
close_reason: Shipped methodology, errata+log feeds, trust components, NewsMediaOrganization JSON-LD, myths ClaimReview surface, BB-095 disclaimer integration. 9 web + 5 domain trust tests pass.

title: BB-085: Operator contribution lane: Claude sessions, CLI, and admin quick-add
description:
Expansion bead BB-085 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (operator contribution lane: Claude sessions, CLI, and admin quick-add).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("my periodic working on the project via claude sessions or something else to directly add information, or via the admin surfaces... make sure the search and agents are thorough with some really really really solid skills and packages")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-029, BB-044, BB-056

Why: the owner's working model is periodic deep sessions (Claude Code or admin console) between automated runs. Today there is no first-class path for an operator to add a source, lead, or claim without hand-crafting Firestore writes — which would bypass the pipeline. The operator lane makes direct contribution fast WITHOUT bypassing any gate: same quarantine, same research cases, same promotion rules, streamlined ergonomics.

Deliver:
- Operator ingestion CLI (repo script/package): submit a lead (URL/description/location/era), register a source, attach evidence to a case, or bulk-import from CSV/markdown notes — each lands in the standard quarantine/research-case pipeline stamped with operator identity and session ID; full audit; publishing still requires the promotion gates (proposer != approver holds — the operator proposes, publication approval is a distinct fresh-auth action).
- Admin console quick-add (BB-056 surface extension): the same operations as UI forms — paste a URL, the system fetches through BB-030 safety, captures via Wayback, pre-fills citation metadata, and opens a draft research case.
- Claude-session research workflow as project skills (.claude/skills/): well-crafted skills wrapping the real packages —
  - research-intake: take a URL/topic from the owner, run it through capture + citation scaffolding + draft-case creation via the CLI;
  - triage-graylist: walk parked weak-signal candidates, surface corroboration options, prepare accept/reject proposals;
  - discovery-run: launch a bounded adapter campaign and summarize yield;
  - case-drafting: assemble claims/evidence/confidence into a review-ready research case.
  Skills call the same TypeScript/Python packages the workers use (single implementation, no parallel logic in prompts); each skill documents exactly which invariants it cannot cross (no publish, no quarantine bypass).
- Session ergonomics: a docs/runbooks/operator-session.md runbook (start here, common tasks, end-of-session checklist including bd + git close protocol) so any future session — human or agent — is productive in minutes.
- Solid-packages rule (acceptance-level): pipeline capabilities land as tested package code (@black-book/* or the Python worker packages), and skills/automation are thin callers — never business logic living only in prompts or one-off scripts.
design: The owner works in periodic deep sessions (Claude Code or admin console) between automated runs. Making direct contribution fast WITHOUT bypassing gates keeps the single pipeline honest: same quarantine, same cases, same promotion rules, better ergonomics. Skills call the same code the workers run - single implementation.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Operator ingestion CLI: submit lead, register source, attach evidence, bulk-import CSV/markdown - all landing in the standard quarantine/research-case pipeline stamped with operator identity + session ID; a test proves publishing still requires the distinct promotion approval (proposer is not approver). 2. Admin console quick-add: paste a URL -> BB-030-safe fetch -> Wayback capture -> prefilled citation -> draft research case. 3. Project skills committed (.claude/skills/): research-intake, triage-graylist, discovery-run, case-drafting - each a thin caller over tested package code, each documenting the invariants it cannot cross. 4. docs/runbooks/operator-session.md committed (start-here, common tasks, end-of-session checklist incl. bd + git close protocol). 5. Solid-packages rule enforced in review: pipeline capabilities live in tested @black-book/* or Python worker packages; skills and automation are thin callers; no business logic that exists only in prompts or one-off scripts.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: packages/operator-cli/ — thin CLI (submit-lead, register-source, attach-evidence, bulk-import, research-intake, discovery-run commands) over real, tested package functions. Every intake path routes through the REAL createQuarantinedSubmission (@black-book/security, BB-029) and createResearchCase (@black-book/domain, BB-044) — no parallel logic. commit.ts is the only function that writes anything, via the real commitWithAudit (BB-018), idempotent on replay. fetch.ts is a real DNS-pinned, SSRF-safe transport implementing @black-book/security's SafeFetchDependencies contract (BB-030) — no bespoke fetch path. Wayback capture is explicitly NOT wired (nothing in this repo calls an archival API yet, confirmed by search) — documented as a capture-plan note, not faked.

promotion-boundary.test.ts is the required proof: evaluatePromotionGate refuses same-identity proposer/approver; a fresh-auth publication-role token is required for any promote action; an export-surface scan confirms the package has literally no promote/approve/publish/activate/retract function. 38/38 operator-cli tests pass, typecheck clean.

Admin quick-add (apps/admin/src/app/quick-add/): a live route calling the same runResearchIntake/createNodeSafeFetchDependencies functions (no reimplementation) via a Next.js server action. VERIFIED LIVE by the parent session in a running dev server: submitted a real URL (rfc-editor.org), got a real BB-030-safe fetch, real citation prefill, real quarantine submission + draft research case with real UUIDs, 'nothing written yet' correctly shown pending commit. A real  for apps/admin succeeds (18 routes, /quick-add prerenders at 107kB).

Four project skills committed at .claude/skills/black-book/{research-intake,triage-graylist,discovery-run,case-drafting}/SKILL.md — each names the exact CLI command/package function to call and carries an explicit Do/Never invariants list (no publish, no quarantine bypass).

docs/runbooks/operator-session.md: start-here, common tasks, end-of-session checklist referencing AGENTS.md's exact bd+git close protocol.

IMPORTANT FIX by the parent session: .gitignore blanket-ignored .claude/ (ADR-0027, Construct-generated/machine-specific), which would have silently prevented these four skill files from EVER being committed — directly conflicting with this bead's explicit 'skills committed' acceptance criterion. Fixed with a narrow, tested carve-out (.claude/skills/black-book/ un-ignored one directory level at a time; verified via git check-ignore that the carve-out works AND that other Construct-generated .claude/ content, e.g. the 100+ generic skill catalog, remains correctly ignored).

Investigated and resolved the agent's flagged 'cross-package enum drift' concern: NOT a real drift — packages/firebase/src/firestore/types.ts imports AUDIT_EVENT_ACTIONS directly from @black-book/domain (same array reference) for its z.enum(), so BB-077's 'deletion.purged' action flows through automatically with zero schema changes needed.

Solid-packages rule self-check confirmed: all business logic lives in tested packages/operator-cli/src/*.ts functions; cli.ts only parses flags; the admin server action only reads form fields and calls the package function; all four skills are prose pointing at real commands with zero business logic in any prompt.

Documented gaps: (1) no read/list CLI commands yet — triage-graylist/case-drafting skills honestly route to the admin console or direct Firestore access for reading; BB-085 only built the write/propose side. (2) Wayback capture integration point documented, not wired (no archival-API caller exists anywhere in this repo yet).
close_reason: Operator ingestion CLI + admin quick-add both landing in the real BB-029/044/018/030 pipeline (proposer-not-approver proven by test and an export-surface scan), four committed project skills, and an operator-session runbook. Verified live end-to-end (real fetch, real quarantine submission, real draft case). Fixed a real .gitignore conflict that would have silently dropped the required skill files.

title: BB-084: Self-updating operations: scheduled automation backbone
description:
Expansion bead BB-084 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (self-updating operations: the scheduled automation backbone).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("I need a lot of this to be self updating and will rely a lot on the automated processes that run")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-018, BB-035, BB-037

Why: the product must stay alive between owner sessions. Many beads now assume recurring jobs (deletion-sync, link checks, discovery campaigns, recalibration, dataset refreshes) but no bead owns the scheduling backbone. ADR-007 already picked the mechanism (Cloud Scheduler -> Cloud Tasks -> Cloud Run jobs/workers); this bead builds the framework those jobs plug into.

Deliver:
- Scheduled-job registry (versioned config, BB-037 registry discipline): every recurring job declares owner, cadence, budget, timeout, idempotency key scheme, kill switch, and target worker. No ad-hoc crons.
- The initial job roster:
  - discovery campaigns per adapter on per-source cadences (Wikimedia/federal weekly-monthly; RSS hourly-daily; Internet Archive/DPLA weekly; web-search campaigns budget-gated) (BB-039/073/075)
  - Reddit deletion-sync (contractual 48h window — BB-074/077)
  - citation link-health sweeps (BB-083)
  - external dataset refresh checks: FBI hate-crime annual, Tougaloo/Mapping Inequality revisions (BB-082)
  - relevance/confidence recalibration report (BB-081)
  - source drift + adapter run-health checks (BB-037), gold-corpus regression on engine changes (BB-047)
  - backup verification + restore-drill scheduling (BB-020/061), cost/budget report (BB-033)
- Run-health and missed-run alerting: every job emits run records (the research worker's run-health pattern generalized); a job that misses N consecutive runs or exceeds budget raises an operator alert (BB-034) — silence is a failure mode, not a default.
- Invariants hold under automation: all jobs run in the internal environment with least-privilege SAs; nothing scheduled can publish (assertDiscoveryCannotPublish and promotion gates stay binding) — automation PROPOSES (candidates, reports, flags); the owner or reviewers DISPOSE via the admin console or operator lane (BB-085). The only fully automatic public-facing effects are mechanical and reversible: link repairs to archived copies (BB-083) and release-coupled rebuilds (BB-070 map source, search index) — each with its own kill switch.
- Every automated write goes through the BB-018 audit/outbox path with a job-run correlation ID, so any automated change is traceable to the exact run that made it.
design: ADR-007 already picked the mechanism (Cloud Scheduler -> Cloud Tasks -> Cloud Run jobs); this bead builds the framework jobs plug into. Generalizes the research worker run-health pattern. Automation proposes; the owner/reviewers dispose via admin console or the BB-085 operator lane.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Scheduled-job registry (versioned config): every recurring job declares owner, cadence, budget, timeout, idempotency scheme, kill switch, target worker; no unregistered recurring jobs. 2. Initial roster registered: per-adapter discovery campaigns, Reddit deletion-sync, citation link-health sweeps, external dataset refresh checks (FBI annual, Tougaloo/Mapping Inequality revisions), recalibration report, source drift + run-health checks, gold-corpus regression, backup verification + drill scheduling, cost/budget report. 3. Run records + missed-run and over-budget operator alerts, tested (silence is a failure mode). 4. Publish invariants proven under automation: assertDiscoveryCannotPublish and promotion gates hold for every scheduled job; the only fully-automatic public-facing effects are mechanical and reversible (link repairs, release-coupled rebuilds), each with a kill switch. 5. Every automated write carries a job-run correlation ID through the BB-018 audit/outbox path.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: scheduling framework (packages/config/src/scheduled-jobs/) implementing ADR-007's Cloud Scheduler -> Cloud Tasks -> Cloud Run Jobs mechanism as a versioned, fail-closed registry (mirrors BB-037/BB-038's registry pattern exactly) — every job declares owner, cadence, budget, timeout, idempotency scheme, kill-switch reference, and target worker; unregistered job ids are rejected.

15-entry roster (roster.ts): 4 REAL job bodies wired to already-shipped, tested code — source-drift-run-health-check (-> @black-book/domain evaluateRunHealth + new workers/research/.../scheduled_jobs.py), gold-corpus-regression (-> @black-book/testing evaluateCorpus, BB-047), backup-verification-daily and restore-drill-quarterly (-> real subprocess calls into scripts/backup-restore/, BB-020) — plus 11 documented stubs (each carrying implementationOwnerBead) for capabilities owned by not-yet-built beads: 4 discovery-campaign cadence classes (BB-073/075), reddit-deletion-sync (BB-074/077), citation-link-health-sweep (BB-083), 2 external-dataset-refresh checks (BB-082), relevance-confidence-recalibration-report (BB-081), cost-budget-report (BB-033's evaluator exists in packages/security/src/resource-controls.ts but wasn't cheap to wire without a new dependency — flagged, not silently skipped), release-coupled-rebuild (BB-070).

Run-health/alerting: generalizes the research worker's run_health.py shape; missed-consecutive-runs and over-budget both raise alerts through BB-034's real SEC-SRC-01/SEC-COST-01 alert policies (buildAlertPayload) — not a new channel. Publish invariant: publish-guard.ts calls straight through to the real assertDiscoveryCannotPublish (BB-039); tested that the two pre-approved public-effect exceptions (link-repair, release-rebuild) are not a bypass route for anything else. Audit correlation: job writes are shaped as real DomainAuditEvent/DomainOutboxMessage objects (commitWithAudit-compatible, verified via a devDependency-only import test) with jobRunId as the correlation id throughout.

infra/gcp/scheduler/scheduled-jobs.json is the declarative Cloud Scheduler mirror (status: 'design', not applied) with its own JSON Schema + an 8-test validation suite.

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session after clearing an unrelated stale packages/domain/dist/ artifact that was blocking a clean tsc build for every domain-touching agent in this wave, not caused by BB-084): pnpm --filter @black-book/config test 72/72 pass, typecheck clean. uv run pytest workers/research 60/60 pass (57 pre-existing + 3 new). node --test infra/gcp/scheduler/scheduled-jobs.test.mjs 8/8 pass.

Disclosed design compromises: (1) backup-verification-daily/restore-drill-quarterly are tagged targetWorker.package='security' even though their scripts live in scripts/backup-restore/, outside all three ADR-007 worker packages — a real, minor ADR-007 conformance gap, flagged for a follow-up bead to migrate that logic into workers/security. (2) Discovery-campaign granularity is 4 representative cadence-class entries, not one row per literal adapter id — a cheap, purely mechanical fan-out once BB-073/075 land.
close_reason: Scheduled-job registry (fail-closed, ADR-007-conformant), run-health/alerting reusing BB-034's real alert policies, publish-invariant enforcement wired to the real BB-039 guard, and audit-correlated job writes delivered. 4/15 roster entries wired to real shipped code; 11 documented stubs for not-yet-built beads. 72+60+8 tests passing.

title: BB-083: Citation integrity and link-rot management
description:
Expansion bead BB-083 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (citation integrity and link-rot management).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("everything possible is cited and linked appropriately, and there is a pattern for updating dead links and at a minimum, marking the link as such but providing a term to help the user search")
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-016, BB-019, BB-030

Why: an archive of record lives and dies by its citations, and the web rots — a significant share of cited URLs die within years. The provenance model (BB-016) stores captures; nothing yet detects rot, repairs links, or degrades gracefully for the reader.

Deliver:
- Citation completeness gate: every published claim carries at least one citation with URL (or offline-source designation), source name, capture pointer, and retrieval date — enforced fail-closed at projection build (BB-019), not left to reviewer diligence. Offline/analog sources (books, physical archives, oral interviews) get structured citations without URLs; "unsourced" is not a publishable state.
- Link-health checker (scheduled via BB-084): periodic re-verification of every cited URL through the BB-030 URL-safety fetch policy; classify alive / redirected / changed (content drift vs capture) / dead (404/410/NXDOMAIN/paywalled). Respect robots/rate norms; budget-capped; state machine with retry before declaring death.
- Automatic repair ladder, in order: (1) follow and record permanent redirects; (2) swap to the stored Wayback capture as the primary public link, original kept as "originally published at"; (3) if no capture exists, attempt Save Page Now retroactively; (4) only then mark dead.
- Reader-facing degraded citation (the owner's "Try searching for" pattern): a dead-link citation renders as clearly marked ("Original link unavailable — archived copy" or "link dead as of <date>") plus a generated search suggestion built deterministically from stored citation metadata (source title + author + key named entities from the capture) — e.g. Try searching for: "Rosewood massacre grand jury report 1923 Florida". No LLM required; the suggestion derives from fields we already store.
- Content-drift flag: if a live page's current content no longer matches the capture (hash/fuzzy-similarity check), flag for research review — the citation may now cite something different from what the claim relied on; the capture remains the evidentiary anchor.
- Citation health telemetry: rot rate per source class feeds the source registry's authority signals (a source family whose links rot fast is a durability signal for BB-043), and an operator dashboard tile in the admin console (BB-056 surface).
design: Link rot is the default fate of citations; an archive of record degrades gracefully or loses credibility. The Wayback capture (BB-073/077) is the evidentiary anchor; the live link is a courtesy. The Try-searching-for pattern gives readers a path even when both fail - derived from fields we already store.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Publication-time citation completeness gate, fail-closed at projection build: every published claim has at least one citation with URL or structured offline-source designation, source name, capture pointer, retrieval date. 2. Scheduled link-health sweep (via BB-084) classifying alive/redirected/drifted/dead, fetching through BB-030 policy, budget-capped, with retry-before-death. 3. Repair ladder implemented and tested: permanent-redirect update -> archived-copy swap (original kept as originally-published-at) -> retroactive Save Page Now -> dead-mark. 4. Dead-citation UI: clearly marked with date, archived copy when available, and a deterministic Try-searching-for suggestion generated from stored citation metadata (title + author + key named entities) - no LLM dependency. 5. Content-drift detection (capture vs live hash/similarity) flags research review. 6. Rot-rate telemetry per source class feeds source-registry authority signals and an admin dashboard tile.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: packages/domain/src/citations/ — citation.ts (Citation type + buildCitationFromEvidence adapting BB-016's provenance model), completeness-gate.ts (fail-closed assertProjectionCitationCompletenessGate; tested that an unsourced claim cannot pass a guarded buildReleaseManifest call), link-health.ts (classifyLinkCheckAttempt alive/redirected/drifted/dead + advanceLinkHealthState retry-before-dead state machine, dependency-injected port, never fetches directly), drift-detection.ts (hash-exact-match + Jaccard trigram similarity, fails closed toward flag-for-review when unverifiable), spn-client.ts (minimal Save Page Now caller — BB-073 hadn't built one when checked concurrently; documented as a near-duplicate worth merging later), repair-ladder.ts (4-step ladder permanent_redirect -> wayback_swap -> retroactive_spn -> dead_mark, pure decision + async orchestrator, order explicitly tested), try-searching-for.ts (buildTrySearchingForSuggestion, deterministic/synchronous, no-LLM property explicitly tested), rot-telemetry.ts (computeRotRateBySourceClass).

Real scheduled job (packages/config/src/scheduled-jobs/jobs/citation-link-health-sweep.ts): real BB-030 wiring via a Node DNS/transport mirroring packages/operator-cli/src/fetch.ts (domain can't depend on security). Auto-commits ONLY the declared link-repair-archived-copy effect (wayback_swap/dead_mark) — permanent-redirect and retroactive-SPN repairs are computed but returned as proposals, never auto-applied, a deliberate documented scope decision matching the pre-approved-automatic-effects boundary BB-084 established. roster.ts flipped to rosterStatus:'real'.

UI: Citation.tsx gained optional linkStatus/deadAsOfDate/archivedHref/trySearchingFor props — additive, all existing call sites across apps/web verified unaffected via typecheck.

Admin: new route apps/admin/src/app/citation-health/ — rot-rate-by-source-class table + degraded-citation preview, fixture data.

Confidence engine: additive citationRotRateAuthoritySignal in engine.ts, coexisting cleanly with 3 other concurrent agents' additive functions in the same file this session (BB-073/076/089), auto-exported via the existing export *.

Gate status: documented, not live-wired. The claim->projection assembly calling buildReleaseManifest lives in Python workers (workers/publication/, per ADR-007) outside this TS package; the one TS call site (research-case/workflow.ts) wasn't in this bead's file ownership. completeness-gate.ts documents the exact integration point and is fully tested standalone.

Note on shared-tree commit history: this agent's concurrent edits to packages/domain/src/index.ts, packages/config/package.json, roster.ts, roster.test.ts, and scheduled-jobs/index.ts were already swept into the earlier 'Land BB-081' commit (6a03a44), since that commit staged the same shared files while BB-083's edits were already present in the working tree. Content verified correct/intact in HEAD via this bead's own test run — nothing lost, just blended attribution, noted here for an accurate paper trail (same pattern as BB-070's map barrel export landing under 'Land BB-077' earlier this session).

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session after all 5 concurrent agents in this wave landed): pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test 392/392 pass, typecheck clean; @black-book/config test 85/85 pass, typecheck clean; @black-book/ui test 26/26 pass, typecheck clean; @black-book/admin typecheck clean; apps/web typecheck clean. pnpm validate passes clean end to end.
close_reason: Citation completeness gate, link-health checker with retry-before-dead state machine, the full 4-step repair ladder (order-tested), a deterministic no-LLM 'Try searching for' suggestion builder, content-drift detection, and rot-rate telemetry feeding both the confidence engine and a new admin dashboard tile. Real scheduled job wired into BB-084's roster, auto-committing only the pre-approved mechanical/reversible repair effect. Citation.tsx extended additively with zero regressions to existing call sites.

title: BB-082: Historic safety and place-context engine
description:
Expansion bead BB-082 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (historic safety and place-context engine).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("think through the scoring for things like the historic safety... consider proximity of historic events, consider crime reporting, consider whatever other data may be relevant and accessible, and how that should be updated")
Plan: plan.md
Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-014, BB-017, BB-040, BB-043, BB-050

Why: the gold corpus already names sundown-town candidates as a required category, and the Green Book lineage makes place-safety context core to the mission. But a naive "safety score" is the single most dangerous feature this product could ship — blending historic racial violence with modern crime statistics into one number reproduces redlining. The design below is layered, cited, and deterministic, matching the confidence-engine discipline.

Deliver:
- LAYERED SIGNALS, NEVER ONE OPAQUE NUMBER. Each layer is separately computed, separately cited, separately dated, and shown with its own methodology note:
  1. Documented historic events layer: proximity/density of events already in our corpus plus vetted external datasets — lynching records (EJI / Seguin-Rigby dataset), massacres and race riots, documented displacement (urban renewal, highway construction). Time-banded by era, never decayed to zero — history does not expire.
  2. Sundown-town layer: Tougaloo College Historical Database of Sundown Towns taxonomy preserved verbatim (possible / probable / surely) — their statuses are claims with confidence, exactly our claims model; never flattened to a boolean.
  3. Exclusion-infrastructure layer: HOLC redlining grades (Mapping Inequality, University of Richmond), racially restrictive covenant projects where digitized — structural context, clearly historical.
  4. Presence/affirmation layer (the counterweight — this engine must show life, not only harm): Green Book sites, historic Black churches/schools/business districts, HBCUs, heritage markers from our own corpus.
  5. Modern context layer (handle with extreme care): FBI hate-crime statistics (annual, on-mission) as the primary modern signal. General crime reporting (FBI CDE/NIBRS) only as clearly labeled context with an explicit bias caveat — reported crime measures policing patterns, not safety for Black people; it never feeds any composite. NAACP-style travel advisories recorded as sourced claims when they exist.
- Composite "place context" summary ONLY from layers 1-4, with published weights, versioned methodology (BB-043 pattern: versioned engine, input fingerprints, audited recalculation), and the full layer breakdown one interaction away. Constitution language rules apply: procedural, evidence-capped phrasing; no place is branded "unsafe" — the site presents documented history and lets it speak.
- Every datum cited: source, retrieval date, capture pointer (BB-083 integration); every layer's dataset registered through the BB-037 source registry with rights/attribution metadata (EJI and Tougaloo have specific citation requirements — record them).
- Update cadence per source, wired into BB-084 automation: FBI data annually on release; Tougaloo/Mapping Inequality checked for dataset revisions; our own corpus layers recompute on release activation. Each layer displays "as of" dates.
- Dispute path: place-context layers are claims — the BB-053/BB-055 dispute and correction machinery applies to them like any other claim.
- Map integration (with BB-051/BB-070): layers renderable as opt-in map overlays, following the dignity rules — muted rendering, no red danger shading, narrative off-ramps to the underlying evidence.

Ontology integration (corrected 2026-07-17 stress test — see below): sundown-town, redlining-grade, and exclusion-infrastructure designations are stored as BB-082's OWN time-scoped, evidence-backed layer records on place entities — following the same time-scoped-designation pattern BB-090 statusHistory uses, but a distinct field/collection, never merged into the generic entity statusHistory array (Tougaloo possible/probable/surely stays claim confidence); area conditions bind to BB-091 jurisdiction/place geometry (an area over a time range, never a point marker), rendered only at the precision level actually documented (a county-level source renders as a county polygon, never interpolated down to imply town-level specificity); the modern-context layer (layer 5) surfaces exclusively through the BB-095 advisory presentation.

Stress-test clarification (2026-07-17): the exclusion-infrastructure layer (layer 3) explicitly includes HOLC redlining grades as its own designation record type (A/B/C/D grade, distinct vocabulary from the sundown possible/probable/surely taxonomy) — named explicitly here so it is not conflated with or dropped in favor of the sundown-town layer during implementation; both are BB-082 layer records, not BB-090 statusHistory.
design:
Datasets: EJI / Seguin-Rigby lynching records; Tougaloo College Historical Database of Sundown Towns; Mapping Inequality HOLC redlining (Univ. of Richmond); digitized covenant projects; Green Book sites + our own corpus for the presence layer; FBI hate-crime statistics (annual) as the primary modern signal; FBI CDE/NIBRS general crime ONLY as labeled context - reported crime measures policing patterns, not safety for Black people, and blending it into a composite reproduces redlining. Language: constitution caps apply; the site presents documented history, it does not brand places unsafe.

Stress-test correction (2026-07-17): sundown-town/redlining/exclusion designations are BB-082's own layer records, not BB-090 statusHistory — the original amendment language conflated an entity's lifecycle status (BB-090's scope) with a place's historical condition record (BB-082's scope); corrected to keep these fields separate so statusHistory doesn't become a catch-all. Redlining grades and sundown-town confidence use different vocabularies (A-D vs. possible/probable/surely) and must not be forced into one shape.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Five layers implemented, each separately computed, cited, dated, with its own methodology note: documented-events, sundown-town, exclusion-infrastructure, presence/affirmation, modern-context. 2. Composite place-context summary derives ONLY from layers 1-4 with published weights; a test proves general crime statistics can never enter the composite; the hate-crime layer renders with an explicit bias caveat. 3. Tougaloo taxonomy preserved verbatim (possible/probable/surely - statuses are claims with confidence, never booleans); EJI and Tougaloo citation requirements recorded in the source registry. 4. Versioned methodology engine with input fingerprints and audited recalculation (BB-043 pattern). 5. Per-source update cadences registered in BB-084; as-of dates displayed on every layer. 6. Dispute/correction path works on layer claims like any claim. 7. Map overlays are opt-in and follow the BB-051 dignity rules (muted rendering, no danger shading, narrative off-ramps to evidence). 8. Sundown-town and redlining-grade designations are stored as BB-082's own time-scoped, evidence-backed layer records on place entities (not BB-090 statusHistory), preserving possible/probable/surely as claim confidence for sundown and the A-D grade vocabulary for redlining; a point-in-time query answers a place's designation for any decade for either layer. 9. Area conditions bind to BB-091 jurisdiction/place geometry — a sundown town renders as an area over a time range, never a point marker. 10. The modern-context layer surfaces only through the BB-095 advisory presentation; the crime-stats test extends to prove advisory data never enters any scoring input. 11. Area-condition rendering never infers a finer geographic precision than its source documentation supports (a county-level-only source renders as a county polygon; it is never interpolated down to shade or point-mark individual towns within it as if independently documented). 12. The exclusion-infrastructure layer explicitly names HOLC/redlining grades as a distinct designation record type from the sundown-town layer; the Mapping Inequality (University of Richmond + NARA, public domain) historic polygon dataset is registered through BB-037 and consumed by BB-094 as a named launch corpus for this layer.
notes: Owner brief 2026-07-17: thoughtful scoring for historic safety - proximity of historic events, crime reporting, other relevant accessible data, and how it updates. The layered design is the guard against building a redlining machine.
close_reason: Historic-safety module complete: 83 tests across 12 files pass, domain typecheck clean. Added composite.crime-never-scores, modern-context, source-registry, exclusion-infrastructure tests. Fixed assertGeneralCrimeContextValid defense-in-depth scan.

title: BB-080: Tracker, plan, and source-spec hygiene
description:
Expansion bead BB-080 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (tracker, plan, and source-spec hygiene).

Source: bead audit 2026-07-17
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: none

Why: the tracker's substance lives in an unversioned PDF in ~/Downloads; plan.md tables contradict bd statuses; ~20 closed beads still carry plan-todo labels; deferred beads carry no rationale; and closed work (BB-047/BB-056 outputs) sits uncommitted. Two sources of truth actively disagree.

Deliver:

design: Bead audit 2026-07-17: all bead substance lives in an unversioned PDF in Downloads; two sources of truth (plan.md vs bd) actively disagree; closed work uncommitted. Single-writer rule: this bead owns plan.md edits for its wave.

Real inlined acceptance criteria on every still-open imported bead (BB-049/050/052/053/054/055/057/058/059/060/062/063). 3. plan.md tables reconciled with bd (Tranche 2/3/4 contradictions fixed); no closed bead carries a plan-todo label. 4. architecture.md active-tranche line and docs/README index fixed (docs/research/, docs/admin/ listed); design-system port drift fixed (3048). 5.
