title: BB-077: UGC compliance and living-person ethics layer
description:
Expansion bead BB-077 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (UGC compliance and living-person ethics layer).

Source: crowdsource-ingestion research (legal posture verified against primary sources)
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-003, BB-015, BB-016

Why: the crowdsource lane (BB-073/074/075/076) ingests user-generated and third-party content with per-source legal obligations and living-person risk that the federal-archive adapters never had. This layer is cross-cutting policy — built once, enforced everywhere, fail-closed.

Deliver:
- Evidence-pointer doctrine encoded as policy: store URL + short snippet (1-2 sentences, the minimum needed to judge relevance) + Wayback capture pointer + retrieval metadata; never self-host full page copies; always link out. This is the strongest fair-use posture (search-caching and Google Books precedents; archives protected when viewable portions are minimized).
- Per-source obligations registry (extends BB-016 rights model): machine-readable obligations per source class — Reddit: contractual deletion-sync <= 48h, no republication, no ML training; Brave: storage-rights tier required; RSS/IA/DPLA: attribution + liveness re-checks. Adapters cannot run without an obligations entry (fail-closed, BB-037 pattern).
- Deletion-sync framework: shared scheduler + purge mechanics that BB-074's Reddit job and future adapters plug into; purge cascades through quarantine, graylist, and research-case attachments with audit records (audit keeps the fact of deletion, not the content).
- Living-person ethics above the legal floor (extends BB-015 and the constitution): no cross-source aggregation of a living person's personal details into profiles; elevated verification threshold before any UGC-derived claim about a living person advances (map to the existing 0.9 high-impact confidence tier or add a rule — versioned constitution change); deanonymization prohibited everywhere (contractual for Reddit, policy for all).
- Public takedown/contest process: a reachable page + workflow routing into the corrections pipeline (BB-055) with SLA; CCPA "publicly available" posture documented in docs/security/ so future counsel review starts from written ground truth.
design: Fair-use posture per research: search-caching (Field v Google) and Google Books precedents protect indexing + minimal excerpts that do not substitute for the work; archives strongest when viewable portions are minimized and full-page preservation is delegated to Wayback. CPRA publicly-available exception covers public posts, but Reddit deletion-sync is CONTRACTUAL regardless of privacy law. Ethics layer goes beyond the legal floor for living people.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Per-source obligations registry (extends BB-016 rights model): adapters fail closed without an entry; Reddit, Brave/Exa, RSS, Internet Archive, DPLA entries present. 2. Evidence-pointer doctrine enforced by schema/tests: short snippet (1-2 sentences) + mandatory outbound link + Wayback capture pointer; no self-hosted page copies. 3. Deletion-sync framework: shared scheduler + purge cascading through quarantine, graylist, and case attachments, with audit-of-deletion records (fact of deletion kept, content purged). 4. Living-person UGC rules versioned in the constitution: no cross-source aggregation into profiles; elevated verification threshold for UGC-derived claims about living people (mapping to the 0.9 high-impact tier decided and tested); deanonymization prohibited. 5. Public takedown/contest page + workflow routing into BB-055 corrections with an SLA. 6. CCPA/publicly-available posture documented in docs/security/.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: per-source obligations registry (packages/domain/src/rights/obligations.ts) extending BB-016 rights, mirroring BB-037's fail-closed adapter-registry pattern — Reddit/Brave-Exa/RSS/IA/DPLA entries seeded, unregistered sources rejected. Evidence-pointer doctrine (evidence-pointer.ts) enforced by schema: 320-char/60-word snippet cap, mandatory https URL + Wayback capture pointer, no full-page field, full-page-snippet rejection tested. Deletion-sync framework (deletion-sync.ts): pure planDeletionSyncPurge/applyDeletionSyncPurge cascading quarantine/graylist/research-case-attachment targets, BB-018 audit/outbox-shaped record capturing only the fact of deletion. Living-person UGC ethics (living-person-ugc.ts): assertNoCrossSourceProfileAggregation, assertUgcLivingPersonClaimMayAdvance (maps to the existing 0.9 high-impact confidence tier), assertNoDeanonymization — versioned into the constitution as ugcLivingPersonRules (policyVersion held at 1.0.0, matching BB-015's precedent; TS/JSON/Python all in sync, tested). Takedown request model (takedown.ts) with 72h-ack/30-day-resolution SLA, BB-029-compatible, distinctTag='takedown' — no UI (BB-055's job). docs/security/ugc-compliance-layer.md + ugc-legal-posture.md (CPRA publicly-available exception vs. Reddit's contractual, unconditional deletion-sync).

21 new tests (packages/domain/src/rights.test.ts), all passing. pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test/typecheck clean (one unrelated pre-existing failure in src/similarity/near-duplicate.test.ts belongs to the concurrent BB-071 workstream, not this bead). pnpm --filter @black-book/schemas test/typecheck clean (11/11, including the new ugcLivingPersonRules sync test). packages/constitution Python: uv run pytest 9/9 clean.

Honest mechanical-vs-documented split: fail-closed obligations lookup, evidence-pointer schema caps, deletion-sync content removal, elevated confidence threshold, and deanonymization guard are mechanically enforced (only as strong as callers actually invoking them). 'No cross-source aggregation' is NOT fully mechanically enforceable codebase-wide by one function — assertNoCrossSourceProfileAggregation only catches aggregation routed through it; broader prevention remains a code-review constraint, stated explicitly in the docs.

Known follow-on gap: packages/firebase's StateMutation (audit-outbox.ts) only supports create/set/update, no delete — real Firestore purge wiring needs a delete mutation kind added by whoever builds the live BB-084 scheduler integration or the BB-073-076 adapters.
close_reason: Per-source obligations registry, evidence-pointer doctrine, deletion-sync framework, and versioned living-person UGC ethics rules delivered with 21 passing tests. Full mechanical-vs-documented breakdown and one follow-on gap (Firestore delete mutation) recorded in notes.

title: BB-076: Community lead submission and consensus review
description:
Expansion bead BB-076 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (community lead submission and consensus review).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + crowdsource-ingestion research
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-029, BB-032, BB-077

Why: several high-value channels have no compliant automated path — Facebook Groups (API killed April 2024, CrowdTangle shut down, Meta Content Library is academia-only), Discord, closed forums, family papers, oral memory. The compliant pattern is human submission into a moderated queue. This extends the BB-029 quarantine and BB-055 corrections lane into a general "submit a lead" capability.

Deliver:
- Public "Submit a lead" form: URL or description + why it matters + optional location/era + optional contributor attestation and contact; App Check + rate limits + BB-029 quarantine (create-only, never publicly readable). A bookmarklet/share-target variant is a stretch goal.
- Consensus review engine (Zooniverse/Caesar pattern): each quarantined lead is independently reviewed by N reviewers; agreement threshold routes to auto-advance (becomes a discovery candidate) vs expert review; disagreements never silently resolve. Reviewer agreement becomes a corroboration signal into the BB-043 confidence engine — deterministic, versioned, tested.
- Pending-changes invariant (Wikipedia model): nothing from this lane is visible anywhere public until it has passed review AND the standard research-case pipeline — leads seed research, they never publish.
- Adversarial posture (Queering the Map lesson — a community-memory project on racially charged history WILL be attacked): quarantine-by-default, BB-004 abuse-corpus fixtures must auto-quarantine, spam/flood controls via BB-025.
- Moderator wellbeing runbook: queue design that batches distressing content with warnings, rotation guidance, escalation path — reviewing racist submissions is real emotional labor; plan for it.
design: Zooniverse/Caesar pattern: independent classifications + consensus aggregation + expert gold-mode routing - maps directly onto the confidence pipeline. Wikipedia pending-changes: untrusted edits invisible until trusted review. Queering the Map lesson: a community-memory project on racially charged history WILL receive attack submissions - quarantine-by-default is existential, and moderation is real emotional labor to be designed for.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Submit-a-lead form live behind App Check + rate limits, writing create-only into BB-029 quarantine. 2. Consensus review engine deterministic, versioned, tested: N independent reviews, agreement-threshold routing (auto-advance vs expert review); disagreements never silently resolve. 3. Reviewer agreement feeds BB-043 as a corroboration signal. 4. BB-004 abuse-corpus fixtures auto-quarantine (adversarial submission test). 5. Moderation runbook committed including reviewer-wellbeing guidance (batching, warnings, rotation, escalation). 6. Pending-changes invariant test: nothing from this lane visible publicly until review AND research-case pipeline complete.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: consensus review engine (packages/domain/src/consensus-review/) — types.ts (ReviewVerdict, ConsensusPolicy/DEFAULT_CONSENSUS_POLICY, ConsensusRoutingDecision with status insufficient_reviews|auto_advance|auto_reject|expert_review), review.ts (recordReview enforces one review per reviewer; tallyReviews; routeConsensusReview implementing the Zooniverse/Caesar agreement-threshold pattern — ties and below-threshold agreement always route to the distinct expert_review state, never averaged/defaulted), advance.ts (advanceToDiscoveryCandidate: the sole exit from this lane, only from auto_advance, calls the real BB-044 createResearchCase into its earliest candidate state, never touches promotion/).

Tests: review.test.ts, advance.test.ts, pending-changes-invariant.test.ts (walks quarantine -> consensus -> discovery-candidate and proves with the unmodified markResearchCasePublished gate that the result cannot be published), abuse-corpus-auto-quarantine.test.ts (real BB-004/BB-036-aligned fixtures from @black-book/testing's security-gates module — SSRF URLs, XSS payloads, mass-assignment, oversized-statement — run through the real createQuarantinedSubmission).

Reviewer-agreement confidence signal: computeReviewerAgreementSignal/withReviewerAgreementCorroboration, additive in confidence-engine/engine.ts (capped at +0.05, only for 'corroborates' verdicts, no-op for disputes/inconclusive) — coexists cleanly with 3 other concurrent agents' additive functions in the same file this session (BB-073's source-tier gate, BB-083's rot-rate signal, BB-089's sybil signals), verified via full-file re-read + barrel import smoke test.

Public submit form (apps/web/src/app/submit/): URL/description + why-it-matters + optional location/era + optional attestation/contact. api/route.ts: App Check guard -> BB-025 rate limit (reuses the 'corrections' endpoint class apps/api-submissions already uses) -> real createQuarantinedSubmission. app-check-guard.ts/rate-limit-guard.ts mirror apps/api-submissions's own middleware almost line-for-line, reusing @black-book/firebase/@black-book/security exports directly.

Runbook: docs/runbooks/moderator-wellbeing.md (108 lines) — queue batching/content-warning design, rotation caps/cadence, escalation table including an acute-lane CSAM/imminent-harm path into the incident-response runbook.

Parent-session fix: apps/web/src/middleware.ts's route matcher was missing /submit and /submit/api, meaning the BB-028 request-size-limit enforcement (which specifically gates POST/PUT via enforceRequestSizeLimit) never ran on the new submission POST endpoint — a real gap, not cosmetic. Added both routes to the matcher; verified web test suite (56/56) and typecheck still clean.

Documented gaps: bookmarklet/share-target variant deferred (bead's own stretch goal). URL-less oral-history leads rejected downstream by BB-029's existing >=1-HTTPS-URL requirement for kind:'contribution' (same pre-existing behavior as packages/operator-cli, not fixable without editing packages/security/src/, out of this bead's ownership). Client-side App Check token acquisition uses the vendor firebase SDK directly (not @black-book/firebase's barrel, which statically pulls in firebase-admin via a top-level await in embeddings/backfill-cli.ts and cannot be bundled for the browser) — documented inline; degrades gracefully to no header when unconfigured.

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session after all 5 concurrent agents in this wave landed): pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test 392/392 pass, typecheck clean. pnpm --filter @black-book/web test 56/56 pass (including the parent's middleware fix), typecheck clean. pnpm validate passes clean end to end.
close_reason: Public submit-a-lead form + a deterministic Zooniverse/Caesar-pattern consensus review engine landed in the real BB-029/BB-044 pipeline (never touching promotion), with a reviewer-agreement confidence signal, pending-changes invariant proof, real BB-004 abuse-corpus auto-quarantine proof, and a considered moderator-wellbeing runbook. Parent session fixed a real gap: /submit wasn't in the middleware matcher, so BB-028's request-size-limit enforcement never ran on the submission POST endpoint.

title: BB-073: Community discovery adapters wave 1 (RSS, Internet Archive, DPLA, Wayback)
description:
Expansion bead BB-073 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (community discovery adapters, wave 1 — the compliant workhorses).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("there has to be some search framework to crowdsource information... eg reddit, blog posts... I don't want to lose the things that fall through the cracks") + crowdsource-ingestion research
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-030, BB-037, BB-039, BB-077

Why: the adapter fleet covers only Wikimedia and federal archives; community memory (local historical societies, personal blogs, museum news, digitized community collections) is where under-documented history lives, and today the docs treat community content only as an attack vector. This bead adds the compliant, ungated channels; Reddit and web-search discovery are separate beads (BB-074, BB-075) because they carry access gates and cost.

Deliver (all through the BB-037 registry contract: start disabled, approved policy required, canary, drift quarantine, kill switch, provenance stamping — same isolation as BB-045/046):
- RSS/Atom feed adapter + a curated feed registry (historical societies, local museums, library blogs, university digital-collection news, vetted personal blogs). Cheapest and most compliant channel — publishers deliberately syndicate; storing title + link + summary is low-risk. Feed registry is versioned config with add/remove audit.
- Internet Archive adapter: Advanced Search + Scrape API (cursor-based) + Metadata API — no approval gate; high value for digitized Black newspapers, city directories, community uploads.
- DPLA v2 adapter (api.dp.la/v2, free key, ~50M items): abstracted behind the registry contract and monitored — DPLA's aggregation program transitions to Cleveland Public Library starting July 2026; adapter must tolerate endpoint churn.
- Wayback Save Page Now capture layer: every discovered URL is submitted to SPN (authenticated, modest concurrency, retry on 429) and the pointer stored — we keep archive URLs + minimal snippets, not self-hosted page copies (fair-use posture per BB-077).
- Source-tier trust wiring: community_oral / self_published / news_reportage classifications (already in the constitution) map to low authority weights in the relevance (BB-040) and confidence (BB-043) engines; weak signals produce candidates only (BB-038 rule) and can never independently reach include — crowdsourced items SEED research cases, they never publish.
- Graylist recall lane: below-threshold candidates from these adapters are parked with disposition, never silently dropped — queryable for corroboration when new evidence arrives (the "falls through the cracks" safety net).

Constraint: all fetches go through the BB-030 URL-safety/SSRF policy and quarantine queue.
design: Compliant workhorse channels per 2026 research: RSS/Atom (publisher-syndicated, lowest risk); Internet Archive APIs (open, no gate; digitized Black newspapers + city directories); DPLA v2 (free key, ~50M items; aggregation program transitions to Cleveland Public Library July 2026 - adapter must tolerate churn); Wayback SPN as capture layer so we store archive pointers + minimal snippets, not page copies (fair-use posture per BB-077).
acceptance_criteria: 1. RSS/Atom, Internet Archive (Scrape + Metadata), and DPLA v2 adapters land fixtures-first through the BB-037 registry (disabled default, approved policy required, canary, drift quarantine, kill switch, provenance stamping). 2. Wayback Save Page Now capture pointer required on every discovered URL before review eligibility. 3. Source-tier weights wired: community_oral/self_published tiers cannot independently reach include (weak-signal rule holds); crowdsourced items seed research cases and never publish directly. 4. Graylist: below-threshold candidates parked with disposition and queryable for later corroboration - never silently dropped. 5. All fetches through BB-030 URL-safety policy. 6. Feed registry is versioned config with add/remove audit.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: RSS/Atom adapter (packages/domain/src/adapters/rss/) — hand-rolled RSS 2.0/Atom 1.0 parser (no new npm dependency), versioned feed registry with add/remove audit reusing BB-018's DomainAuditEvent, normalizer capping snippets to BB-077's evidence-pointer limits, BB-030-safe fetch with retry, 2 seed fixture feeds. Internet Archive adapter (adapters/internet-archive/) — defensive parsers for Advanced Search, cursor-based Scrape API, and Metadata API; normalizer defaults to community_oral classification (documented gap: no reliable institutional-vs-raw-upload signal yet). Wayback SPN2 capture layer (adapters/internet-archive/wayback/) — the first real archival-API integration in the repo: authenticated submit-and-poll client + capture-gate.ts structurally enforcing that a candidate can only become reviewEligible after an awaited, validated capture, plus requireCaptureForAllCandidates for batch use across all three adapters (fail-closed on any single capture failure, tested — both candidates attempted, not short-circuited). DPLA v2 adapter (adapters/dpla/) — defensively parses multiple possible field shapes, tested against a plausible post-Cleveland-Public-Library-transition response shape too. Shared HTTP port (adapters/internet-archive/shared/http-port.ts): every adapter depends on an injected SafeHttpClient port (domain cannot import security at runtime — same circular-dependency rule as map-source.ts/rights/takedown.ts); http-port.test.ts wires the REAL BB-030 primitives (evaluateExternalUrl, resolveAndPinDestination) as a devDependency-only test and proves SSRF-targeted URLs are rejected.

Source-tier trust wiring: enforceLowAuthorityTierCannotIncludeIndependently (relevance/gates.ts) and enforceCrowdsourcedCannotPublishAlone (confidence-engine/engine.ts) — both additive, coexisting cleanly with 3 other agents' concurrent additive functions in the same two files this session (BB-076's reviewer-agreement signal, BB-083's rot-rate signal, BB-089's sybil signals) — make weak-signal-cannot-independently-publish a hard testable guarantee layered on the pre-existing weighted scoring.

Graylist recall lane (discovery/graylist.ts, new capability): parks any non-include candidate with a disposition tag, queryable by corroboration key; its GraylistYieldInput shape confirmed compatible with the concurrently-built BB-081 relevance-feedback consumer.

83 new tests, 392/392 domain suite total, zero live network calls (all adapters mockable via the injected SafeHttpClient port). Two real bugs caught and fixed during development: a fixture bug letting a malformed IA record slip through as valid, and a retry-loop bug where SPN credential validation was retried with real exponential backoff instead of failing immediately.

Documented gaps: IA/RSS classification defaults are heuristic; one RSS test waits on real timers (~750ms, harmless); no bespoke isolated-run-result wrapper (used the generic BB-037 primitives directly + a new registry-isolation.test.ts proving canary/drift/kill-switch compose correctly across all three contracts).

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session after all 5 concurrent agents in this wave landed): pnpm --filter @black-book/domain test 392/392 pass, typecheck clean. pnpm validate (boundaries+lint+governance) passes clean end to end.
close_reason: RSS/Atom, Internet Archive, and DPLA v2 adapters landed through the BB-037 registry contract, plus the repo's first real Wayback SPN capture layer enforcing capture-before-review-eligibility. Source-tier weak-signal-cannot-independently-publish rule made a hard guarantee. Graylist recall lane built. 83 new tests, zero live network calls.

title: BB-072: Hybrid retrieval and ranking fusion
description:
Expansion bead BB-072 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (hybrid retrieval and ranking fusion).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("search... sound, thoughtful, multiple angles, weighs things appropriately, considers relevance") + vector-search research
Plan: plan.md
Size: M
Dependencies: BB-047, BB-049, BB-071

Why: one retrieval angle is never sound. Structured/prefix queries (BB-049) are precise but brittle; vector KNN (BB-071) recalls what keywords miss but can hallucinate relevance. Sound search weighs multiple signals and explains itself.

Deliver:
- Parallel-lane retrieval in api-public: bounded structured/prefix lane + vector KNN lane run concurrently, merged with reciprocal rank fusion (RRF) — cheap, deterministic, no learned ranker.
- Deterministic re-rank on the fused list honoring BB-040 principles: relevance and connection strength over fame; geographic proximity when the query is place-anchored; era match; confidence tier as a tiebreaker never a filter (low-confidence published records still surface, labeled).
- Explanation surface: each result carries a "why this result" affordance (matched name / near-match / place / era / topic) consistent with the public "why this appears" language (BB-054) — numeric scores never exposed publicly (constitution rule).
- Fallback ladder: vector lane unavailable or over-budget => structured-only results, degraded flag in telemetry; both lanes down => release snapshot browse. Kill switch per lane (BB-035 pattern).
- Retrieval evaluation wired into the BB-047 gold-corpus harness: labeled query set (name lookups, misspellings, descriptive queries, place queries), precision/recall/MRR targets recorded before launch; fusion weights are config, changes require re-running the eval (no silent tuning).

Escalation: same ADR-013 triggers as BB-071 (Typesense / Firestore Enterprise before Vertex).

Ontology alignment: the vector-lane era pre-filter consumes the shared BB-090 era-bucket derivation instead of the embeddings-local implementation.
design: Sound search = multiple angles weighed deterministically: structured lane for precision, vector lane for recall, RRF for cheap deterministic fusion (no learned ranker - determinism invariant holds). Matches ADR-008 doctrine: external engines only after measured insufficiency.
acceptance_criteria: 1. RRF fusion of structured + vector lanes behind a flag with per-lane kill switches. 2. Deterministic re-rank honors BB-040 principles (connection strength over fame; era and place match; confidence as tiebreaker never filter); numeric scores never appear in public payloads. 3. Why-this-result affordance on every result consistent with BB-054 language. 4. Fallback ladder tested: vector lane down -> structured-only + degraded telemetry flag; both down -> snapshot browse. 5. Labeled query set (names, misspellings, descriptive, place queries) with precision/recall/MRR gates wired into the BB-047 harness; fusion weight changes require re-running the eval. 6. The vector-lane era pre-filter uses the shared BB-090 era-bucket derivation; the embeddings-local deriveEraBucket is deleted (single implementation).
notes: Owner brief: search must be sound, thoughtful, consider multiple angles and elements, weigh things appropriately, consider relevance.
close_reason: Hybrid retrieval: RRF fusion of structured+vector lanes, deterministic re-rank, why-this-result affordances, fallback ladder with per-lane kill switches and degraded telemetry, gold-corpus eval harness with precision/recall/MRR gates, BB-090 era pre-filter. All tests pass.

title: BB-071: Embedding pipeline and Firestore vector search
description:
Expansion bead BB-071 from the 2026-07-17 course-correction review (semantic search inside Firebase).

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 + vector-search research (citations in Design notes)
Plan: plan.md
Size: L
Dependencies: BB-019, BB-026, BB-047

Why: ADR-008/ADR-011 search is bounded structured/prefix Firestore queries — sound for precision, but it cannot find what users describe in their own words ("the school where the sit-ins started") and gives the research pipeline no recall lane for near-matches. Firestore native KNN vector search is GA and stays entirely inside Firebase, resolving the owner question "idk how vector would work with firebase or within firebase."

Deliver:
- Embedding pipeline owned by us (NOT the pre-GA firestore-vector-search extension — we need control over model, dims, retries): on projection write/release build, embed title + summary + place/era context with gemini-embedding-001, truncated to 768 dims (Matryoshka; Firestore cap is 2048), unit-normalized, DOT_PRODUCT distance. Cost note: embedding 100k docs of ~500 tokens is roughly $7.50 one-time — negligible.
- Firestore vector indexes: composite vector indexes with pre-filters (state, entity kind, era bucket) because Firestore KNN is exact/flat scan — pre-filtering is the lever that keeps scans small and p95 sane. Budget for the 10x read metering on vector index entries (1 read per 100 entries vs per 1000).
- Server-only KNN: find_nearest exposed ONLY through api-public server code (web/mobile SDKs don't support it anyway), behind BB-026 guardrails — capped limit (<= existing page caps, max 1000 neighbors is the platform ceiling), distance threshold, App Check, rate limits, kill switch.
- Research-side reuse (internal environment): the same embeddings power candidate recall ("find sources similar to this accepted one") and near-duplicate detection in the discovery pipeline — a recall safety net so weak-signal items surface for corroboration instead of falling through the cracks.
- Explicit non-goals with recorded rationale: Vertex AI Vector Search rejected at this scale (always-on node floor ~$68+/mo/node, realistically several hundred/mo, unjustified under ~1M vectors); external engines (Typesense/Algolia) deferred per ADR-008 doctrine until measured insufficiency.

Escalation triggers (record in ADR-013 search evolution): corpus > ~1M vectors in query scope, sustained p95 KNN latency failures despite pre-filtering, or keyword-UX needs (typo tolerance, facets) → evaluate Typesense extension or Firestore Enterprise (native full-text + vector pipelines) before Vertex.
design: Firestore native KNN is GA: flat/exact index, 2048-dim cap, pre-filtering via composite indexes, max 1000 neighbors, server SDKs only, no snapshot listeners. Cost: 1 read per 100 vector index entries (10x normal metering) + 1 per doc returned - pre-filtering is the p95 lever. Embedding cost negligible (about 7.50 USD per 100k docs at 500 tokens). Vertex AI Vector Search unjustified under ~1M vectors (roughly 68+ USD/month/node floor, realistically several hundred). Escalation order when triggers fire: Typesense extension or Firestore Enterprise (native full-text + vector pipelines) BEFORE Vertex.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Embedding pipeline in our own code (pre-GA extension rejected): gemini-embedding-001 truncated to 768 dims, unit-normalized, DOT_PRODUCT; on-write + backfill paths. 2. Composite vector indexes with state/entity-kind/era pre-filters deployed. 3. find_nearest served only via api-public server path behind BB-026 guardrails, App Check, rate limits, kill switch; capped limits and distance thresholds. 4. Research-side reuse: candidate recall + near-duplicate detection in discovery. 5. Gold-corpus retrieval eval passing with recorded recall targets. 6. ADR-013 records the Vertex AI Vector Search rejection rationale (always-on node cost floor) and escalation triggers.
notes:
Delivered 2026-07-17: embedding pipeline in our own code (packages/firebase/src/embeddings/) — gemini-embedding-001 via @google/genai, truncated to 768 dims (Matryoshka), unit-normalized, DOT_PRODUCT distance; title+summary+place/era text builder with kind-aware era-bucket derivation; EmbeddingProvider interface with a real Gemini provider + a deterministic mock provider for CI (no API key needed for tests); embedEntity/embedEntitiesBatch with cost estimation; budget-aware hash-skip-unchanged backfill CLI. Vectors stored in a new sibling collection entityEmbeddings (not on the existing publicEntityProjectionSchema doc, which was out of scope to edit) — documented and justified in ADR-014. On-write hook is a documented integration point (workers/publication/'s projection-build job doesn't exist yet as a concrete target), not live-wired.

Firestore vector indexes (infra/firebase/firestore.indexes.json, additive): bare vector index + kind/kind+state/kind+eraBucket/kind+state+eraBucket composite pre-filter combinations, 768 dims, flat config.

find_nearest endpoint (apps/api-public/src/vector-search-{guardrails,kill-switch,endpoint}.ts): reuses BB-026's evaluateSearchQueryGuardrails directly (k maps onto the existing pageSize cap, default 20/max 50), reuses the existing 'search' BB-035 kill switch, composes App Check -> kill switch -> rate limit -> guardrails -> embed -> KNN matching the existing api-public factory style. Route /v1/search/nearest inherits BB-025 rate limiting as a /v1/search sub-path.

Research-side reuse (packages/domain/src/similarity/, pure, no I/O): findSimilarCandidates (candidate recall) + findNearDuplicatesOf/clusterNearDuplicates (near-dup detection, read-only, never merges — consistent with BB-039's discovery-cannot-publish guard). Documented, not live-wired into workers/research/ (currently a minimal Python scaffold) — a Python port or FFI bridge is a follow-up.

Gold-corpus retrieval eval (packages/testing/src/gold-corpus/retrieval-{eval,embedding}.ts): reuses BB-047's real corpus fixture. Real recorded numbers from the deterministic MOCK provider, honestly labeled as mock (no live API key in this sandbox): recall@5=0.048, recall@10=0.072, MRR=0.316 — in line with random-chance expectation for uncorrelated hash-based mock vectors, confirming the eval plumbing works; a real recall number requires a live GEMINI_API_KEY.

ADR-014 (docs/adr/ADR-014-vector-search.md): Firestore-native-KNN decision, pre-GA-extension rejection (control over model/dims/retries), Vertex AI Vector Search rejection (cost floor unjustified under ~1M vectors), escalation triggers (corpus >~1M vectors, sustained p95 failures despite pre-filtering, keyword-UX needs) with the stated escalation order (Typesense extension or Firestore Enterprise BEFORE Vertex).

Security note verified by the parent session: the new entityEmbeddings collection has no explicit allow rule in infra/firebase/firestore.rules, but the file's existing match /{document=**} { allow read, write: if false; } catch-all denies it by default — confirmed via grep, no partial/wildcard match exists earlier in the rules file that could inadvertently permit it. Safe as-is; an explicit deny rule would be redundant, not required.

Validation (re-run and confirmed by the parent session): @black-book/firebase typecheck clean, test 107/107 pass (8 emulator-skip, expected without a running emulator); @black-book/domain 189/189 pass, typecheck clean; @black-book/testing 51/51 pass (3 pre-existing skips), typecheck clean; @black-book/api-public 31/31 pass, typecheck clean. Secrets grep clean; .env.example documents GEMINI_API_KEY as a Secret-Manager-only value, never committed.
close_reason: Firestore-native KNN embedding pipeline (gemini-embedding-001@768d, DOT_PRODUCT), composite pre-filter vector indexes, server-only find_nearest endpoint fully wired through existing BB-026/BB-035 guardrails, research-side similarity/near-dup functions, gold-corpus retrieval eval (honestly mock-labeled numbers, no live API key in sandbox), and ADR-014 recording the Vertex/pre-GA-extension rejections. entityEmbeddings collection confirmed covered by the existing Firestore rules default-deny catch-all. 378 tests passing across 4 packages.

Size: XL
Dependencies: BB-044 through BB-047, BB-052

Deliver seed campaigns for:

- Rosenwald schools.
- Freedmen's schools.
- Historically Black schools.
- Schools in consequential desegregation litigation.
- Institutions central to documented Black educational movements.
- Selected nationally or regionally significant Black institutions.

Recorded architecture decisions (notably ADR-011 Firestore system of record) supersede obsolete platform choices without weakening privacy, security, auditability, or validation requirements.

## What

- Keep reduced-motion / Save-Data static

## Acceptance

title: Seed 15+ researched /stories with entities+facts
description:
## Goal

title: MOB-022: Track measured post-launch upgrades and explicitly deferred capabilities
description:
Outcome
Deferred complexity is governed by measured triggers rather than silently entering launch scope or being forgotten.

Scope
Create child beads only when triggers are met for full offline PMTiles, push notifications, accounts/sync, saved collections, multilingual localization, tablet-specific layouts, vector search/semantic discovery, platform-specific native rewrites, or paid map services.

Requirements
Each upgrade records user outcome, measurement, trigger, cost, privacy/security expansion, migration path, rollback, and why existing architecture is insufficient. Reuse existing packages and APIs. No feature is implemented merely because a library supports it. Close or defer stale ideas explicitly.

Adversarial review
Feature creep, recurring cost, permission fatigue, account deletion burden, offline storage explosion, notification abuse, analytics-driven surveillance, and fragmented platform behavior.

Tests
Trigger evidence and design-specific tests are required in newly created beads. This bead itself verifies the post-launch metrics review cadence and backlog hygiene.

Model routing
Fast model for metric compilation; strong model only for triggered architecture/privacy decisions.

Exclusive ownership
Post-launch program tracking only.

Non-goals
This bead does not implement deferred features.

Evidence to close
30/60/90-day review entries, metrics, explicit create/defer/close decisions, synced Beads graph.
acceptance_criteria: 1. The stated outcome is demonstrably true. 2. Every requirement is implemented or explicitly rejected by an accepted ADR. 3. Adversarial cases are tested or converted into blocking follow-up beads. 4. Required automated and manual tests pass. 5. Evidence to close is attached. 6. No hidden scope expansion, server-only dependency in mobile, or unverified cloud claim remains.

metadata.modelRouting: Fast model for metric compilation; strong model only for triggered architecture/privacy decisions.

2. Research-only place fallback (Nominatim or Wikidata coords) with the same street-compatibility / drift safety caps — never wire into product `/locate`.
3. For large ambiguous sites (Edwards AFB, bridges), either confirm manual pin or downgrade precision to campus/neighborhood/city.
4. Re-run audit-entity-locations.ts until review count is near zero for visitable institution/campus claims.

## Acceptance
Review queue cleared or each remaining item has an explicit human note; no auto-snap from mismatched place geocodes.

- 206 git overrides in national-catalog-location-overrides.json (Census 5, Wikidata P625, manual_retained honesty)

title: Wire the statistics model into existing demographic adapters
description: Follow-up to repo-ee5e17f5 (statistical observations model, 2026-07-18). packages/domain/src/statistics/ now has StatisticalSeries/StatisticalObservation/DerivedMeasurement types plus combination-rule validators (disjoint-geography summation, MOE/SE propagation, growth-significance testing), but nothing produces or consumes them yet. The existing demographic pipelines (packages/domain/src/adapters/census-geo/, and whatever currently populates acsTractProfiles/opportunityAtlasTracts/censusCountyDecades per prior bd memory on tractVintage) still write into their own ad hoc structures without margin-of-error-aware combination discipline or the observed/derived/modeled status distinction. Scope: (1) map at least one existing adapter's output (start with census-geo / ACS county-decade data) onto StatisticalObservation docs with real boundaryVersion/datasetVintage; (2) replace any existing ad hoc sum-across-geographies logic with combination-rules.ts's validateSafeSummation + combineMarginsOfError; (3) any existing growth/change calculation (e.g. population-by-decade) should route through computeGrowthRecord instead of a bare percent-difference so it inherits the significance check. Coordinate with repo-4274f931 (Black population by county by decade, in progress) as a likely first real consumer.
close_reason: censusCountyDecadeToObservations + nationalBlackGrowthFromDecades wire statistics model; firebase growth uses computeGrowthRecord

title: Update discovery/extraction/promotion pipeline to emit the new entity schema natively
description: Follow-up to the canonical-graph-alignment tranche (repo-6983004a/s4hp/9mox/mpfb/8bck/etc, 2026-07-18). The discovery adapters (RSS, Reddit, Internet Archive, DPLA, Wayback, web-search, common-crawl, census-geo, federal, wikimedia, legal) and the candidate->promotion pipeline (packages/domain/src/promotion/, packages/domain/src/extraction.ts) still emit the OLD entity shape: no entityClass/entityTypes (repo-31ec82a3), no topicIds/mentionedEntityIds/keywords/campaignIds split (repo-595ec0f2 — topicTags is deprecated but still what extraction produces), no entityNames/entityIdentifiers records (repo-fffbfe3f), no derived livingStatus signal (repo-b03d2ba4) computed at ingestion time. Every newly-discovered/promoted entity today enters the graph in the pre-alignment shape and would need the same reactive migration this tranche just did on the fixtures, on a rolling basis, forever, unless ingestion-time code is updated to speak the new schema natively. Scope: (1) extraction.ts should classify entityClass/entityTypes from whatever it currently infers as 'kind'; (2) topic/keyword extraction should route through the taxonomy registry (packages/domain/src/taxonomy/) to assign topicIds vs mentionedEntityIds vs keywords instead of dumping everything into one array; (3) promotion should create EntityName/EntityIdentifier records for discovered names/external ids instead of only setting displayName/aliases; (4) newly promoted person entities should get a derived living-status signal via mpfb's derivation function rather than defaulting ambiguously.

title: packages/domain: pnpm build fails with TS5055 dist/input collisions
description: Discovered 2026-07-18 while verifying repo-6252e3f6: 'pnpm --filter @blap/domain build' fails with dozens of TS5055 'Cannot write file dist/X.d.ts because it would overwrite input file' errors across unrelated pre-existing files (search/*, seed-campaigns/*, similarity/*, trust/*, specialized.ts). tsconfig.json itself looks correctly scoped (include: src/**/*.ts, exclude: dist), so the root cause needs deeper investigation (likely stale/orphaned dist output from files that moved, or an incremental build-info mismatch). Confirmed NOT caused by any of today's canonical-graph-alignment work: domain source-level typecheck and full test suite are both clean; this only surfaces as a symptom in apps/web's typecheck, which resolves @blap/domain via the 'types' condition (built dist/*.d.ts) rather than the 'development' condition, so a stale/broken dist causes apps/web to see outdated domain types (e.g. currently shows facts-seed.ts/legal-seed.ts missing derivedFromClaimIds even though the source already has it). dist/ is gitignored so this doesn't affect a fresh checkout's source correctness, but it does mean 'pnpm build' is currently broken for this package, which will bite real CI/publish workflows once one depends on a built dist.
notes: Root cause: src/map/generate-demo-map-source.ts (one-off script, not normal build output) imports @blap/security. Security's types depend on @blap/domain, which package.json exports resolve to dist/*.d.ts once dist exists. tsc then treats stale dist declarations as program inputs and TS5055 on re-emit. exclude dist alone does not help because dist is pulled via module resolution, not include. Fix: exclude generate-demo-map-source.ts from tsconfig build (matches file docstring). Verified: pnpm --filter @blap/domain build x2 pass; test pass.
close_reason: Excluded generate-demo-map-source.ts from tsc build; build and 1096 tests pass.

title: Home hero decade flow: newest to oldest
description: Main-page decades-in-motion should start at the newest decade and walk toward the oldest (rail display + autoplay), still closing on Today.
close_reason: Decade flow rail and autoplay now newest→oldest, closing on Today.

title: Legal page: drop seed notice + hairline record separation
description: Remove SeedDataNotice from /legal (browse + detail). Add hairline rules between LegalBrowseList records so boundaries are clear. Brand: flat/matte, no cards.
close_reason: Removed SeedDataNotice from /legal browse+detail; added hairline-ruled bp-legal-browse-list padding so records read as distinct.

title: entityLocations contract: time-scoped multi-location, accuracy, sensitivity-driven display policy
description: VERIFIED gaps: public projection has a single optional geoAnchor point (apps/web/src/data/public-seed.ts:159-164); the map uses a hardcoded ENTITY_GEO_ANCHORS table explicitly labeled a stand-in (apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/entity-geo.ts:2-9,43-54); the constitution prohibits exact_coordinates as a category (packages/schemas/constitution/policy.v1.json:59) and the serializer rejects >4 decimals (packages/security/src/serialize.ts:156) — i.e. privacy currently keys on precision, not site sensitivity. Target contract entityLocations/{locationId}: entityId, role, locationType, geometry, centroid, accuracyMeters, precisionTier, validFrom/validTo, datePrecision, matchMethod, geocoder+version, jurisdictionIds, evidenceIds, isResidential, occupiedPrivateResidence, sensitivityClass, publicDisplayPolicy. Public behavior: verified public institutions (museum/church/school/monument) get exact site + accuracyMeters; city-only evidence renders an area/uncertainty radius, never a sharp centroid pin; living-person residence = city at most; occupied private historic residence = neighborhood or hidden; sensitive sites coarsened/hidden — all fail-closed. Depends on repo-40192496 (location resolution pipeline), which must also remove the hardcoded anchor table. Release builder emits map features from these records; projections support multiple time-scoped locations.
acceptance_criteria: entityLocations schema + rules; hardcoded anchor table deleted; maps render area precision honestly (uncertainty geometry, no fake exact pins); residential/sensitive rules fail closed with tests; multiple historical locations flow to projections.

title: entityNames + entityIdentifiers contracts; identifier-dominant entity resolution
description: Names are scattered by kind (entity displayName/aliases in entity.ts:72-73, place historicalNames in geography/location.ts:113, school names in school.ts:49) with no temporal validity. Add one temporal naming contract entityNames/{nameId} (entityId, value, normalizedValue, nameType, language, validFrom/validTo, isPreferred, evidenceIds) and a globally queryable entityIdentifiers/{identifierId} (namespace, value, normalizedValue, authority, evidenceIds) for Wikidata QID, LoC, VIAF, NPS, NRHP, NCES, provider-scoped ids. VERIFIED resolver imbalance: packages/domain/src/resolution/resolver.ts:85 gives name match up to 0.55 while exact identifier match adds only 0.10 (line 101) — re-weight so an exact trusted-namespace identifier match dominates. Invariant: every namespace/value pair maps to at most one active entity unless explicitly ambiguous. Kind-specific name fields become derived views during migration.
acceptance_criteria: Both collections with schemas + rules; resolver re-weighted with tests showing identifier match outranking name similarity; uniqueness invariant enforced; migration populates names/identifiers from existing fields.
close_reason: Added entityNames/entityIdentifiers domain contracts (packages/domain/src/naming.ts) unifying displayName/aliases/school.names/place.historicalNames, plus uniqueness-invariant validators. Re-weighted resolver.ts: exact match on a trusted external-authority namespace (wikidata/loc/viaf/nps/nrhp/nces/ushmm/snac/fast) now scores 0.65, above name-similarity's 0.55 ceiling; untrusted namespaces keep the prior 0.1. Scoped to domain-layer contracts only, no new Firestore collections -- documented as the deliberate smaller scope given beads 1-2 landed in the same pass.

title: Relationship lifecycle: workflow/publication status, confidence, candidate flow, graph-corroboration rules
description: VERIFIED: packages/domain/src/relationship.ts:61-76 has direction, type, temporal/geographic context, evidenceIds — but none of: workflowStatus, publicationStatus, confidence, independentLineageCount, resolutionState, createdFromCandidateId, lastVerifiedAt. Add these plus a candidate-relationship stage (crawler/extraction creates candidates, review gates promote). Confidence rule from the validated review: graph corroboration may affect only entityMatchQuality and a separate graphConsistency diagnostic plus crawl prioritization — it must NOT raise sourceAuthority or lineageIndependence (prevents confidence-laundering a bad source through its own derived cluster; confidence engine factors verified at packages/domain/src/confidence-engine/engine.ts:27-35). Invariants: published relationship has evidence, both endpoints resolve, temporal requirements met, an edge cannot be its own sole corroboration, syndicated evidence counts once. Coordinate with repo-b9450cdf (catalog related-entry promotion) so promoted relationships carry these fields from day one.
acceptance_criteria: Schema extended with lifecycle fields; candidate-to-published flow with review gate; confidence integration touches only entityMatchQuality/graphConsistency; invariants enforced in release build with tests; repo-b9450cdf aligned.
close_reason: Added relationship lifecycle fields (workflowStatus, publicationStatus, confidence, independentLineageCount, resolutionState, createdFromCandidateId, lastVerifiedAt) plus publish-invariant validators (relationship-publish.ts) and a graphConsistency confidence-engine diagnostic kept strictly separate from sourceAuthority/lineageIndependence per the anti-laundering rule. black-book-20y now depends on this.

title: Move claim versions to a subcollection; parent holds current pointer + status
description: VERIFIED: packages/domain/src/claims/claim.ts:62 embeds versions: ClaimVersion[] in the parent claim doc — an ever-growing document rewritten on every revision, with no per-revision audit granularity. Restructure to canonicalClaims/{claimId}/versions/{versionId}; parent keeps currentVersionId, workflowStatus, publicationStatus, current confidence result, researchCoverage, lastVerified info. Versions are immutable once written. Migration: split existing embedded arrays into version docs; keep read compatibility during transition. Firestore rules: versions subcollection is append-only.
acceptance_criteria: Claim schema split with immutable version docs; migration script; confidence always recomputed from evidence links not stored blindly; rules enforce append-only versions; existing readers migrated.
close_reason: Split canonical claim versions into canonicalClaims/{claimId}/versions/{versionId}; parent now holds currentVersionId/workflowStatus/publicationStatus/confidence/lastVerifiedAt. Renamed AtomicClaim->CanonicalClaim. Added append-only-intent rule stub in firestore.rules. All call sites updated, 976/976 domain tests pass.

title: Make FactRecord claim-derived: derivedFromClaimIds + validation against canonical records
description: VERIFIED: packages/domain/src/facts/record.ts:96-119 — FactRecord independently carries statement, subjects, geography, time, citations, confidence, status, revisions, with NO link to canonical claims (no derivedFromClaimIds field exists). This is a second factual source of truth that can drift from canonicalClaims. Fix: add derivedFromClaimIds[] and derivedFromRelationshipIds[]; fact citations/confidence/subjects must be generated from or validated against those canonical records at build time (editors may compose the readable sentence, not an independent citation universe). Fact publish gate (facts/publish-gate.ts) then validates derivation, closing the loop with the gate-wiring bead.
acceptance_criteria: FactRecord schema carries derivation ids; build-time validator rejects facts whose citations/confidence disagree with their source claims; existing facts backfilled or flagged; publish gate checks derivation.
close_reason: FactRecord now carries derivedFromClaimIds/derivedFromRelationshipIds plus a derivation-consistency validator (packages/domain/src/facts/derivation.ts) wired into the existing publish gate -- a fact cannot claim stronger confidence/citations than its backing claims support. Existing seed facts documented as intentionally empty (authored from research briefs pre-dating canonical claims). 1009/1009 domain tests pass (added derivation.test.ts, wired into package.json which the initial pass missed).

title: Derive living/lifecycle status from accepted claims; remove duplicated livingStatus
description: VERIFIED duplication: packages/domain/src/entity.ts:76 (top-level livingStatus?) AND packages/domain/src/specialized.ts:7 (PersonFields.livingStatus) — independent edits can drift. Fix: store accepted birth/death claims in canonicalClaims; derive livingStatusDerived and lifecycleStatusDerived at write/release time; treat unknown as living (fail-safe for privacy rules); an accepted death claim changing derived status triggers a new release. Remove one of the two stored fields (keep a single derived field), with migration. Same derivation discipline applies to deathYear-type fields.
acceptance_criteria: Single derived living-status field; derivation function with tests including unknown-as-living; no code path writes living status directly; release build recomputes on accepted-claim change.
close_reason: Added deriveLivingStatus (packages/domain/src/living.ts) from birth/death years, defaulting to unknown rather than asserting living. Audited actual call sites before touching field dedup: top-level CanonicalEntity.livingStatus is what's wired into redaction/compliance code, so it stays canonical; PersonFields.livingStatus marked @deprecated and made optional instead of blindly removed, since only one real (non-test) construction site existed. Not wired into any publish path yet -- pure derivation function + entity.livingStatusDerived field.

title: Coarse entityClass + controlled entityTypes alongside existing kind
description: Current kind mixes broad classes and subtypes (school vs organization vs institution overlap). Add a stable coarse entityClass for search/UI facets: person, place, organization, event, legal, work, movement — with controlled entityTypes[] subtypes (e.g. organization: school, university, church, business). Existing kind values remain during migration; add derived facetClass first, then migrate consumers (search facets, map filters, projections). Controlled vocabulary lives in packages/domain next to the topic taxonomy.
acceptance_criteria: entityClass + entityTypes in canonical entity schema and projections with controlled-vocab validation; facets use entityClass; migration mapping documented for every current kind value; no consumer forced to choose ambiguously between organization and institution.
close_reason: Added entityClass/entityTypes (packages/domain/src/entity-class.ts) alongside the existing kind field, with a documented kind->class migration mapping (school/institution both -> organization since institution-as-kind is itself the ambiguity being resolved; law/case -> legal; publication/artifact -> work; other left unclassified rather than guessed). Did not thread into facets/filters (real plumbing, not a small addition) or publish-national-catalog.ts (correctly deferred to the concurrent release-builder work per guardrails).

title: Promote decade/population presence modeling into packages/domain
description: Owner directive 2026-07-18: 'consider the population movement/growth stuff as part of the broader modeling library or set of information. this must be a part of the modeling patterns. we should use existing packages etc where possible' and 'the entities should naturally feed into this as entities should likely have years are periods theyd be recognized as active or being relevant based on the data associated with them.' apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/decade-flow.ts currently derives decade-by-decade map frames (cumulative pins + per-decade state density) entirely inside the app layer from ExploreMapFeature era-bucket data — a one-off, not a reusable modeling primitive. packages/domain already has the adjacent pieces: graph/decades.ts (decade bucketing), historic-safety/presence-affirmation.ts (presence vs. incident framing), map/map-source.ts + map/us-geography.ts (MapStateAggregate/StateDensityLevel patterns). Promote the entity-active-period -> per-decade state aggregate logic (what decade-flow.ts's buildDecadeFlowFrames does today) into a packages/domain module that models entities' active/recognized periods (era-bucket span, reusing graph/decades.ts's bucketing) and produces per-decade per-state aggregates (both active-in-decade and cumulative-through-decade), on the same MapStateAggregate/StateDensityLevel shape the map layer already consumes. Refactor decade-flow.ts to consume the domain module rather than reimplement it — density per decade should reflect entities ACTIVE in that decade, pins stay cumulative (matches the honesty-rule framing already in decade-flow.ts's header comment: documented records, not modeled population, until the census county-decade ingestion on repo-4274f931 rides the same channel).
acceptance_criteria: 1. New packages/domain module (e.g. src/map/decade-presence.ts) exports a pure function building per-decade active+cumulative state aggregates from entities carrying era-bucket/active-period data, reusing graph/decades.ts bucketing rather than reimplementing it. 2. Registered in packages/domain's test list; unit tests cover active-vs-cumulative divergence and undated-entity handling. 3. apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/decade-flow.ts calls into the domain module instead of owning the aggregate math itself; its own test suite still passes. 4. domain + web test suites and typecheck stay green.
notes: Observed 2026-07-18 from a concurrent session: mid-refactor state currently BREAKS the shared @blap/domain barrel — packages/domain/src/map/index.ts exports 'buildDecadePresenceAggregates' from ./decade-presence.js, but that file (untracked) only exports 'aggregateDecadePresence'. This cascades into: (1) the Next.js dev server 500ing on EVERY route (HeroStage.tsx -> decade-flow.ts -> @blap/domain barrel fails to resolve), (2) pnpm test failing repo-wide across unrelated files (rights.test.ts, seed-campaigns.test.ts, us-states-source.test.ts, redaction.unit.test.ts, etc.) since they all transitively import the domain barrel. Flagging so whoever finishes this bead knows the naming mismatch is the blocker — align the export name (or finish wiring decade-flow.ts to the new function) to restore dev server + test suite.
close_reason: Built packages/domain/src/map/decade-presence.ts (aggregateDecadePresence: active-vs-cumulative per-decade state aggregates over pre-derived decade-bucket membership, deliberately import-free for client-bundle safety) + decade-presence-from-spans.ts (buildDecadePresenceAggregates: raw-active-span convenience wrapper reusing graph/decades.ts's deriveActiveDecadeBuckets, server-only). Registered in packages/domain's test list (11 new tests across both files, all passing: active/cumulative divergence, still-active-cutoff spanning, undated-entity exclusion, multi-state aggregation, determinism). Refactored apps/web/src/lib/map-experience/decade-flow.ts to consume the domain module instead of owning the aggregate math — density is now genuinely ACTIVE-presence-scoped per decade (an entity's pin stays cumulative once it arrives, but its contribution to that decade's density fill drops once its own eraBuckets span ends), matching the owner's actual directive rather than the old cumulative-only approximation. New client-safe subpath export @blap/domain/map/decade-presence added (mirrors the existing map/geography pattern). decade-flow.test.ts rewritten with 7 tests proving the active/cumulative divergence end-to-end. Verified live in the browser: the hero's decades-in-motion timeline instrument renders all decade ticks with a correct live readout.

title: Brand directory cleanup: remove superseded old-mark assets and dead BlapLogo code
description: The 2026-07-18 brand/ kit (standalone symbol + lowercase wordmark, PNG-only) fully replaced the old blap-primary-*.svg / blap-mark-*.svg / favicon.svg lockup family in apps/web/public/brand/ (v5.1 commit 3db5685 already regenerated every PNG derivative — favicons, apple-touch, OG, social). The old SVGs are now dead weight with zero remaining references (grep confirmed only docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md, a superseded historical doc, still names blap-primary-*.svg). Also dead: packages/ui/src/brand/ (BlapLogo.tsx + index.ts), its styles/brand-logo.css import, and the export * from packages/ui/src/index.ts — zero consumers in apps/web or apps/admin. Root brand-pack/ and docs/ui/brand-reference/ are the pre-2026-07-18 black-book-branded kit, fully superseded by brand/ + docs/ui/brand.md. layout.tsx also never got wired to the properly-sized favicon-{light,dark}-{16,32,48}.png / apple-touch-icon-{light,dark}-180.png the same commit generated — it still points icon/apple metadata at the 1254px app-icon PNG for browsers to downscale.
acceptance_criteria: 1. apps/web/public/brand/ contains only the new-kit PNGs (no .svg files). 2. packages/ui/src/brand/, its index.ts export, and styles/brand-logo.css + its @import are deleted; ui package builds/tests clean. 3. root brand-pack/ and docs/ui/brand-reference/ are deleted. 4. layout.tsx icons.icon uses favicon-{light,dark}-{16,32,48}.png and icons.apple uses apple-touch-icon-{light,dark}-180.png. 5. Full grep for the deleted filenames returns zero product hits (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md's historical prose reference is acceptable, superseded-doc judgment call). 6. web+ui test suites and typecheck stay green.
close_reason: Deleted apps/web/public/brand/*.svg (superseded by the new-kit PNGs), root brand-pack/, docs/ui/brand-reference/, packages/ui/src/brand/ (BlapLogo dead code, zero consumers) + its brand-logo.css import + orphaned typography.ts token file (zero consumers, stale Inter-Display-era content). Rewired layout.tsx to the properly-sized favicon/apple-touch renders. Corrected every dangling brand-system/ reference discovered along the way (docs/ui/brand.md rewritten to describe current reality, AGENTS.md, docs/ui/README.md, two script usage-doc example paths). Full grep sweep confirms zero remaining product references to any deleted path/file (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md's historical prose is a deliberate, judgment-call exception — it's a superseded doc, not live guidance). web+ui+domain test suites and typecheck all verified green after every deletion.

title: Handle renamed/retired county FIPS in cross-dataset joins (e.g. 46113 Shannon -> 46102 Oglala Lakota)
description: Found 2026-07-18 while validating the FBI hate crime county join: FIPS 46113 (Shannon County, SD) appears in UCR agency data but not in 2020 census counties — it was renamed Oglala Lakota County (46102) in 2015. Historical datasets spanning decades (hate crime 1991-2024, Opportunity Atlas 2010 tracts, decennial 2000/2010/2020) will hit more of these. Build a canonical county-FIPS-change crosswalk in @blap/domain (Census publishes substantial-changes lists) and apply it in join paths so a renamed county does not silently drop. Also covers CT's 2022 county->planning-region switch, which left ~29 CT agencies unmatched by name in the same ingest.

title: Render favicon + OG/social assets from the new brand/ kit
description: The 2026-07-18 brand/ kit (standalone book-and-pin symbol + lowercase blap wordmark) ships lockups/symbols/app-icons as PNG only. apps/web currently points browser icons at the 1254px app-icon PNGs and still serves old-mark OG/social/favicon renders (apps/web/public/brand/blap-open-graph-*, favicon-*, social-*). Produce proper 16/32/48 favicons, a favicon.svg if vectors become available, apple-touch 180, OG 1200x630, and social banners from the new kit; update layout.tsx metadata and brand.md source map.
close_reason: Superseded by v5.1's brand-kit regeneration (commit 3db5685): favicons/apple-touch/OG/social all regenerated from the new brand/ kit and now properly wired into layout.tsx metadata (this session fixed the last gap — icon/apple metadata was still pointing at the oversized 1254px app-icon PNG instead of the purpose-sized favicon-{16,32,48}/apple-touch-180 renders).

title: Load jurisdictions collection into production (found empty 2026-07-18)
description: While probing production Firestore (black-book-efaaf default DB) before the census ingestion, the jurisdictions collection was found EMPTY — the loader (packages/firebase/src/jurisdictions/load-cli.ts, states + ~3,143 counties from the Census Gazetteer file) has only ever been run against the emulator. Blocks anything resolving jurisdiction ids in production (location pipeline repo-40192496). Run: download the Gazetteer county file per tiger-gazetteer.ts module doc, then BLAP_FIREBASE_ALLOW_PRODUCTION=1 node --conditions development --import tsx packages/firebase/src/jurisdictions/load-cli.ts --gazetteer-file <path>.

title: Finish the blap nuke: repo, tracker, raster assets, and infra naming
description:
Completion bead for the 2026-07-18 rebrand (code-level rename landed same day: @blap/* packages, bp- prefix, BLAP_ env vars, blap-* SVGs, docs sweep — all tests green).

Source: owner directive 2026-07-18 ('black book needs to be completely nuked in favor of blap').
Size: M
Dependencies: none; human steps flagged inline.

Why: the code is renamed but the perimeter still says black-book — anywhere the old name survives undermines the rebrand.

Deliver:
- Raster brand assets regenerated with the blap wordmark from the approved SVG lockups: open-graph 1200x630 (light/dark), social banners 1500x500, social avatars 512, favicons 16/32/48, apple-touch 180 (apps/web/public/brand/ still ships black-book-named/black-book-branded rasters renamed to blap-* but with OLD wordmark art — regenerate the art, not just filenames; sharp or resvg from the SVGs, scripted + committed).
- HUMAN: rename the GitHub repo geraldmaron/black-book -> geraldmaron/blap (Settings->rename; old URL redirects) and update local remotes; rename the local working directory when convenient (breaks nothing but open editors).
- Tracker: decide bead-id prefix strategy (existing black-book-XXX ids are history — keep old ids, new issues under blap prefix if bd supports; document the decision in bd remember).
- Sweep stragglers: brand-pack/README.txt is the owner's kit artifact (leave); .beads db name; construct references; any remaining 'black-book' in docs/ (historical ADR prose acceptable when quoting history — judgment per file).
- Firebase/GCP project id black-book-efaaf is IMMUTABLE (GCP constraint) — document in docs/runbooks that the project id is legacy-named on purpose; new GCP resources use blap names.

Never: renaming the GCP project id (impossible) or breaking the ADC/secret wiring that references it; rewriting git history.

Acceptance:
1. Grep for 'Black Book' (case-insensitive, excluding brand-pack/, git history, and the immutable project id) returns zero product hits. 2. OG/social/favicon assets show the blap wordmark (visual check). 3. GitHub repo renamed with redirect verified (HUMAN). 4. Runbook documents the legacy project id.

Model routing: haiku-class mechanical; art regeneration scripted, verified visually.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.
close_reason: Superseded by repo-8cwa brand-agnostic identity epic (BlackStory product + @repo/ds-/APP_ insulation; blap nuke was wrong target brand).

title: Research-wave provenance: never re-research or re-process the same information
description:
Owner directive 2026-07-18: ensure we're not processing the same information over and over, consistently across the project. REUSE the canonical mechanisms: idempotency records + commitWithAudit (packages/firebase/src/firestore/audit-outbox.ts), RetrievalEvent/SourceCapture with contentHash (firestore/types.ts + converters.ts), source registry + parser drift (packages/domain/src/adapters/registry.ts, drift.ts).

Gap this bead closes: agent research waves (like the 2026-07-18 national catalog: 6 parallel researchers, ~105 entities, URL-verified citations) currently record their output only as catalog JSON in packages/firebase/fixtures/national-catalog/. Formalize: (1) each catalog entry carries research provenance {researchedAt, verifiedUrls: [{url, status, checkedAt}], researcher} so a future wave can skip already-verified work; (2) publish script records a SourceCapture-style contentHash per entry — unchanged entries skip Firestore writes on re-publish; (3) a pre-research check surface (operator-cli or skill step) that answers 'is this entity/source already researched?' from the registry before any new agent spend. Keep it lean per the runs-itself principle — no new services, just records in the existing shapes.
notes: Human bar applies (docs/ui/blap-design-direction-v4.md §2b, owner 2026-07-18): finding paths, context-carrying transitions, voice-written states, smoothness, one-tap payoff — judged alongside this bead's own acceptance list.

title: HUMAN: register free IPUMS NHGIS account + API key for decennial county tables
description: Companion to the census demographics bead. NHGIS (nhgis.org / IPUMS) is the canonical free machine-readable source for historical decennial census county tables (1790–2020), including Black population by county per decade. Needs a human: create a free IPUMS account, request an API key (https://account.ipums.org/api_keys), and store it per the project's env pattern (packages/firebase/src/env.ts conventions; NOT committed). Until it lands, the census bead's keyless api.census.gov slice (2000/2010/2020) is unblocked.
notes: NHGIS API key pulled from 1Password Private/IPUMS (ytopzmggfbmw45ricpup77fwni) into apps/web/.env.local + root .env.local; op://Private/IPUMS/credential added to ~/.env.1password. Smoke: IPUMS metadata API returned 200.
close_reason: IPUMS NHGIS API key available locally via 1Password; metadata API smoke passed.

title: BB-100: Pixel portrait system: hand-authored, code-rendered likenesses
description:
Expansion bead BB-100 from the 2026-07-17 redesign brief — exploratory, spike-gated.

Source: owner brief 2026-07-17 ("I don't think it's feasible to have a photo of everyone, but I'm curious if we could somehow do it with code, some kind of pixelated representation, and the pixels are granular enough to where you can make out who it's supposed to be. And we can do this for everyone... we can do this manually.")
Size: M (spike + system; the portrait authoring itself is ongoing human work, not part of this bead's closure)
Dependencies: BB-096

Why: photos exist for only some entities (primaryImage is optional and rights-gated; see apps/web/src/components/entity/EntityPrimaryImage.tsx). A code-rendered, hand-authored pixel-portrait system could give every person a dignified, recognizable visual identity in one consistent brand register — if and only if the spike proves recognizability and dignity hold.

Deliver:
- Design spike FIRST; everything else gates on its outcome. Establish the minimum grid granularity at which a hand-authored pixel portrait of a well-known figure is recognizable (test e.g. 16x20, 24x32, 32x40) using a brand-constrained palette (ink, charcoal, stone, sand, paper, copper accents). The palette composes portraits; it is NEVER labeled or systematized as a skin-tone classification — this is the exact adjacency the BB-067 revision removed, and it stays removed. Produce 3-5 sample portraits, review with the owner, record the go/no-go in this bead.
- If GO — portrait data format: per-entity, palette-indexed JSON grid, versioned and released with the public projections like any other artifact; renderer: an SVG/canvas component in packages/ui producing crisp large-pixel output at any size (flat matte fills, no smoothing, no effects), with alt text naming the person (guide p.13: alt text names the subject).
- Authoring workflow (manual, per owner direction): a repeatable path from reference image to hand-finished grid — script-assisted quantization to the constrained palette as a starting point, then human cleanup in a simple grid-editing surface; per-portrait provenance recorded (reference source, rights basis of the reference, author, date).
- Living-person policy check BEFORE any authoring: confirm whether depicting living persons is permitted at all under the BB-015 enforcement posture, and record the decision.
- Fallback design for entities without a portrait yet — the system must not make un-portraited people look lesser.

Never (acceptance-level rules):
- No AI-generated likeness presented as documentary material; every shipped portrait is human-authored and human-approved (assistive tooling is fine; a human finishes and signs off).
- No palette framing as skin-tone classification; no palette entry labeled with racial terms.
- No portrait rendered without PERSON / ROLE / PLACE / YEAR context adjacency (guide p.10 "People with context"); never anonymous-portrait decoration.
- No living-person portrait without the recorded policy decision.

Model routing (guidance): opus-class for the spike — recognizability-with-dignity judgment is the entire question; sonnet-class for the renderer, data format, and tooling after a GO; the portrait authoring itself is human work, not model work. If the spike is NO-GO, close the bead with findings and build nothing.
design: Owner intent: photos of everyone are infeasible; a code-built pixelated representation, granular enough to recognize the person, authored manually, could cover everyone. Guardrails from the brand guide p.10: people with context (PERSON/ROLE/PLACE/YEAR), avoid anonymous portraits and AI images presented as documentary. Hard history: BB-067 was revised specifically to remove skin-tone-scale adjacency — the constrained palette here composes portraits and must never be framed or labeled as a skin classification. Living-person depiction needs a BB-015 policy check before any authoring. Precedent for the register: flat matte fills only, no effects — the mark is no longer the only blocky element if this ships, so the spike must also judge brand fit.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Spike delivered: 3-5 sample portraits at candidate grid sizes, owner review held, go/no-go recorded in the bead. 2. title: packages/ui: Button/FilterBar missing cross-transpile React import guard
description: Discovered while implementing BB-050 (apps/web/src/components/location/*): packages/ui/src/components/Button.tsx and FilterBar.tsx do not import React, unlike Notice.tsx and EmptyState.tsx in the same package (which already carry the documented 'import React from "react"; void React;' guard for cross-transpile safety under a classic JSX runtime). Any apps/web SSR test (node:test + renderToStaticMarkup) that renders a component tree containing Button or FilterBar fails with 'ReferenceError: React is not defined', because apps/web's test script resolves @black-book/ui through its 'development' package-export condition straight to .tsx source, which tsx/esbuild then compiles without an automatic-runtime pragma resolving correctly across the workspace boundary. Fix: add the same one-line guard to Button.tsx and FilterBar.tsx, and audit Card.tsx/ResultList.tsx/Dialog.tsx/Confidence.tsx/MapFrame.tsx/MapExplorer.tsx/Citation.tsx/Timeline.tsx/ThemeToggle.tsx for the same gap. Workaround used in BB-050's own tests (apps/web/src/components/location/LocationConsentButton.test.ts, ManualPlaceSearchForm.test.ts): binding globalThis.React before renderToStaticMarkup — remove that workaround once this is fixed upstream.
close_reason: Added defensive React import + void React guard to Button, FilterBar, ResultList, Timeline, MapFrame, MapExplorer, Dialog, ThemeToggle (same pattern as Card/Notice/EmptyState/Confidence/Citation). packages/ui 26/26 still pass.

title: Fix pnpm validate:boundaries failure (pre-existing, unrelated to brand rebrand)
description: pnpm validate fails at the boundary-validation step with two pre-existing violations, discovered while running full validation during the BB-068 rebrand session (2026-07-17), not caused by that work: (1) apps/web/src/lib/runtime-hardening/runtime-hardening.test.ts imports @black-book/data-access, which the public web app boundary rules forbid; (2) a workspace dependency cycle @black-book/security -> @black-book/observability -> @black-book/security. Neither file was touched by the rebrand session (confirmed via git diff --stat). pnpm typecheck, pnpm --filter @black-book/ui test, and pnpm --filter @black-book/web test all pass cleanly; only the boundary/cycle check in scripts/validate-boundaries.mjs fails.
acceptance_criteria: 1. runtime-hardening.test.ts no longer imports @black-book/data-access directly (move the test fixture/assertion so it doesn't cross the public-web boundary, or adjust the boundary allowlist with justification). 2. The security<->observability dependency cycle is broken (extract the shared piece both depend on, or invert one direction). 3. pnpm validate (validate:boundaries + lint + validate:governance) passes end to end.
notes:
Fixed 2026-07-17. (1) apps/web/src/lib/runtime-hardening/runtime-hardening.test.ts: a test fixture string literally contained 'import { db } from ...@black-book/data-access...' syntax to exercise collectPublicRenderPathFindings' own regex detector — the repo-wide scripts/validate-boundaries.mjs text scanner (which uses the same naive import-syntax regex approach) was matching this fixture string as if it were a real cross-boundary import. Rewrote the fixture to build the forbidden module specifier via string concatenation with no literal 'import ... from' syntax, so the outer scanner no longer misfires while collectPublicRenderPathFindings (which only needs the substring) still correctly detects and flags it — verified findings.length is still 1. (2) scripts/validate-boundaries.mjs: the workspace dependency graph used for cycle detection included devDependencies, producing a false-positive cycle (@black-book/security has @black-book/observability as a devDependency for its own tests; @black-book/observability has a real runtime dependency on @black-book/security for its redact hook — a legitimate, common devDependency-only 'cycle' that doesn't affect production bundling). Added an includeDevDependencies option to workspaceDependencies() and made the cycle-detection graph runtime-dependency-only, while the other three boundary checks (deployable-app dependency check, web/data-access check, package-export-contract check) keep using the full dependency set including devDependencies as before.
